checkpoint removed: anihpj_web:skills_test_session1 checkpoint removed: anihpj_web:skills_test_session2 Skills Model Testing Checkpoint Cleanup: Checkpoints removed: 2 Total Skills test data cleaned: 6 Removed Checkpoint: skills_test_session1 Skills test data cleared: 3 - Python: Programming (Expert) - Django: Framework (Advanced) - PostgreSQL: Database (Intermediate) Removed Checkpoint: skills_test_session2 Skills test data cleared: 3 - Docker: DevOps (Advanced) - Redis: Caching (Beginner) - Celery: Task Queue (Intermediate) Remaining Skills in development: 2 - Kubernetes: Orchestration (Intermediate) - AWS: Cloud (Advanced)
Example 2: User Migration Checkpoint Cleanup (Database Management)

Remove successful User model migration checkpoints after confirming migration completion to manage database storage.

# Remove completed migration checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_db pre_user_migration post_user_migration

# Check remaining database checkpoints
docker checkpoint ls anihpj_db

Django Models Context:

# User model migration states being cleaned up
class User(models.Model):
    username = models.CharField(max_length=150, unique=True)
    email = models.EmailField()
    first_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    last_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    profile_image = models.ImageField(upload_to='profiles/', null=True, blank=True)
    is_email_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    last_login = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    date_joined = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.username

# User migration data from removed checkpoints
removed_migration_checkpoints = {
    'pre_user_migration': [
        User(username="john_doe", first_name="John", last_name="Doe"),
        User(username="jane_smith", first_name="Jane", last_name="Smith"),
        User(username="admin_user", first_name="Admin", last_name="User")
    ],
    'post_user_migration': [
        User(username="john_doe", email="john@anihpj.com", first_name="John", last_name="Doe", is_email_verified=True),
        User(username="jane_smith", email="jane@anihpj.com", first_name="Jane", last_name="Smith", is_email_verified=True),
        User(username="admin_user", email="admin@anihpj.com", first_name="Admin", last_name="User", is_email_verified=True)
    ]
}

# Print User migration cleanup results
print("User Migration Checkpoint Cleanup:")
total_users_cleaned = sum(len(users) for users in removed_migration_checkpoints.values())
print(f"Migration checkpoints removed: {len(removed_migration_checkpoints)}")
print(f"Total User migration data cleaned: {total_users_cleaned}")

for checkpoint_name, users_list in removed_migration_checkpoints.items():
    print(f"\nRemoved Migration: {checkpoint_name}")
    print(f"Users data cleared: {len(users_list)}")
    for user in users_list:
        email_status = user.email if hasattr(user, 'email') and user.email else 'No email'
        verification = getattr(user, 'is_email_verified', 'N/A')
        print(f"  - {user.username}: {email_status} (Verified: {verification})")

# Current User state after migration cleanup
current_users = [
    User(username="new_user1", email="new1@anihpj.com", first_name="New", last_name="User1", is_email_verified=True),
    User(username="new_user2", email="new2@anihpj.com", first_name="New", last_name="User2", is_email_verified=False)
]
print(f"\nCurrent Users after cleanup: {len(current_users)}")
for user in current_users:
    print(f"  - {user.username}: {user.email} (Verified: {user.is_email_verified})")
checkpoint removed: anihpj_db:pre_user_migration checkpoint removed: anihpj_db:post_user_migration User Migration Checkpoint Cleanup: Migration checkpoints removed: 2 Total User migration data cleaned: 6 Removed Migration: pre_user_migration Users data cleared: 3 - john_doe: No email (Verified: N/A) - jane_smith: No email (Verified: N/A) - admin_user: No email (Verified: N/A) Removed Migration: post_user_migration Users data cleared: 3 - john_doe: john@anihpj.com (Verified: True) - jane_smith: jane@anihpj.com (Verified: True) - admin_user: admin@anihpj.com (Verified: True) Current Users after cleanup: 2 - new_user1: new1@anihpj.com (Verified: True) - new_user2: new2@anihpj.com (Verified: False)
Example 3: Author Profile Feature Cleanup (Development Branch Management)

Remove experimental Author model feature checkpoints after merging successful features to main branch.

# Remove experimental feature checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web author_experiment1 author_social_test author_rating_prototype

# List remaining Author development checkpoints
docker checkpoint ls anihpj_web | grep author

Django Models Context:

# Author model experimental features being cleaned up
class Author(models.Model):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    bio = models.TextField(max_length=500, blank=True)
    website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    specializations = models.ManyToManyField(Skills)
    years_experience = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    portfolio_url = models.URLField(blank=True)
    is_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"Author: {self.user.username}"

# Author experimental data from removed checkpoints
removed_author_experiments = {
    'author_experiment1': [
        Author(user_id=1, bio="Initial experiment bio", years_experience=3),
        Author(user_id=2, bio="Testing author features", years_experience=5)
    ],
    'author_social_test': [
        Author(user_id=1, website="https://johndoe.dev", portfolio_url="https://portfolio.johndoe.dev"),
        Author(user_id=2, website="https://janesmith.com", portfolio_url="https://work.janesmith.com"),
        Author(user_id=3, bio="Social features testing", years_experience=2)
    ],
    'author_rating_prototype': [
        Author(user_id=1, is_verified=True, years_experience=8),
        Author(user_id=2, is_verified=False, years_experience=4),
        Author(user_id=3, is_verified=True, years_experience=6),
        Author(user_id=4, bio="Rating system prototype", is_verified=False)
    ]
}

# Print Author feature cleanup results
print("Author Feature Experiment Cleanup:")
total_author_experiments = sum(len(authors) for authors in removed_author_experiments.values())
print(f"Experimental checkpoints removed: {len(removed_author_experiments)}")
print(f"Total Author experimental data cleaned: {total_author_experiments}")

for checkpoint_name, authors_list in removed_author_experiments.items():
    print(f"\nRemoved Experiment: {checkpoint_name}")
    print(f"Author experiments cleared: {len(authors_list)}")
    for author in authors_list:
        print(f"  - User ID {author.user_id}: Experience: {author.years_experience} years")
        print(f"    Bio: {author.bio[:30]}..." if author.bio else "    No bio")
        print(f"    Verified: {author.is_verified}")
        if hasattr(author, 'website') and author.website:
            print(f"    Website: {author.website}")

# Current Author state after experiment cleanup
final_authors = [
    Author(user_id=1, bio="Final production bio", website="https://johndoe.dev", 
           years_experience=8, is_verified=True),
    Author(user_id=2, bio="Merged features author", website="https://janesmith.com", 
           years_experience=6, is_verified=True)
]
print(f"\nFinal Authors after cleanup: {len(final_authors)}")
for author in final_authors:
    print(f"  - User ID {author.user_id}: {author.bio[:40]}...")
    print(f"    Experience: {author.years_experience} years, Verified: {author.is_verified}")

Verified: False - User ID 2: Experience: 5 years Bio: Testing author features... Verified: False Removed Experiment: author_social_test Author experiments cleared: 3 - User ID 1: Experience: 0 years No bio Verified: False Website: https://johndoe.dev - User ID 2: Experience: 0 years No bio Verified: False Website: https://janesmith.com - User ID 3: Experience: 2 years Bio: Social features testing... Verified: False Removed Experiment: author_rating_prototype Author experiments cleared: 4 - User ID 1: Experience: 8 years No bio Verified: True - User ID 2: Experience: 4 years No bio Verified: False - User ID 3: Experience: 6 years No bio Verified: True - User ID 4: Experience: 0 years Bio: Rating system prototype... Verified: False Final Authors after cleanup: 2 - User ID 1: Final production bio... Experience: 8 years, Verified: True - User ID 2: Merged features author... Experience: 6 years, Verified: True
Example 4: Location Processing Debug Cleanup (Performance Optimization)

Remove Location model performance debugging checkpoints after resolving query optimization issues.

# Remove performance debugging checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web location_slow_query location_index_test location_optimization_v1

# Verify performance checkpoint cleanup
docker checkpoint ls anihpj_web | grep -v location

Django Models Context:

# Location model performance debugging states being cleaned up
class Location(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=50, db_index=True)
    region = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=50, db_index=True)
    postal_code = models.CharField(max_length=10)
    latitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=9, decimal_places=6)
    longitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=9, decimal_places=6)
    is_remote_friendly = models.BooleanField(default=False, db_index=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['country', 'city']),
            models.Index(fields=['latitude', 'longitude'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.city}, {self.country}"

# Location performance debugging data from removed checkpoints
removed_performance_checkpoints = {
    'location_slow_query': [
        Location(name="Test Location 1", country="USA", city="San Francisco", 
                latitude=37.7749, longitude=-122.4194, is_remote_friendly=True),
        Location(name="Test Location 2", country="UK", city="London", 
                latitude=51.5074, longitude=-0.1278, is_remote_friendly=False),
        Location(name="Test Location 3", country="Germany", city="Berlin", 
                latitude=52.5200, longitude=13.4050, is_remote_friendly=True)
    ],
    'location_index_test': [
        Location(name="Index Test 1", country="Canada", city="Toronto", 
                latitude=43.6532, longitude=-79.3832, is_remote_friendly=True),
        Location(name="Index Test 2", country="Australia", city="Sydney", 
                latitude=-33.8688, longitude=151.2093, is_remote_friendly=False),
        Location(name="Index Test 3", country="Japan", city="Tokyo", 
                latitude=35.6762, longitude=139.6503, is_remote_friendly=True),
        Location(name="Index Test 4", country="France", city="Paris", 
                latitude=48.8566, longitude=2.3522, is_remote_friendly=False)
    ],
    'location_optimization_v1': [
        Location(name="Optimized Query 1", country="USA", city="New York", 
                latitude=40.7128, longitude=-74.0060, is_remote_friendly=True),
        Location(name="Optimized Query 2", country="Netherlands", city="Amsterdam", 
                latitude=52.3676, longitude=4.9041, is_remote_friendly=True)
    ]
}

# Print Location performance cleanup results
print("Location Performance Debug Cleanup:")
total_debug_locations = sum(len(locations) for locations in removed_performance_checkpoints.values())
print(f"Performance debug checkpoints removed: {len(removed_performance_checkpoints)}")
print(f"Total debug Location data cleaned: {total_debug_locations}")

for checkpoint_name, locations_list in removed_performance_checkpoints.items():
    print(f"\nRemoved Debug Session: {checkpoint_name}")
    print(f"Debug locations cleared: {len(locations_list)}")
    remote_count = sum(1 for loc in locations_list if loc.is_remote_friendly)
    print(f"Remote-friendly locations in debug: {remote_count}/{len(locations_list)}")
    
    for location in locations_list:
        print(f"  - {location.name}")
        print(f"    {location.city}, {location.country}")
        print(f"    Coordinates: ({location.latitude}, {location.longitude})")
        print(f"    Remote: {location.is_remote_friendly}")

# Current optimized Location state
optimized_locations = [
    Location(name="Production Location 1", country="USA", city="Seattle", 
            latitude=47.6062, longitude=-122.3321, is_remote_friendly=True),
    Location(name="Production Location 2", country="UK", city="Edinburgh", 
            latitude=55.9533, longitude=-3.1883, is_remote_friendly=True)
]
print(f"\nOptimized Locations in production: {len(optimized_locations)}")
for location in optimized_locations:
    print(f"  - {location.name}: {location.city}, {location.country} (Remote: {location.is_remote_friendly})")

Example 5: JobPost Queue Management Cleanup (Background Task Optimization)

Remove JobPost processing queue checkpoints after optimizing Celery worker performance and task management.

# Remove JobPost queue optimization checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_worker jobpost_queue_heavy_load jobpost_processing_bottleneck jobpost_optimization_test

# Check remaining worker checkpoints
docker checkpoint ls anihpj_worker

Django Models Context:

# JobPost model queue processing states being cleaned up
class JobPost(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    description = models.TextField()
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    location = models.ForeignKey(Location, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    required_skills = models.ManyToManyField(Skills)
    salary_min = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    salary_max = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    employment_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, default='FULL_TIME', db_index=True)
    experience_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, default='MID_LEVEL', db_index=True)
    is_remote = models.BooleanField(default=False, db_index=True)
    is_published = models.BooleanField(default=False, db_index=True)
    priority_score = models.IntegerField(default=0, db_index=True)
    processing_status = models.CharField(max_length=20, default='PENDING')
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['is_published', 'created_at']),
            models.Index(fields=['employment_type', 'experience_level']),
            models.Index(fields=['priority_score', 'processing_status'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.title

# JobPost queue optimization data from removed checkpoints
removed_queue_checkpoints = {
    'jobpost_queue_heavy_load': [
        JobPost(title="Senior Developer Heavy Load Test", author_id=1, location_id=1,
               salary_min=120000, salary_max=150000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=True, priority_score=5, processing_status='PROCESSING'),
        JobPost(title="Data Scientist Load Test", author_id=2, location_id=2,
               salary_min=100000, salary_max=130000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='MID_LEVEL', is_remote=False, priority_score=3, processing_status='PENDING'),
        JobPost(title="DevOps Engineer Load", author_id=1, location_id=3,
               salary_min=110000, salary_max=140000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=True, priority_score=4, processing_status='PROCESSING')
    ],
    'jobpost_processing_bottleneck': [
        JobPost(title="Backend Developer Bottleneck", author_id=2, location_id=4,
               salary_min=90000, salary_max=120000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='MID_LEVEL', is_remote=True, priority_score=2, processing_status='FAILED'),
        JobPost(title="Frontend Developer Bottleneck", author_id=1, location_id=5,
               salary_min=85000, salary_max=115000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='MID_LEVEL', is_remote=False, priority_score=2, processing_status='RETRY'),
        JobPost(title="QA Engineer Bottleneck", author_id=2, location_id=6,
               salary_min=70000, salary_max=95000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='MID_LEVEL', is_remote=True, priority_score=1, processing_status='FAILED')
    ],
    'jobpost_optimization_test': [
        JobPost(title="ML Engineer Optimization", author_id=1, location_id=1,
               salary_min=140000, salary_max=180000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=True, priority_score=5, processing_status='COMPLETED'),
        JobPost(title="Product Manager Optimization", author_id=2, location_id=2,
               salary_min=120000, salary_max=150000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
               experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=False, priority_score=4, processing_status='COMPLETED')
    ]
}

# Print JobPost queue cleanup results
print("JobPost Queue Optimization Cleanup:")
total_queue_jobs = sum(len(jobs) for jobs in removed_queue_checkpoints.values())
print(f"Queue optimization checkpoints removed: {len(removed_queue_checkpoints)}")
print(f"Total JobPost queue data cleaned: {total_queue_jobs}")

for checkpoint_name, jobs_list in removed_queue_checkpoints.items():
    print(f"\nRemoved Queue State: {checkpoint_name}")
    print(f"Jobs in queue cleared: {len(jobs_list)}")
    
    # Calculate queue statistics
    processing_count = sum(1 for job in jobs_list if job.processing_status == 'PROCESSING')
    completed_count = sum(1 for job in jobs_list if job.processing_status == 'COMPLETED')
    failed_count = sum(1 for job in jobs_list if job.processing_status == 'FAILED')
    avg_priority = sum(job.priority_score for job in jobs_list) / len(jobs_list) if jobs_list else 0
    remote_count = sum(1 for job in jobs_list if job.is_remote)
    
    print(f"Processing: {processing_count}, Completed: {completed_count}, Failed: {failed_count}")
    print(f"Average Priority: {avg_priority:.1f}, Remote Jobs: {remote_count}/{len(jobs_list)}")
    
    for job in jobs_list:
        print(f"  - {job.title}")
        print(f"    Salary: ${job.salary_min:,} - ${job.salary_max:,}")
        print(f"    Status: {job.processing_status}, Priority: {job.priority_score}")
        print(f"    Remote: {job.is_remote}, Level: {job.experience_level}")

# Current optimized JobPost queue state
optimized_queue = [
    JobPost(title="Optimized Senior Engineer Role", author_id=1, location_id=1,
           salary_min=130000, salary_max=160000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
           experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=True, priority_score=5, processing_status='COMPLETED'),
    JobPost(title="Optimized Team Lead Position", author_id=2, location_id=2,
           salary_min=140000, salary_max=170000, employment_type='FULL_TIME',
           experience_level='SENIOR', is_remote=True, priority_score=5, processing_status='COMPLETED')
]
print(f"\nOptimized Queue State: {len(optimized_queue)} jobs")
for job in optimized_queue:
    print(f"  - {job.title}: ${job.salary_min:,}-${job.salary_max:,}")
    print(f"    Status: {job.processing_status}, Priority: {job.priority_score}")
checkpoint removed: anihpj_worker:jobpost_queue_heavy_load checkpoint removed: anihpj_worker:jobpost_processing_bottleneck checkpoint removed: anihpj_worker:jobpost_optimization_test JobPost Queue Optimization Cleanup: Queue optimization checkpoints removed: 3 Total JobPost queue data cleaned: 8 Removed Queue State: jobpost_queue_heavy_load Jobs in queue cleared: 3 Processing: 2, Completed: 0, Failed: 0 Average Priority: 4.0, Remote Jobs: 2/3 - Senior Developer Heavy Load Test Salary: $120,000 - $150,000 Status: PROCESSING, Priority: 5 Remote: True, Level: SENIOR - Data Scientist Load Test Salary: $100,000 - $130,000 Status: PENDING, Priority: 3 Remote: False, Level: MID_LEVEL - DevOps Engineer Load Salary: $110,000 - $140,000 Status: PROCESSING, Priority: 4 Remote: True, Level: SENIOR Removed Queue State: jobpost_processing_bottleneck Jobs in queue cleared: 3 Processing: 0, Completed: 0, Failed: 2 Average Priority: 1.7, Remote Jobs: 2/3 - Backend Developer Bottleneck Salary: $90,000 - $120,000 Status: FAILED, Priority: 2 Remote: True, Level: MID_LEVEL - Frontend Developer Bottleneck Salary: $85,000 - $115,000 Status: RETRY, Priority: 2 Remote: False, Level: MID_LEVEL - QA Engineer Bottleneck Salary: $70,000 - $95,000 Status: FAILED, Priority: 1 Remote: True, Level: MID_LEVEL Removed Queue State: jobpost_optimization_test Jobs in queue cleared: 2 Processing: 0, Completed: 2, Failed: 0 Average Priority: 4.5, Remote Jobs: 1/2 - ML Engineer Optimization Salary: $140,000 - $180,000 Status: COMPLETED, Priority: 5 Remote: True, Level: SENIOR - Product Manager Optimization Salary: $120,000 - $150,000 Status: COMPLETED, Priority: 4 Remote: False, Level: SENIOR Optimized Queue State: 2 jobs - Optimized Senior Engineer Role: $130,000-$160,000 Status: COMPLETED, Priority: 5 - Optimized Team Lead Position: $140,000-$170,000 Status: COMPLETED, Priority: 5
# Remove specific checkpoint from Django web container
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web debug_state

# Remove multiple checkpoints at once
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web old_checkpoint1 old_checkpoint2

# Remove checkpoint from database container
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_db test_data_checkpoint

Key Options for anihpj Django Development

  • CONTAINER: Name or ID of the container containing the checkpoints (anihpj_web, anihpj_db, anihpj_worker).
  • CHECKPOINT: One or more checkpoint names to remove from the container.
Syntax
docker checkpoint rm [OPTIONS] CONTAINER CHECKPOINT [CHECKPOINT...]

Django-Specific Use Cases

Checkpoint Maintenance
# Clean up old Django server checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web outdated_debug_state

# Remove testing checkpoints after feature completion
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web feature_test_state pre_merge_state

# Clean up worker checkpoints from completed tasks
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_worker old_task_state job_processing_backup

Maintain clean checkpoint history by removing obsolete states after completing development phases or fixing bugs.

Real-world anihpj Examples

Cleanup Operations
# Remove completed debug checkpoint
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web jobpost_bug_reproduction

# Clean up multiple old checkpoints
docker checkpoint rm anihpj_web before_refactor old_experiment legacy_state
checkpoint removed: anihpj_web:jobpost_bug_reproduction

🔧 Compose Commands

Docker Compose commands provide multi-container application management for orchestrating your anihpj Django project services. These commands enable you to define, configure, and manage complex Django application stacks including web servers, databases, caches, background workers, and supporting services through declarative YAML configuration files.

📋 Compose Commands Overview

docker compose - Multi-container application management
docker compose up - Start and run entire application stack
docker compose down - Stop and remove application containers
docker compose build - Build or rebuild services
docker compose ps - List running containers
docker compose logs - View output from services
🎯 Django Focus: This documentation shows practical examples of orchestrating Django applications using Docker Compose with the anihpj project. Learn how to manage multi-service Django deployments including web servers, PostgreSQL databases, Redis caching, Celery workers, and nginx load balancers for your jobpost app models (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost).

docker compose

Official Docs: docker compose | Docker CLI: docker compose

Define and run multi-container Docker applications with YAML configuration.

Full Description

The docker compose command is a tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications. For your anihpj Django project, Compose enables you to configure and orchestrate all application services - web servers, databases, caching layers, background workers, and load balancers - using a single YAML configuration file that defines the entire application stack.

When managing your anihpj Django application with Compose, you define services for Django web containers, PostgreSQL databases, Redis caches, Celery workers for background tasks, nginx proxies, and any additional supporting services. Compose handles service dependencies, networking, volume management, and environment configuration, enabling consistent deployments across development, testing, and production environments for your jobpost application components.

Syntax

# Basic compose operations
docker compose [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]

# Start application stack
docker compose up

# Start in detached mode
docker compose up -d

# Stop application stack
docker compose down

# View running services
docker compose ps

# Build services
docker compose build

# View service logs
docker compose logs [SERVICE]

# Execute commands in service
docker compose exec [SERVICE] [COMMAND]

Parameters Description

  • -f, --file: Specify alternate compose file. Default is docker-compose.yml. Use for environment-specific configurations like docker-compose.dev.yml, docker-compose.prod.yml for Django deployments.
  • --profile: Specify service profiles to enable. Useful for conditional Django services like debugging tools, monitoring services, or development utilities.
  • --project-name: Specify alternate project name. Default is directory name. Use descriptive names like 'anihpj-dev', 'anihpj-staging', 'anihpj-prod' for Django environments.
  • --env-file: Specify alternate environment file. Load Django settings, database credentials, and API keys from .env files.

Attributes

  • Project Name: Unique identifier for the compose application stack. Used to namespace containers, networks, and volumes for Django application isolation.
  • Service Count: Number of defined services in compose file. Track Django web services, databases, workers, and supporting infrastructure components.
  • Network Configuration: Container networking setup for service communication. Enables Django web containers to communicate with databases and caches.
  • Volume Management: Data persistence configuration for databases, media files, and application state across container restarts.
  • Environment Variables: Configuration values for Django settings, database connections, and service-specific configurations.

Methods

Multi-Service Management

Syntax: docker compose [up|down|restart] [SERVICE...]

Parameters: SERVICE (string) - Optional service names to target specific Django application components

Start, stop, or restart specific Django services within the application stack, enabling selective service management and rolling updates.

Environment-Specific Deployment

Syntax: docker compose -f {compose-file} --env-file {env-file} up

Parameters: compose-file (string) - Environment-specific compose configuration, env-file (string) - Environment variables file

Deploy Django applications with environment-specific configurations for development, staging, and production deployments with appropriate service scaling and resource allocation.

Return Value

Returns command-specific output based on the compose operation. Shows service status, container information, logs, or error details for Django application stack management.

  • Success (0): Compose operation completed successfully
  • Error (1): Service configuration error or container runtime failure
  • Error (125): Invalid command usage or compose file syntax error

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Basic Django Application Stack (Skills API Service)

Set up complete Django application stack with web server, database, and cache for Skills model API development.

# Create and start Django application stack
docker compose up -d

# View running services
docker compose ps

# Check service logs
docker compose logs web

Django Models Context & Compose Configuration:

# docker-compose.yml for Skills API
version: '3.8'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - DEBUG=True
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:password@db:5432/anihpj_skills
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis
    volumes:
      - .:/app
    command: python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
  
  db:
    image: postgres:14
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=anihpj_skills
      - POSTGRES_USER=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
  
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"

volumes:
  postgres_data:
# Skills model for API service
class Skills(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, db_index=True)
    category = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    level = models.CharField(max_length=20)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [models.Index(fields=['category', 'level'])]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} - {self.level}"

# Skills data for API testing
skills_data = [
    Skills(name="Python", category="Programming", level="Expert", description="Advanced Python development"),
    Skills(name="Django", category="Framework", level="Expert", description="Django web framework mastery"),
    Skills(name="PostgreSQL", category="Database", level="Advanced", description="Relational database management"),
    Skills(name="Redis", category="Caching", level="Intermediate", description="In-memory data structure store")
]

# Print Skills API data
print("Skills API Service - Docker Compose Stack:")
print(f"Total Skills available: {len(skills_data)}")
for skill in skills_data:
    print(f"  - {skill.name} ({skill.category}): {skill.level}")
    print(f"    Description: {skill.description}")
anihpj-web-1 Started anihpj-db-1 Started anihpj-redis-1 Started Skills API Service - Docker Compose Stack: Total Skills available: 4 - Python (Programming): Expert Description: Advanced Python development - Django (Framework): Expert Description: Django web framework mastery - PostgreSQL (Database): Advanced Description: Relational database management - Redis (Caching): Intermediate Description: In-memory data structure store

docker compose up

Official Docs: docker compose up | Docker CLI: docker compose up

Create and start containers for all services defined in the compose file.

Full Description

The docker compose up command creates and starts containers for all services defined in your compose configuration. For your anihpj Django project, this command orchestrates the entire application stack startup including Django web servers, PostgreSQL databases, Redis caches, Celery workers, and any supporting services in the correct dependency order.

When starting your anihpj Django application with compose up, the command builds images if needed, creates containers, establishes networking between services, mounts volumes for data persistence, and starts all services according to their dependency relationships. This provides a single command to launch your complete Django development or production environment with all required services for your jobpost application features.

Syntax

# Start all services
docker compose up

# Start in detached mode
docker compose up -d

# Start specific services
docker compose up web db

# Start with build
docker compose up --build

# Start with scaling
docker compose up --scale web=3

# Start with recreation
docker compose up --force-recreate

# Start and remove orphaned containers
docker compose up --remove-orphans

Parameters Description

  • -d, --detach: Run containers in background. Essential for Django production deployments to free terminal and enable background operation.
  • --build: Build images before starting containers. Ensures Django code changes are included in the application deployment.
  • --scale: Scale services to specified number of containers. Enable Django web service scaling for load distribution and high availability.
  • --force-recreate: Recreate containers even if configuration hasn't changed. Useful for Django development when testing container configuration changes.
  • --remove-orphans: Remove containers for services not defined in current compose file. Cleanup old Django services during stack updates.

Attributes

  • Startup Order: Service dependency resolution and startup sequence. Ensures Django databases start before web services and workers.
  • Health Checks: Service readiness and health monitoring during startup. Validates Django application and database connectivity before serving requests.
  • Port Binding: Host port mapping for service access. Exposes Django web interface and database ports for development and administration.
  • Volume Mounting: Data persistence and code mounting for Django development. Enables live code reloading and database data retention.
  • Network Creation: Inter-service communication networking. Provides isolated network for Django services to communicate securely.

Methods

Scaled Service Deployment

Syntax: docker compose up --scale {service}={count}

Parameters: service (string) - Django service name to scale, count (integer) - Number of service instances

Deploy multiple instances of Django web services for load distribution and high availability in production environments.

Development Environment Startup

Syntax: docker compose up --build --force-recreate

Parameters: None - Forces complete rebuild and recreation of Django development environment

Start Django development environment with fresh builds and clean containers for testing new code changes and configurations.

Return Value

Returns startup status and service logs during container creation and initialization. Shows Django application startup progress and any service configuration errors.

  • Success (0): All services started successfully
  • Error (1): Service startup failure or dependency issues
  • Error (125): Invalid compose file or command options

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: User Authentication Service Stack (Complete Django Setup)

Start complete Django authentication service with web server, database, and session storage for User model operations.

# Start authentication service stack
docker compose up -d

# View service status
docker compose ps

# Check web service logs
docker compose logs web

Django Models Context & Stack Configuration:

# User model for authentication service
class User(models.Model):
    username = models.CharField(max_length=150, unique=True, db_index=True)
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True, db_index=True)
    password_hash = models.CharField(max_length=128)
    first_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    last_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    is_staff = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_superuser = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    last_login = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    date_joined = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['username', 'is_active']),
            models.Index(fields=['email', 'is_active'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.username

# User authentication data for testing
user_data = [
    User(username="john_doe", email="john@anihpj.com", first_name="John", last_name="Doe", is_active=True, is_staff=False),
    User(username="jane_admin", email="jane@anihpj.com", first_name="Jane", last_name="Smith", is_active=True, is_staff=True),
    User(username="bob_user", email="bob@anihpj.com", first_name="Bob", last_name="Wilson", is_active=True, is_staff=False),
    User(username="alice_super", email="alice@anihpj.com", first_name="Alice", last_name="Johnson", is_active=True, is_superuser=True)
]

# Print User authentication service data
print("User Authentication Service - Docker Compose Stack:")
print(f"Total registered users: {len(user_data)}")
staff_count = sum(1 for user in user_data if user.is_staff)
superuser_count = sum(1 for user in user_data if user.is_superuser)
print(f"Staff users: {staff_count}, Superusers: {superuser_count}")

for user in user_data:
    roles = []
    if user.is_superuser:
        roles.append("Superuser")
    elif user.is_staff:
        roles.append("Staff")
    else:
        roles.append("Regular")
    
    print(f"  - {user.username} ({user.email}): {', '.join(roles)}")
    print(f"    Name: {user.first_name} {user.last_name}, Active: {user.is_active}")
anihpj-web-1 running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-db-1 running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp User Authentication Service - Docker Compose Stack: Total registered users: 4 Staff users: 1, Superusers: 1 - john_doe (john@anihpj.com): Regular Name: John Doe, Active: True - jane_admin (jane@anihpj.com): Staff Name: Jane Smith, Active: True - bob_user (bob@anihpj.com): Regular Name: Bob Wilson, Active: True - alice_super (alice@anihpj.com): Superuser Name: Alice Johnson, Active: True
Example 2: Skills API Development Stack (Multi-Service Development)

Start Django Skills API development stack with web server, database, Redis cache, and Celery workers for background skill processing.

# Start multi-service development stack
docker compose up --build

# Scale web workers for load testing
docker compose up --scale web=2 --scale worker=3

# Monitor service startup
docker compose logs -f web worker

Django Models Context & Multi-Service Stack:

# Skills model for API development stack
class Skills(models.Model):
    SKILL_TYPES = [
        ('technical', 'Technical Skill'),
        ('soft', 'Soft Skill'), 
        ('language', 'Programming Language'),
        ('framework', 'Framework/Library'),
        ('tool', 'Development Tool'),
        ('platform', 'Platform/Infrastructure')
    ]
    
    PROFICIENCY_LEVELS = [
        ('beginner', 'Beginner (0-1 years)'),
        ('intermediate', 'Intermediate (1-3 years)'),
        ('advanced', 'Advanced (3-5 years)'),
        ('expert', 'Expert (5+ years)'),
        ('master', 'Master (10+ years)')
    ]
    
    skill_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True, db_index=True)
    category = models.CharField(max_length=50, db_index=True)
    skill_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SKILL_TYPES, default='technical')
    proficiency_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=PROFICIENCY_LEVELS, default='intermediate')
    demand_score = models.FloatField(default=50.0, help_text="Market demand (0-100)")
    growth_rate = models.FloatField(default=0.0, help_text="Annual growth percentage")
    learning_difficulty = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('easy', 'Easy'), ('moderate', 'Moderate'), ('hard', 'Hard'), ('expert', 'Expert Level')
    ], default='moderate')
    industry_relevance = models.JSONField(default=list)
    prerequisites = models.ManyToManyField('self', blank=True)
    avg_salary_impact = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0.00)
    certification_available = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_trending = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    last_updated = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['skill_type', 'proficiency_level']),
            models.Index(fields=['demand_score', 'is_trending']),
            models.Index(fields=['category', 'learning_difficulty'])
        ]
        ordering = ['-demand_score', 'name']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} ({self.proficiency_level})"

# Skills development data for multi-service stack
skills_development_data = [
    Skills(name="Python", category="Programming Languages", skill_type="language", proficiency_level="expert", 
           demand_score=98.5, growth_rate=15.2, learning_difficulty="moderate", 
           industry_relevance=["Web Development", "Data Science", "AI/ML"], avg_salary_impact=25000.00,
           certification_available=True, is_trending=True),
    Skills(name="Django", category="Web Frameworks", skill_type="framework", proficiency_level="advanced", 
           demand_score=89.3, growth_rate=12.8, learning_difficulty="moderate",
           industry_relevance=["Web Development", "API Development"], avg_salary_impact=20000.00,
           certification_available=False, is_trending=True),
    Skills(name="Docker", category="DevOps", skill_type="tool", proficiency_level="advanced", 
           demand_score=92.1, growth_rate=18.5, learning_difficulty="hard",
           industry_relevance=["DevOps", "Cloud Computing"], avg_salary_impact=22000.00,
           certification_available=True, is_trending=True),
    Skills(name="PostgreSQL", category="Databases", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="intermediate", 
           demand_score=85.7, growth_rate=8.3, learning_difficulty="moderate",
           industry_relevance=["Backend Development", "Data Engineering"], avg_salary_impact=15000.00,
           certification_available=True, is_trending=False),
    Skills(name="Redis", category="Caching", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="intermediate", 
           demand_score=78.4, growth_rate=14.1, learning_difficulty="easy",
           industry_relevance=["Backend Development", "Performance Optimization"], avg_salary_impact=12000.00,
           certification_available=False, is_trending=True)
]

# Print Skills API development stack
print("Skills API Development Stack - Multi-Service Configuration:")
print(f"Total skills in development database: {len(skills_development_data)}")
trending_skills = [s for s in skills_development_data if s.is_trending]
expert_skills = [s for s in skills_development_data if s.proficiency_level == 'expert']
print(f"Trending skills: {len(trending_skills)}, Expert level skills: {len(expert_skills)}")

avg_demand_score = sum(s.demand_score for s in skills_development_data) / len(skills_development_data)
total_salary_impact = sum(s.avg_salary_impact for s in skills_development_data)
print(f"Average demand score: {avg_demand_score:.1f}/100")
print(f"Total salary impact: ${total_salary_impact:,.0f}")

print("\n🚀 Skills API Development Services:")
for skill in skills_development_data:
    trend_icon = "📈" if skill.is_trending else "📊"
    difficulty_icon = "🟢" if skill.learning_difficulty == 'easy' else "🟡" if skill.learning_difficulty == 'moderate' else "🔴"
    cert_badge = " 📜" if skill.certification_available else ""
    
    print(f"\n  {trend_icon} {skill.name}: {skill.category}")
    print(f"     Type: {skill.skill_type.title()} | Level: {skill.proficiency_level.title()} {difficulty_icon}")
    print(f"     Demand: {skill.demand_score}/100 | Growth: +{skill.growth_rate}% annually")
    print(f"     Salary Impact: +${skill.avg_salary_impact:,.0f}{cert_badge}")
    print(f"     Industries: {', '.join(skill.industry_relevance)}")

print(f"\n🔧 Multi-Service Stack Ready: Django API + PostgreSQL + Redis + Celery workers")
print(f"   Skills processing pipeline operational for {len(skills_development_data)} skill categories")
Creating anihpj-web-1 ... done Creating anihpj-worker-1 ... done Creating anihpj-db-1 ... done Creating anihpj-redis-1 ... done Creating anihpj-celery-1 ... done anihpj-web-1 running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-web-2 running 0.0.0.0:8001->8000/tcp anihpj-worker-1 running anihpj-worker-2 running anihpj-worker-3 running anihpj-db-1 running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-celery-1 running Skills API Development Stack - Multi-Service Configuration: Total skills in development database: 5 Trending skills: 4, Expert level skills: 1 Average demand score: 88.8/100 Total salary impact: $94,000 🚀 Skills API Development Services: 📈 Python: Programming Languages Type: Language | Level: Expert 🟡 Demand: 98.5/100 | Growth: +15.2% annually Salary Impact: +$25,000 📜 Industries: Web Development, Data Science, AI/ML 📈 Django: Web Frameworks Type: Framework | Level: Advanced 🟡 Demand: 89.3/100 | Growth: +12.8% annually Salary Impact: +$20,000 Industries: Web Development, API Development 📈 Docker: DevOps Type: Tool | Level: Advanced 🔴 Demand: 92.1/100 | Growth: +18.5% annually Salary Impact: +$22,000 📜 Industries: DevOps, Cloud Computing 📊 PostgreSQL: Databases Type: Technical | Level: Intermediate 🟡 Demand: 85.7/100 | Growth: +8.3% annually Salary Impact: +$15,000 📜 Industries: Backend Development, Data Engineering 📈 Redis: Caching Type: Technical | Level: Intermediate 🟢 Demand: 78.4/100 | Growth: +14.1% annually Salary Impact: +$12,000 Industries: Backend Development, Performance Optimization 🔧 Multi-Service Stack Ready: Django API + PostgreSQL + Redis + Celery workers Skills processing pipeline operational for 5 skill categories
Example 3: Author Portfolio Production Deployment (Scaled Production Stack)

Deploy Django Author portfolio service in production with load balancing, database clustering, and monitoring for high-availability portfolio platform.

# Production deployment with scaling
docker compose up -d --scale web=3 --scale worker=2

# Force recreation for production updates
docker compose up --force-recreate --remove-orphans

# Monitor production startup
docker compose ps
docker compose logs --tail=50 web

Django Models Context & Production Portfolio:

# Author model for production portfolio platform
class Author(models.Model):
    EXPERTISE_LEVELS = [
        ('junior', 'Junior (1-3 years)'),
        ('mid', 'Mid-Level (3-6 years)'), 
        ('senior', 'Senior (6-10 years)'),
        ('lead', 'Team Lead (10-15 years)'),
        ('principal', 'Principal (15+ years)'),
        ('architect', 'Solution Architect (20+ years)')
    ]
    
    AVAILABILITY_STATUS = [
        ('available', 'Available for Work'),
        ('busy', 'Currently Busy'),
        ('selective', 'Selective Projects'),
        ('unavailable', 'Unavailable'),
        ('sabbatical', 'On Sabbatical')
    ]
    
    author_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='author_profile')
    username = models.CharField(max_length=50, unique=True, db_index=True)
    display_name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    bio = models.TextField(max_length=2000)
    expertise_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EXPERTISE_LEVELS)
    specializations = models.JSONField(default=list)
    portfolio_url = models.URLField(blank=True)
    github_username = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True, db_index=True)
    linkedin_profile = models.URLField(blank=True)
    website_url = models.URLField(blank=True)
    location = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    hourly_rate = models.DecimalField(max_digits=8, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    availability_status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=AVAILABILITY_STATUS, default='available')
    portfolio_views = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    profile_completion = models.FloatField(default=0.0)
    verification_status = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    featured_author = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    total_projects = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    client_rating = models.DecimalField(max_digits=3, decimal_places=2, default=5.0)
    response_time_hours = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=24)
    last_active = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['expertise_level', 'availability_status']),
            models.Index(fields=['featured_author', 'verification_status']),
            models.Index(fields=['location', 'timezone']),
            models.Index(fields=['client_rating', 'total_projects'])
        ]
        ordering = ['-featured_author', '-client_rating', '-total_projects']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.display_name} ({self.expertise_level})"

# Author production data for portfolio platform
production_authors = [
    Author(username="elena_architect", display_name="Elena Rodriguez", expertise_level="architect", 
           bio="Principal Software Architect with 22 years building enterprise systems", 
           specializations=["System Architecture", "Cloud Native", "Microservices", "Team Leadership"],
           github_username="elena-architect", location="San Francisco, CA", timezone="PST",
           hourly_rate=300.00, availability_status="selective", portfolio_views=2847, 
           profile_completion=98.5, verification_status=True, featured_author=True, 
           total_projects=89, client_rating=4.98, response_time_hours=6),
    Author(username="marcus_lead", display_name="Marcus Chen", expertise_level="lead",
           bio="Technical Lead specializing in scalable web applications and DevOps",
           specializations=["Full-Stack Development", "DevOps", "Team Management", "Agile"],
           github_username="marcus-lead", location="Austin, TX", timezone="CST",
           hourly_rate=180.00, availability_status="available", portfolio_views=1456,
           profile_completion=92.3, verification_status=True, featured_author=True,
           total_projects=67, client_rating=4.89, response_time_hours=4),
    Author(username="sarah_principal", display_name="Sarah Kim", expertise_level="principal",
           bio="Principal Engineer focused on AI/ML platforms and data architecture",
           specializations=["Machine Learning", "Data Engineering", "Python", "Cloud Architecture"],
           github_username="sarah-principal", location="Seattle, WA", timezone="PST", 
           hourly_rate=250.00, availability_status="busy", portfolio_views=3892,
           profile_completion=96.7, verification_status=True, featured_author=True,
           total_projects=134, client_rating=4.95, response_time_hours=8),
    Author(username="david_senior", display_name="David Thompson", expertise_level="senior",
           bio="Senior Full-Stack Developer with expertise in modern web technologies",
           specializations=["React", "Django", "PostgreSQL", "API Development"],
           github_username="david-senior", location="Remote", timezone="EST",
           hourly_rate=120.00, availability_status="available", portfolio_views=987,
           profile_completion=88.1, verification_status=True, featured_author=False,
           total_projects=45, client_rating=4.76, response_time_hours=12)
]

# Print Author portfolio production status
print("Author Portfolio Production Platform - Deployment Status:")
print(f"Total authors in production: {len(production_authors)}")
featured_authors = [a for a in production_authors if a.featured_author]
available_authors = [a for a in production_authors if a.availability_status == 'available']
print(f"Featured authors: {len(featured_authors)}, Currently available: {len(available_authors)}")

avg_rating = sum(a.client_rating for a in production_authors) / len(production_authors)
total_projects = sum(a.total_projects for a in production_authors)
total_views = sum(a.portfolio_views for a in production_authors)
print(f"Platform average rating: {avg_rating:.2f}/5.0")
print(f"Total completed projects: {total_projects}")
print(f"Total portfolio views: {total_views:,}")

print("\n💼 Production Author Portfolio Platform:")
for author in production_authors:
    level_icon = "🏛️" if author.expertise_level == 'architect' else "👑" if author.expertise_level == 'principal' else "🔥" if author.expertise_level == 'lead' else "⭐"
    status_icon = "🟢" if author.availability_status == 'available' else "🔴" if author.availability_status == 'busy' else "🟡"
    featured_badge = " 🌟 FEATURED" if author.featured_author else ""
    verified_badge = " ✅" if author.verification_status else ""
    
    print(f"\n  {level_icon} {author.display_name}: {author.expertise_level.title()}{featured_badge}{verified_badge}")
    print(f"     Status: {author.availability_status.title()} {status_icon} | Rate: ${author.hourly_rate}/hr")
    print(f"     Location: {author.location} ({author.timezone})")
    print(f"     Projects: {author.total_projects} | Rating: {author.client_rating}/5.0")
    print(f"     Portfolio Views: {author.portfolio_views:,} | Profile: {author.profile_completion}% complete")
    print(f"     Specializations: {', '.join(author.specializations[:3])}...")
    print(f"     Response Time: {author.response_time_hours}h | GitHub: @{author.github_username}")

print(f"\n🚀 Production Portfolio Platform: {len(production_authors)} authors across expertise levels")
print(f"   Load balanced with {len(featured_authors)} featured authors and high-availability deployment")
Recreating anihpj-web-1 ... done Recreating anihpj-web-2 ... done Recreating anihpj-web-3 ... done Recreating anihpj-worker-1 ... done Recreating anihpj-worker-2 ... done Recreating anihpj-db-1 ... done Recreating anihpj-redis-1 ... done Recreating anihpj-nginx-1 ... done anihpj-web-1 running anihpj-web-2 running anihpj-web-3 running anihpj-worker-1 running anihpj-worker-2 running anihpj-db-1 running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-nginx-1 running 0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:443->443/tcp Author Portfolio Production Platform - Deployment Status: Total authors in production: 4 Featured authors: 3, Currently available: 2 Platform average rating: 4.90/5.0 Total completed projects: 335 Total portfolio views: 9,182 💼 Production Author Portfolio Platform: 🏛️ Elena Rodriguez: Architect 🌟 FEATURED ✅ Status: Selective 🟡 | Rate: $300.0/hr Location: San Francisco, CA (PST) Projects: 89 | Rating: 4.98/5.0 Portfolio Views: 2,847 | Profile: 98.5% complete Specializations: System Architecture, Cloud Native, Microservices... Response Time: 6h | GitHub: @elena-architect 👑 Sarah Kim: Principal 🌟 FEATURED ✅ Status: Busy 🔴 | Rate: $250.0/hr Location: Seattle, WA (PST) Projects: 134 | Rating: 4.95/5.0 Portfolio Views: 3,892 | Profile: 96.7% complete Specializations: Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Python... Response Time: 8h | GitHub: @sarah-principal 🔥 Marcus Chen: Lead 🌟 FEATURED ✅ Status: Available 🟢 | Rate: $180.0/hr Location: Austin, TX (CST) Projects: 67 | Rating: 4.89/5.0 Portfolio Views: 1,456 | Profile: 92.3% complete Specializations: Full-Stack Development, DevOps, Team Management... Response Time: 4h | GitHub: @marcus-lead ⭐ David Thompson: Senior ✅ Status: Available 🟢 | Rate: $120.0/hr Location: Remote (EST) Projects: 45 | Rating: 4.76/5.0 Portfolio Views: 987 | Profile: 88.1% complete Specializations: React, Django, PostgreSQL... Response Time: 12h | GitHub: @david-senior 🚀 Production Portfolio Platform: 4 authors across expertise levels Load balanced with 3 featured authors and high-availability deployment
Example 4: Location Services Geo-Stack (Geographic Data Processing)

Launch Django Location services with PostGIS database, GeoServer, mapping APIs, and geographic data processing pipeline for location-based job matching.

# Start geographic services stack
docker compose up -d postgis geoserver redis

# Launch with specific location services
docker compose up web worker geolocation-api mapping-service

# Monitor geo-services startup
docker compose logs -f postgis geoserver

Django Models Context & Geographic Services:

# Location model for geographic services stack
from django.contrib.gis.db import models as gis_models
from django.db import models

class Location(models.Model):
    LOCATION_TYPES = [
        ('country', 'Country'),
        ('state', 'State/Province'),
        ('city', 'City'),
        ('metro', 'Metropolitan Area'),
        ('district', 'District/Borough'),
        ('neighborhood', 'Neighborhood'),
        ('remote', 'Remote/Virtual')
    ]
    
    ECONOMIC_ZONES = [
        ('tier_1', 'Tier 1 (Major Economic Center)'),
        ('tier_2', 'Tier 2 (Regional Hub)'),
        ('tier_3', 'Tier 3 (Emerging Market)'),
        ('startup_hub', 'Startup Hub'),
        ('tech_cluster', 'Technology Cluster'),
        ('government', 'Government Center'),
        ('industrial', 'Industrial Zone')
    ]
    
    location_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    display_name = models.CharField(max_length=250)
    location_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=LOCATION_TYPES)
    parent_location = models.ForeignKey('self', null=True, blank=True, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    country_code = models.CharField(max_length=3, db_index=True)
    state_code = models.CharField(max_length=5, blank=True)
    city_code = models.CharField(max_length=10, blank=True)
    postal_code = models.CharField(max_length=20, blank=True)
    coordinates = gis_models.PointField(geography=True, null=True, blank=True)
    bounding_box = gis_models.PolygonField(geography=True, null=True, blank=True)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    population = models.BigIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    economic_zone = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=ECONOMIC_ZONES, default='tier_2')
    cost_of_living_index = models.FloatField(default=100.0)
    tech_job_density = models.FloatField(default=1.0)
    remote_work_adoption = models.FloatField(default=0.3)
    average_salary_usd = models.DecimalField(max_digits=12, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    major_companies = models.JSONField(default=list)
    tech_stack_popular = models.JSONField(default=list)
    languages_spoken = models.JSONField(default=list)
    visa_requirements = models.JSONField(default=dict)
    quality_of_life_score = models.FloatField(default=70.0)
    internet_speed_mbps = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    last_updated = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['location_type', 'economic_zone']),
            models.Index(fields=['country_code', 'state_code']),
            models.Index(fields=['tech_job_density', 'remote_work_adoption']),
            models.Index(fields=['cost_of_living_index', 'average_salary_usd'])
        ]
        unique_together = [['name', 'location_type', 'parent_location']]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.display_name} ({self.location_type})"

# Location geographic data for processing stack
geographic_locations = [
    Location(name="San Francisco", display_name="San Francisco, CA, USA", location_type="city",
             country_code="US", state_code="CA", timezone="America/Los_Angeles", population=884363,
             economic_zone="tech_cluster", cost_of_living_index=185.7, tech_job_density=9.8,
             remote_work_adoption=0.82, average_salary_usd=165000.00,
             major_companies=["Google", "Meta", "Salesforce", "Uber", "Twitter"],
             tech_stack_popular=["Python", "JavaScript", "Go", "Java", "React"],
             languages_spoken=["English", "Chinese", "Spanish"], quality_of_life_score=78.5,
             internet_speed_mbps=150, visa_requirements={"H1B": "Available", "L1": "Available"}),
    Location(name="Austin", display_name="Austin, TX, USA", location_type="city", 
             country_code="US", state_code="TX", timezone="America/Chicago", population=964254,
             economic_zone="startup_hub", cost_of_living_index=108.3, tech_job_density=7.2,
             remote_work_adoption=0.74, average_salary_usd=125000.00,
             major_companies=["Dell", "IBM", "Oracle", "Tesla", "Apple"],
             tech_stack_popular=["JavaScript", "Python", "Java", "C#", ".NET"],
             languages_spoken=["English", "Spanish"], quality_of_life_score=82.1,
             internet_speed_mbps=125, visa_requirements={"H1B": "Available", "TN": "Available"}),
    Location(name="Bangalore", display_name="Bangalore, Karnataka, India", location_type="city",
             country_code="IN", state_code="KA", timezone="Asia/Kolkata", population=12300000,
             economic_zone="tech_cluster", cost_of_living_index=28.4, tech_job_density=8.9,
             remote_work_adoption=0.89, average_salary_usd=25000.00,
             major_companies=["Infosys", "Wipro", "Microsoft", "Google", "Amazon"],
             tech_stack_popular=["Java", "Python", "JavaScript", "React", "Angular"],
             languages_spoken=["English", "Hindi", "Kannada", "Tamil"], quality_of_life_score=65.3,
             internet_speed_mbps=85, visa_requirements={"Work Permit": "Required", "Business": "Available"}),
    Location(name="Berlin", display_name="Berlin, Germany", location_type="city",
             country_code="DE", timezone="Europe/Berlin", population=3677472,
             economic_zone="startup_hub", cost_of_living_index=72.1, tech_job_density=6.4,
             remote_work_adoption=0.91, average_salary_usd=78000.00,
             major_companies=["SAP", "Siemens", "Zalando", "Delivery Hero", "N26"],
             tech_stack_popular=["JavaScript", "Python", "Java", "PHP", "Go"],
             languages_spoken=["German", "English"], quality_of_life_score=88.7,
             internet_speed_mbps=110, visa_requirements={"EU Blue Card": "Available", "Work Permit": "Required"}),
    Location(name="Remote Global", display_name="Remote Work - Global", location_type="remote",
             country_code="WW", timezone="UTC", economic_zone="tech_cluster",
             cost_of_living_index=95.0, tech_job_density=10.0, remote_work_adoption=1.0,
             average_salary_usd=90000.00, major_companies=["GitLab", "Buffer", "Automattic", "Zapier"],
             tech_stack_popular=["JavaScript", "Python", "React", "Node.js", "AWS"],
             languages_spoken=["English"], quality_of_life_score=75.0, internet_speed_mbps=100,
             visa_requirements={"Digital Nomad": "Various"})
]

# Print Location geographic services
print("Location Geographic Services Stack - Geo-Processing Status:")
print(f"Total locations in geographic database: {len(geographic_locations)}")
tech_clusters = [loc for loc in geographic_locations if loc.economic_zone == 'tech_cluster']
remote_friendly = [loc for loc in geographic_locations if loc.remote_work_adoption >= 0.8]
print(f"Technology clusters: {len(tech_clusters)}, Remote-friendly locations: {len(remote_friendly)}")

avg_cost_index = sum(loc.cost_of_living_index for loc in geographic_locations) / len(geographic_locations)
avg_tech_density = sum(loc.tech_job_density for loc in geographic_locations) / len(geographic_locations)
print(f"Average cost of living index: {avg_cost_index:.1f}")
print(f"Average tech job density: {avg_tech_density:.1f}")

print("\n🌍 Geographic Data Processing Services:")
for location in geographic_locations:
    zone_icon = "🏙️" if location.economic_zone == 'tech_cluster' else "🚀" if location.economic_zone == 'startup_hub' else "🌐"
    remote_icon = " 🌐" if location.location_type == 'remote' else ""
    high_density = " 🔥" if location.tech_job_density >= 8.0 else ""
    
    salary_display = f"${location.average_salary_usd:,.0f}" if location.average_salary_usd else "Variable"
    
    print(f"\n  {zone_icon} {location.display_name}{remote_icon}{high_density}")
    print(f"     Type: {location.location_type.title()} | Zone: {location.economic_zone.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"     Population: {location.population:,}" if location.population else "     Population: Distributed")
    print(f"     Tech Density: {location.tech_job_density:.1f}/10 | Remote Adoption: {location.remote_work_adoption:.1%}")
    print(f"     Avg Salary: {salary_display} | Cost Index: {location.cost_of_living_index:.1f}")
    print(f"     Quality of Life: {location.quality_of_life_score:.1f}/100 | Internet: {location.internet_speed_mbps}Mbps")
    print(f"     Tech Stack: {', '.join(location.tech_stack_popular[:4])}...")
    print(f"     Companies: {', '.join(location.major_companies[:3])}...")

print(f"\n🗺️ Geo-Services Stack: PostGIS + GeoServer + Location APIs operational")
print(f"   Processing {len(geographic_locations)} locations with geographic indexing and mapping services")
Creating anihpj-postgis-1 ... done Creating anihpj-geoserver-1 ... done Creating anihpj-redis-1 ... done Creating anihpj-web-1 ... done Creating anihpj-worker-1 ... done Creating anihpj-geolocation-api-1 ... done Creating anihpj-mapping-service-1 ... done anihpj-web-1 running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-worker-1 running anihpj-postgis-1 running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-geoserver-1 running 0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp anihpj-redis-1 running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-geolocation-api-1 running 0.0.0.0:8001->8001/tcp anihpj-mapping-service-1 running 0.0.0.0:8002->8002/tcp Location Geographic Services Stack - Geo-Processing Status: Total locations in geographic database: 5 Technology clusters: 3, Remote-friendly locations: 4 Average cost of living index: 97.9 Average tech job density: 8.5 🌍 Geographic Data Processing Services: 🏙️ San Francisco, CA, USA 🔥 Type: City | Zone: Tech Cluster Population: 884,363 Tech Density: 9.8/10 | Remote Adoption: 82.0% Avg Salary: $165,000 | Cost Index: 185.7 Quality of Life: 78.5/100 | Internet: 150Mbps Tech Stack: Python, JavaScript, Go, Java... Example 5: JobPost AI Matching Engine (Advanced ML Pipeline)

Deploy Django JobPost service with AI/ML matching engine, vector database, recommendation system, and real-time job-candidate matching pipeline.

# Start AI matching engine stack  
docker compose up -d web worker ml-engine vector-db recommendation-api

# Scale ML processing for high-load matching
docker compose up --scale ml-engine=2 --scale worker=4

# Monitor AI pipeline startup
docker compose logs -f ml-engine recommendation-api vector-db

Django Models Context & AI Matching System:

# JobPost model for AI matching engine
import uuid
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.postgres.fields import ArrayField
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model

class JobPost(models.Model):
    URGENCY_LEVELS = [
        ('low', 'Standard Timeline'),
        ('medium', 'Moderate Urgency'),
        ('high', 'High Priority'),
        ('urgent', 'Urgent Filling'),
        ('critical', 'Mission Critical')
    ]
    
    EMPLOYMENT_TYPES = [
        ('full_time', 'Full-Time'),
        ('part_time', 'Part-Time'), 
        ('contract', 'Contract'),
        ('freelance', 'Freelance'),
        ('internship', 'Internship'),
        ('temporary', 'Temporary'),
        ('remote', 'Remote Only')
    ]
    
    job_id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=300, db_index=True)
    company = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    description = models.TextField()
    requirements = models.TextField()
    employment_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EMPLOYMENT_TYPES)
    urgency_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=URGENCY_LEVELS, default='medium')
    salary_min = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    salary_max = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    location = models.ForeignKey(Location, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='job_posts')
    remote_eligible = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    skills_required = ArrayField(models.CharField(max_length=100), default=list)
    skills_preferred = ArrayField(models.CharField(max_length=100), default=list)
    experience_min = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    experience_max = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    education_required = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    benefits = models.JSONField(default=list)
    company_size = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True)
    industry = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    ai_match_score = models.FloatField(default=0.0)
    vector_embedding = ArrayField(models.FloatField(), size=768, null=True, blank=True)
    semantic_tags = ArrayField(models.CharField(max_length=50), default=list)
    difficulty_score = models.FloatField(default=50.0)
    competitiveness = models.FloatField(default=50.0)
    applications_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    views_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    match_success_rate = models.FloatField(default=0.0)
    posted_by = models.ForeignKey(get_user_model(), on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    featured = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    expires_at = models.DateTimeField()
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['urgency_level', 'employment_type']),
            models.Index(fields=['ai_match_score', 'is_active']),
            models.Index(fields=['industry', 'company_size']),
            models.Index(fields=['salary_min', 'salary_max', 'remote_eligible'])
        ]
        ordering = ['-ai_match_score', '-featured', '-created_at']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} at {self.company}"

# JobPost AI matching data  
ai_matching_jobposts = [
    JobPost(title="Senior AI/ML Engineer", company="TechCorp AI", employment_type="full_time", urgency_level="high",
            salary_min=150000.00, salary_max=220000.00, remote_eligible=True,
            skills_required=["Python", "TensorFlow", "PyTorch", "Machine Learning", "Deep Learning"],
            skills_preferred=["MLOps", "Kubernetes", "AWS", "Computer Vision"], experience_min=5, experience_max=10,
            education_required="Master's in CS/ML", industry="Artificial Intelligence", ai_match_score=98.5,
            semantic_tags=["ai", "ml", "python", "tensorflow", "senior"], difficulty_score=85.0, competitiveness=92.0,
            applications_count=87, views_count=1247, match_success_rate=78.3, featured=True),
    JobPost(title="Full-Stack Python Developer", company="StartupHub", employment_type="full_time", urgency_level="medium",
            salary_min=95000.00, salary_max=130000.00, remote_eligible=True,
            skills_required=["Python", "Django", "React", "PostgreSQL", "REST APIs"],
            skills_preferred=["Docker", "AWS", "Redis", "Celery"], experience_min=3, experience_max=7,
            education_required="Bachelor's in CS", industry="Software Development", ai_match_score=89.2,
            semantic_tags=["fullstack", "python", "django", "react", "mid-level"], difficulty_score=65.0, competitiveness=78.0,
            applications_count=156, views_count=2341, match_success_rate=85.7, featured=True),
    JobPost(title="DevOps Engineer", company="CloudScale Systems", employment_type="contract", urgency_level="urgent",
            salary_min=120000.00, salary_max=160000.00, remote_eligible=True,
            skills_required=["Docker", "Kubernetes", "AWS", "Terraform", "CI/CD"],
            skills_preferred=["Ansible", "Monitoring", "GitLab", "Helm"], experience_min=4, experience_max=8,
            education_required="Bachelor's preferred", industry="Cloud Computing", ai_match_score=94.7,
            semantic_tags=["devops", "kubernetes", "aws", "terraform", "senior"], difficulty_score=78.0, competitiveness=88.0,
            applications_count=67, views_count=891, match_success_rate=72.1, featured=True),
    JobPost(title="React Frontend Developer", company="UX Innovations", employment_type="freelance", urgency_level="low",
            salary_min=80000.00, salary_max=110000.00, remote_eligible=True,
            skills_required=["React", "JavaScript", "TypeScript", "CSS", "HTML"],
            skills_preferred=["Next.js", "GraphQL", "Figma", "Testing"], experience_min=2, experience_max=5,
            education_required="Portfolio required", industry="Web Development", ai_match_score=82.1,
            semantic_tags=["frontend", "react", "javascript", "typescript", "mid-level"], difficulty_score=55.0, competitiveness=70.0,
            applications_count=203, views_count=3456, match_success_rate=91.4, featured=False),
    JobPost(title="Principal Software Architect", company="Enterprise Solutions", employment_type="full_time", urgency_level="critical",
            salary_min=200000.00, salary_max=280000.00, remote_eligible=False,
            skills_required=["System Architecture", "Java", "Microservices", "Leadership", "Cloud Architecture"],
            skills_preferred=["Spring Boot", "Event Sourcing", "Domain Driven Design"], experience_min=12, experience_max=20,
            education_required="Master's preferred", industry="Enterprise Software", ai_match_score=99.1,
            semantic_tags=["architect", "java", "microservices", "leadership", "principal"], difficulty_score=95.0, competitiveness=96.0,
            applications_count=23, views_count=456, match_success_rate=65.2, featured=True)
]

# Print JobPost AI matching engine status
print("JobPost AI Matching Engine - ML Pipeline Status:")
print(f"Total job posts in AI system: {len(ai_matching_jobposts)}")
high_match_jobs = [jp for jp in ai_matching_jobposts if jp.ai_match_score >= 90]
featured_jobs = [jp for jp in ai_matching_jobposts if jp.featured]
print(f"High AI match jobs (90+): {len(high_match_jobs)}, Featured jobs: {len(featured_jobs)}")

avg_match_score = sum(jp.ai_match_score for jp in ai_matching_jobposts) / len(ai_matching_jobposts)
total_applications = sum(jp.applications_count for jp in ai_matching_jobposts)
avg_success_rate = sum(jp.match_success_rate for jp in ai_matching_jobposts) / len(ai_matching_jobposts)
print(f"Average AI match score: {avg_match_score:.1f}/100")
print(f"Total applications processed: {total_applications:,}")
print(f"Average match success rate: {avg_success_rate:.1f}%")

print("\n🤖 AI Job Matching Engine Pipeline:")
for job in ai_matching_jobposts:
    urgency_icon = "🚨" if job.urgency_level == 'critical' else "🔥" if job.urgency_level == 'urgent' else "⭐" if job.urgency_level == 'high' else "📝"
    match_icon = "🎯" if job.ai_match_score >= 95 else "🔥" if job.ai_match_score >= 90 else "📊"
    featured_badge = " 🌟" if job.featured else ""
    remote_badge = " 🌐" if job.remote_eligible else ""
    
    salary_range = f"${job.salary_min:,.0f}-${job.salary_max:,.0f}" if job.salary_min and job.salary_max else "Competitive"
    
    print(f"\n  {urgency_icon} {job.title} at {job.company}{featured_badge}{remote_badge}")
    print(f"     Type: {job.employment_type.replace('_', ' ').title()} | Urgency: {job.urgency_level.title()}")
    print(f"     Salary: {salary_range} | Industry: {job.industry}")
    print(f"     AI Match Score: {job.ai_match_score}/100 {match_icon} | Success Rate: {job.match_success_rate}%")
    print(f"     Difficulty: {job.difficulty_score}/100 | Competition: {job.competitiveness}/100")
    print(f"     Applications: {job.applications_count} | Views: {job.views_count:,}")
    print(f"     Experience: {job.experience_min}-{job.experience_max or '?'} years")
    print(f"     Key Skills: {', '.join(job.skills_required[:4])}...")
    print(f"     Semantic Tags: {', '.join(job.semantic_tags)}")

print(f"\n🧠 AI Matching Pipeline: Vector embeddings + ML scoring + Recommendation engine")
print(f"   Processing {total_applications:,} applications across {len(ai_matching_jobposts)} job postings with AI optimization")

Semantic Tags: fullstack, python, django, react, mid-level 🚨 DevOps Engineer at CloudScale Systems 🌟 🌐 Type: Contract | Urgency: Urgent Salary: $120,000-$160,000 | Industry: Cloud Computing AI Match Score: 94.7/100 🎯 | Success Rate: 72.1% Difficulty: 78.0/100 | Competition: 88.0/100 Applications: 67 | Views: 891 Experience: 4-8 years Key Skills: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Terraform... Semantic Tags: devops, kubernetes, aws, terraform, senior 📝 React Frontend Developer at UX Innovations 🌐 Type: Freelance | Urgency: Low Salary: $80,000-$110,000 | Industry: Web Development AI Match Score: 82.1/100 📊 | Success Rate: 91.4% Difficulty: 55.0/100 | Competition: 70.0/100 Applications: 203 | Views: 3,456 Experience: 2-5 years Key Skills: React, JavaScript, TypeScript, CSS... Semantic Tags: frontend, react, javascript, typescript, mid-level 🚨 Principal Software Architect at Enterprise Solutions 🌟 Type: Full Time | Urgency: Critical Salary: $200,000-$280,000 | Industry: Enterprise Software AI Match Score: 99.1/100 🎯 | Success Rate: 65.2% Difficulty: 95.0/100 | Competition: 96.0/100 Applications: 23 | Views: 456 Experience: 12-20 years Key Skills: System Architecture, Java, Microservices, Leadership... Semantic Tags: architect, java, microservices, leadership, principal 🧠 AI Matching Pipeline: Vector embeddings + ML scoring + Recommendation engine Processing 536 applications across 5 job postings with AI optimization

docker compose down

Official Docs: docker compose down | Docker CLI: docker compose down

Stop and remove containers, networks, and optionally volumes and images created by compose up.

Full Description

The docker compose down command provides a clean shutdown and cleanup of your anihpj Django application stack. This command stops all running containers, removes containers and networks created by compose up, and optionally removes volumes containing database data or built images for complete environment cleanup.

For your anihpj Django project, compose down gracefully shuts down web servers, allows database transactions to complete, stops background workers, and cleans up networking resources. This is essential for development workflow transitions, production maintenance windows, and ensuring clean environment restarts without resource conflicts.

Syntax

# Stop and remove containers/networks
docker compose down

# Remove volumes (DANGEROUS - loses data)
docker compose down -v

# Remove images
docker compose down --rmi all

# Remove orphaned containers
docker compose down --remove-orphans

# Force removal without confirmation
docker compose down --timeout 0

# Remove specific services only
docker compose down web worker

# Verbose output
docker compose down --verbose

Parameters Description

  • -v, --volumes: Remove named volumes and anonymous volumes attached to containers. WARNING: This deletes Django database data permanently.
  • --rmi: Remove images used by services. Options: 'all' (remove all images), 'local' (remove images without custom tags).
  • --remove-orphans: Remove containers for services not defined in current compose file. Cleanup old Django services during configuration updates.
  • --timeout: Specify shutdown timeout in seconds. Set to 0 for immediate termination without graceful Django application shutdown.
  • -t, --timeout: Override container stop timeout. Default allows Django to complete current requests before shutdown.

Attributes

  • Graceful Shutdown: Allows Django applications to complete current requests and close database connections properly before stopping.
  • Resource Cleanup: Removes containers, networks, and optionally volumes to free system resources and prevent conflicts.
  • Data Persistence: By default preserves named volumes containing Django database and media files unless explicitly removed.
  • Network Isolation: Removes custom networks created for Django service communication, cleaning network namespace.
  • Image Management: Optionally removes built images to free disk space and force fresh builds on next startup.

Methods
