Building jobpost-service [+] Building 89.4s (22/22) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.ai => [internal] load .dockerignore => [internal] load metadata for python:3.11-slim => [dependencies 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim => [ml-models 1/4] COPY ai_models/ /app/ai_models/ => [ml-models 2/4] RUN pip install scikit-learn pandas numpy tensorflow => [ml-models 3/4] RUN python -c "import tensorflow as tf; print('TF version:', tf.__version__)" => [app 1/6] WORKDIR /app => [app 2/6] COPY requirements.ai.txt . => [app 3/6] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.ai.txt => [app 4/6] COPY . . => [app 5/6] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput => [app 6/6] RUN python manage.py migrate --run-syncdb => exporting to image Successfully built anihpj-jobpost-service:ai-v2.1 JobPost AI Matching Service - Build Verification: Total job posts configured for AI matching: 4 High AI matching scores (90+): 2/4 Remote/Hybrid opportunities: 4/4 Average salary range: $106,250 Total platform engagement: 788 interactions 🎯 Senior Django Developer at TechCorp Inc Senior | Full-Time | 🏢/🏠 Hybrid Salary: $100,000-150,000 | AI Match Score: 94.5% Engagement: 245 views, 18 applications 📈 Full Stack Python Engineer at StartupXYZ Mid | Full-Time | 🌍 Remote Salary: $85,000-120,000 | AI Match Score: 88.2% Engagement: 167 views, 23 applications 🎯 DevOps Engineer at CloudFirst Solutions Senior | Contract | 🌍 Remote Salary: $120-180/hr | AI Match Score: 91.7% Engagement: 198 views, 12 applications 📈 Junior Python Developer at Education Tech Co Junior | Full-Time | 🏢/🏠 Hybrid Salary: $65,000-85,000 | AI Match Score: 82.3% Engagement: 89 views, 31 applications 🤖 AI Build Complete: JobPost matching service with ML recommendation engine Real-time matching algorithm deployed with 89.2% average match accuracy

docker compose ps

Official Docs: docker compose ps | Docker CLI: docker compose ps

List containers for services defined in the compose file with their current status.

Full Description

The docker compose ps command displays the status of all containers managed by your compose configuration. For your anihpj Django project, this provides a real-time overview of your application stack including web servers, database containers, Redis cache, Celery workers, and any supporting services with their current operational status.

This command is crucial for monitoring Django application health, troubleshooting service issues, verifying deployment status, and understanding resource utilization across your anihpj jobpost application infrastructure. It shows container names, current status, port mappings, and service dependencies.

Syntax

# List all compose services
docker compose ps

# Include stopped containers
docker compose ps -a

# Show only running containers
docker compose ps --filter status=running

# Output in JSON format
docker compose ps --format json

# Show specific services
docker compose ps web db

# Quiet mode (IDs only)
docker compose ps -q

# Show all information
docker compose ps --all

Parameters Description

  • -a, --all: Show all containers including stopped ones. Essential for Django development debugging when containers exit unexpectedly.
  • --filter: Filter containers by status (running, exited, paused). Monitor specific Django service states during deployment or maintenance.
  • --format: Output format (table, json). Enable programmatic Django service monitoring and integration with automation tools.
  • -q, --quiet: Only display container IDs. Useful for Django deployment scripts and automated service management workflows.
  • --services: Display service names instead of containers. Show Django service configuration without runtime status details.

Attributes

  • Container Status: Real-time status of Django application containers (running, exited, restarting, unhealthy).
  • Port Mappings: Host-to-container port bindings for Django web interface and database access.
  • Service Names: Logical service identifiers matching Django compose configuration for easy identification.
  • Container Names: Unique container identifiers for direct Docker operations on Django services.
  • Health Status: Service health indicators for Django application components and dependencies.

Methods

Service Health Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose ps --format json | jq '.[] | select(.Health == "unhealthy")'

Parameters: JSON output filtered for unhealthy Django services

Monitor Django application health status and identify services requiring attention or restart.

Development Status Check

Syntax: docker compose ps --filter status=running

Parameters: status (string) - Filter for actively running Django services

Quick verification that all required Django development services are operational and ready for development work.

Return Value

Returns formatted table or JSON output showing container status, names, ports, and service information for Django application stack monitoring.

  • Success (0): Successfully retrieved service status information
  • Error (1): Service discovery failure or compose configuration issues
  • Empty Output: No containers found matching specified criteria

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Location-Based Job Service Monitoring (Real-time Status Check)

Monitor Django location-based job posting service stack to verify all geographic and search services are operational.

# Check location service stack status
docker compose ps

# Monitor specific location services
docker compose ps web db redis elasticsearch

# Check for any unhealthy services
docker compose ps -a --filter status=exited

Django Models Context & Service Monitoring:

# Location model for geographic job service
class Location(models.Model):
    LOCATION_TYPES = [
        ('country', 'Country'),
        ('state', 'State/Province'),
        ('city', 'City'),
        ('region', 'Region'),
        ('remote', 'Remote Location')
    ]
    
    TIME_ZONES = [
        ('UTC', 'UTC'),
        ('EST', 'Eastern Standard Time'),
        ('CST', 'Central Standard Time'),
        ('MST', 'Mountain Standard Time'),
        ('PST', 'Pacific Standard Time'),
        ('GMT', 'Greenwich Mean Time')
    ]
    
    location_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True, db_index=True)
    location_name = models.CharField(max_length=100, db_index=True)
    location_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=LOCATION_TYPES, default='city')
    country = models.CharField(max_length=50, db_index=True)
    state_province = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True)
    postal_code = models.CharField(max_length=20, blank=True)
    latitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=7, null=True, blank=True)
    longitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=7, null=True, blank=True)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=5, choices=TIME_ZONES, default='UTC')
    is_remote_friendly = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    population = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    cost_of_living_index = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    job_market_score = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=50, help_text="1-100 scale")
    created_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['location_type', 'country']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_remote_friendly', 'job_market_score']),
            models.Index(fields=['latitude', 'longitude']),
            models.Index(fields=['location_name', 'location_type'])
        ]
        unique_together = [['location_name', 'country', 'state_province']]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.location_name}, {self.country}"

# Location-based job service data
location_services = [
    Location(location_name="San Francisco", location_type="city", country="United States", 
             state_province="California", latitude=37.7749, longitude=-122.4194, 
             timezone="PST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=874784, 
             cost_of_living_index=185, job_market_score=95),
    Location(location_name="Austin", location_type="city", country="United States", 
             state_province="Texas", latitude=30.2672, longitude=-97.7431, 
             timezone="CST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=964254, 
             cost_of_living_index=98, job_market_score=88),
    Location(location_name="Remote Work", location_type="remote", country="Global", 
             timezone="UTC", is_remote_friendly=True, job_market_score=92),
    Location(location_name="Toronto", location_type="city", country="Canada", 
             state_province="Ontario", latitude=43.6532, longitude=-79.3832, 
             timezone="EST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=2794356, 
             cost_of_living_index=78, job_market_score=82)
]

# Print Location service monitoring status
print("Location-Based Job Service - Docker Compose Status Monitoring:")
print(f"Total geographic locations configured: {len(location_services)}")
remote_locations = sum(1 for loc in location_services if loc.is_remote_friendly)
print(f"Remote-friendly locations: {remote_locations}/{len(location_services)}")

high_market_locations = [loc for loc in location_services if loc.job_market_score >= 85]
print(f"High job market locations (85+ score): {len(high_market_locations)}")

# Service status simulation
service_status = {
    "anihpj-web-1": {"status": "running", "ports": "0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp", "health": "healthy"},
    "anihpj-db-1": {"status": "running", "ports": "0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp", "health": "healthy"}, 
    "anihpj-redis-1": {"status": "running", "ports": "0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp", "health": "healthy"},
    "anihpj-elasticsearch-1": {"status": "running", "ports": "0.0.0.0:9200->9200/tcp", "health": "healthy"}
}

print(f"\n📊 Service Stack Status:")
for service, details in service_status.items():
    status_icon = "🟢" if details["status"] == "running" else "🔴"
    health_icon = "✅" if details["health"] == "healthy" else "⚠️"
    print(f"  {status_icon} {service}: {details['status'].title()} {health_icon}")
    print(f"     Ports: {details['ports']}")

for location in location_services:
    coordinates = f"({location.latitude}, {location.longitude})" if location.latitude else "No coordinates"
    market_status = "🔥 Hot Market" if location.job_market_score >= 90 else "📈 Good Market" if location.job_market_score >= 80 else "📊 Moderate"
    remote_indicator = "🌍 Remote OK" if location.is_remote_friendly else "🏢 On-site"
    
    print(f"\n  📍 {location.location_name}: {location.location_type.title()} | {market_status}")
    print(f"     Location: {location.country}, TZ: {location.timezone} | {remote_indicator}")
    if location.population:
        print(f"     Population: {location.population:,}, Cost Index: {location.cost_of_living_index}")
    print(f"     Job Market Score: {location.job_market_score}/100 {coordinates}")

print(f"\n🎯 Monitoring Summary: All location-based job services operational")
print(f"   Geographic coverage: {len(location_services)} locations across multiple regions")
print(f"   High-demand markets: {len(high_market_locations)} locations with 85+ job scores")
NAME COMMAND SERVICE STATUS PORTS anihpj-web-1 "python manage.py run…" web running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-db-1 "docker-entrypoint.s…" db running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 "docker-entrypoint.s…" redis running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-elasticsearch-1 "/bin/tini -- /usr/l…" elasticsearch running 0.0.0.0:9200->9200/tcp Location-Based Job Service - Docker Compose Status Monitoring: Total geographic locations configured: 4 Remote-friendly locations: 4/4 High job market locations (85+ score): 3 📊 Service Stack Status: 🟢 anihpj-web-1: Running ✅ Ports: 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp 🟢 anihpj-db-1: Running ✅ Ports: 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp 🟢 anihpj-redis-1: Running ✅ Ports: 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp 🟢 anihpj-elasticsearch-1: Running ✅ Ports: 0.0.0.0:9200->9200/tcp 📍 San Francisco: City | 🔥 Hot Market Location: United States, TZ: PST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 874,784, Cost Index: 185 Job Market Score: 95/100 (37.7749, -122.4194) 📍 Austin: City | 📈 Good Market Location: United States, TZ: CST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 964,254, Cost Index: 98 Job Market Score: 88/100 (30.2672, -97.7431) 📍 Remote Work: Remote | 🔥 Hot Market Location: Global, TZ: UTC | 🌍 Remote OK Job Market Score: 92/100 No coordinates 📍 Toronto: City | 📈 Good Market Location: Canada, TZ: EST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 2,794,356, Cost Index: 78 Job Market Score: 82/100 (43.6532, -79.3832) 🎯 Monitoring Summary: All location-based job services operational Geographic coverage: 4 locations across multiple regions High-demand markets: 3 locations with 85+ job scores
Example 2: Production Health Dashboard (Service Availability Monitoring)

Monitor Django anihpj production service health for User authentication availability, JobPost search responsiveness, Skills verification uptime, Author profile access, and Location geocoding services.

# Check all services with health status
docker compose ps

# Filter for unhealthy services
docker compose ps --filter health=unhealthy

# JSON output for monitoring integration
docker compose ps --format json

# Check specific critical services
docker compose ps web db redis

Django Models Context & Health Monitoring:

# Production health dashboard for Django anihpj services
# Monitoring User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service health

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Health monitoring context
health_context = {
    "monitoring_id": "health_dash_2026_01_14",
    "environment": "production",
    "monitoring_interval": "30_seconds"
}

# Service health status
service_health = {
    "web": {
        "container": "anihpj-web-1",
        "status": "running",
        "health": "healthy",
        "uptime": "12d 5h 23m",
        "restarts": 0,
        "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "response_time_ms": 45,
        "request_count_24h": 124567
    },
    "db": {
        "container": "anihpj-db-1",
        "status": "running",
        "health": "healthy",
        "uptime": "30d 2h 15m",
        "restarts": 1,
        "models_stored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "connections": 18,
        "query_performance": "optimal"
    },
    "redis": {
        "container": "anihpj-redis-1",
        "status": "running",
        "health": "healthy",
        "uptime": "30d 2h 15m",
        "restarts": 0,
        "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
        "memory_usage_mb": 156,
        "hit_rate_percent": 94.2
    },
    "elasticsearch": {
        "container": "anihpj-elasticsearch-1",
        "status": "running",
        "health": "healthy",
        "uptime": "15d 8h 45m",
        "restarts": 2,
        "models_indexed": ["JobPost", "Skills"],
        "index_size_gb": 12.4,
        "search_performance": "good"
    },
    "worker": {
        "container": "anihpj-worker-1",
        "status": "running",
        "health": "healthy",
        "uptime": "12d 5h 20m",
        "restarts": 3,
        "models_processed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
        "queue_length": 42,
        "processing_rate": "235/min"
    }
}

# SLA tracking
sla_metrics = {
    "User_model_sla": {
        "availability_target": "99.9%",
        "current_availability": "99.94%",
        "uptime_24h": "23h 59m",
        "incidents_24h": 0,
        "status": "meeting_sla"
    },
    "JobPost_model_sla": {
        "availability_target": "99.5%",
        "current_availability": "99.87%",
        "uptime_24h": "23h 58m",
        "incidents_24h": 1,
        "status": "meeting_sla"
    },
    "Skills_model_sla": {
        "availability_target": "99.0%",
        "current_availability": "99.45%",
        "uptime_24h": "23h 52m",
        "incidents_24h": 2,
        "status": "meeting_sla"
    },
    "Author_model_sla": {
        "availability_target": "99.0%",
        "current_availability": "99.78%",
        "uptime_24h": "23h 57m",
        "incidents_24h": 0,
        "status": "meeting_sla"
    },
    "Location_model_sla": {
        "availability_target": "98.5%",
        "current_availability": "99.23%",
        "uptime_24h": "23h 49m",
        "incidents_24h": 3,
        "status": "meeting_sla"
    }
}

# Print health dashboard
print("Django Production Health Dashboard - anihpj Services:")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"Monitoring: {health_context['monitoring_id']}")
print(f"Environment: {health_context['environment'].title()}")

print(f"\n💚 Service Health Status:")
for service, details in service_health.items():
    service_name = service.title()
    status = details['status'].title()
    health = details['health'].title()
    uptime = details['uptime']
    
    health_icon = "💚" if health == "Healthy" else "💔"
    
    print(f"\n   {health_icon} {service_name} Service:")
    print(f"      Status: {status} | Health: {health}")
    print(f"      Uptime: {uptime} | Restarts: {details['restarts']}")

print(f"\n📊 Model SLA Compliance:")
for model, sla in sla_metrics.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_sla', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    target = sla['availability_target']
    current = sla['current_availability']
    status = sla['status'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    icon = "🟢" if status == "Meeting Sla" else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Target: {target} | Current: {current}")
    print(f"      Incidents (24h): {sla['incidents_24h']}")

print(f"\n✅ Health Summary:")
print(f"   💚 All 5 services healthy")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models meeting SLA")
print(f"   🎯 Production availability: 99.85%")
NAME COMMAND SERVICE STATUS HEALTH PORTS anihpj-web-1 "gunicorn" web running healthy 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-db-1 "postgres" db running healthy 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 "redis-server" redis running healthy 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-elasticsearch-1 "elasticsearch" elasticsearch running healthy 0.0.0.0:9200->9200/tcp anihpj-worker-1 "celery worker" worker running healthy Django Production Health Dashboard - anihpj Services: ========================================================== Monitoring: health_dash_2026_01_14 Environment: Production 💚 Service Health Status: 💚 Web Service: Status: Running | Health: Healthy Uptime: 12d 5h 23m | Restarts: 0 💚 Db Service: Status: Running | Health: Healthy Uptime: 30d 2h 15m | Restarts: 1 💚 Redis Service: Status: Running | Health: Healthy Uptime: 30d 2h 15m | Restarts: 0 📊 Model SLA Compliance: 🟢 User Model: Target: 99.9% | Current: 99.94% Incidents (24h): 0 🟢 Jobpost Model: Target: 99.5% | Current: 99.87% Incidents (24h): 1 ✅ Health Summary: 💚 All 5 services healthy 📊 All 5 Django models meeting SLA 🎯 Production availability: 99.85%
Example 3: Development Environment Quick Check (Rapid Status Verification)

Quickly verify Django anihpj development environment readiness for User model development, JobPost feature work, Skills testing, Author functionality, and Location service development.

# Quick status check all services
docker compose ps

# Check only running services
docker compose ps --filter status=running

# Compact output for scripting
docker compose ps -q

# Service names only
docker compose ps --services

Django Models Context & Development Verification:

# Development environment verification for Django anihpj
# Verifying User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location development readiness

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Development context
dev_context = {
    "check_id": "dev_check_2026_01_14",
    "developer": "development_team",
    "check_type": "pre_development_verification"
}

# Service status
service_status = {
    "web": {"status": "running", "ready": True},
    "db": {"status": "running", "ready": True},
    "redis": {"status": "running", "ready": True},
    "mailhog": {"status": "running", "ready": True}
}

# Model development readiness
model_readiness = {
    "User": {
        "database_ready": True,
        "migrations_applied": True,
        "test_data_loaded": True,
        "endpoints_available": ["login", "register", "profile"],
        "ready_for_development": True
    },
    "JobPost": {
        "database_ready": True,
        "migrations_applied": True,
        "test_data_loaded": True,
        "endpoints_available": ["create", "list", "detail", "search"],
        "ready_for_development": True
    },
    "Skills": {
        "database_ready": True,
        "migrations_applied": True,
        "test_data_loaded": True,
        "endpoints_available": ["list", "verify", "demand"],
        "ready_for_development": True
    },
    "Author": {
        "database_ready": True,
        "migrations_applied": True,
        "test_data_loaded": True,
        "endpoints_available": ["profile", "portfolio", "ratings"],
        "ready_for_development": True
    },
    "Location": {
        "database_ready": True,
        "migrations_applied": True,
        "test_data_loaded": True,
        "endpoints_available": ["geocode", "search", "market"],
        "ready_for_development": True
    }
}

# Print verification report
print("Django Development Environment - Quick Status Check:")
print("=" * 55)

print(f"\n✅ Services Status:")
all_ready = all(svc['ready'] for svc in service_status.values())
for svc, details in service_status.items():
    icon = "🟢" if details['ready'] else "🔴"
    print(f"   {icon} {svc}: {details['status']}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Readiness:")
for model, ready in model_readiness.items():
    icon = "✅" if ready['ready_for_development'] else "❌"
    print(f"   {icon} {model}: Ready")

print(f"\n🎯 Development Ready: {'Yes' if all_ready else 'No'}")
print(f"   All services running")
print(f"   All models ready")
print(f"   Begin development!")
NAME SERVICE STATUS PORTS anihpj-web-1 web running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj-db-1 db running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj-redis-1 redis running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj-mailhog-1 mailhog running 0.0.0.0:8025->8025/tcp Django Development Environment - Quick Status Check: ======================================================= ✅ Services Status: 🟢 web: running 🟢 db: running 🟢 redis: running 🟢 mailhog: running 📊 Model Readiness: ✅ User: Ready ✅ JobPost: Ready ✅ Skills: Ready ✅ Author: Ready ✅ Location: Ready 🎯 Development Ready: Yes All services running All models ready Begin development!
Example 4: Troubleshooting Failed Services (Issue Diagnosis)

Diagnose Django anihpj service failures affecting User authentication, JobPost operations, Skills verification, Author profiles, and Location services with detailed status information.

# Show all services including stopped
docker compose ps -a

# Filter for exited services
docker compose ps --filter status=exited

# Get exit codes for failed services
docker compose ps --format json | jq '.[] | select(.State == "exited")'

# Check unhealthy services
docker compose ps | grep -i unhealthy

Django Models Context & Failure Diagnosis:

# Service failure diagnosis for Django anihpj
# Diagnosing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service issues

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Troubleshooting context
troubleshoot_context = {
    "session_id": "troubleshoot_2026_01_14",
    "trigger": "service_failures_detected",
    "severity": "high"
}

# Service failure details
service_failures = {
    "worker": {
        "status": "exited",
        "exit_code": 137,
        "reason": "oom_killed",
        "last_restart": "2026-01-14T10:23:15Z",
        "restart_count": 5,
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "Skills", "User"],
        "impact": "background_job_processing_halted"
    },
    "elasticsearch": {
        "status": "unhealthy",
        "health_check_failures": 3,
        "reason": "cluster_yellow_status",
        "last_restart": "2026-01-14T09:45:00Z",
        "restart_count": 2,
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "Skills"],
        "impact": "search_degraded_performance"
    }
}

# Model impact analysis
model_impact = {
    "JobPost": {
        "affected_operations": ["search", "recommendations", "notifications"],
        "severity": "high",
        "user_impact": "search_results_delayed",
        "workaround": "use_cached_search_results"
    },
    "Skills": {
        "affected_operations": ["verification", "demand_analysis"],
        "severity": "medium",
        "user_impact": "verification_temporarily_unavailable",
        "workaround": "queue_verification_requests"
    },
    "User": {
        "affected_operations": ["password_reset_emails", "notifications"],
        "severity": "medium",
        "user_impact": "email_delivery_delayed",
        "workaround": "email_queued_for_retry"
    }
}

# Resolution steps
resolution_steps = [
    {"step": 1, "action": "increase_worker_memory_limit", "estimated_time": "5_min"},
    {"step": 2, "action": "restart_elasticsearch_cluster", "estimated_time": "2_min"},
    {"step": 3, "action": "verify_all_services_healthy", "estimated_time": "1_min"}
]

# Print diagnosis report
print("Django Service Failure Diagnosis - anihpj Troubleshooting:")
print("=" * 61)

print(f"\n🚨 Failed Services:")
for svc, details in service_failures.items():
    print(f"\n   🔴 {svc.title()}:")
    print(f"      Status: {details['status']}")
    print(f"      Reason: {details['reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Models: {', '.join(details['models_affected'])}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Impact:")
for model, impact in model_impact.items():
    severity = impact['severity'].title()
    icon = "🔴" if severity == "High" else "🟡"
    print(f"\n   {icon} {model} Model ({severity}):")
    print(f"      Operations: {len(impact['affected_operations'])}")
    print(f"      Impact: {impact['user_impact'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🔧 Resolution Steps:")
for step in resolution_steps:
    print(f"   {step['step']}. {step['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()} ({step['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')})")

print(f"\n✅ Estimated Recovery: 8 minutes")
NAME SERVICE STATUS EXIT CODE anihpj-web-1 web running anihpj-db-1 db running anihpj-redis-1 redis running anihpj-worker-1 worker exited 137 anihpj-elasticsearch-1 elasticsearch unhealthy Django Service Failure Diagnosis - anihpj Troubleshooting: ============================================================= 🚨 Failed Services: 🔴 Worker: Status: exited Reason: Oom Killed Models: JobPost, Skills, User 🔴 Elasticsearch: Status: unhealthy Reason: Cluster Yellow Status Models: JobPost, Skills 📊 Model Impact: 🔴 Jobpost Model (High): Operations: 3 Impact: Search Results Delayed 🟡 Skills Model (Medium): Operations: 2 Impact: Verification Temporarily Unavailable 🔧 Resolution Steps: 1. Increase Worker Memory Limit (5 min) 2. Restart Elasticsearch Cluster (2 min) 3. Verify All Services Healthy (1 min) ✅ Estimated Recovery: 8 minutes
Example 5: Resource Usage Monitoring (Performance Analysis)

Monitor Django anihpj service resource usage for User authentication load, JobPost search capacity, Skills verification performance, Author profile access, and Location geocoding efficiency.

# List all services
docker compose ps

# Get container IDs for resource checking
docker compose ps -q

# Check with stats (requires additional docker stats)
docker compose ps -q | xargs docker stats --no-stream

# Monitor specific high-load services
docker compose ps web worker elasticsearch

Django Models Context & Resource Monitoring:

# Resource usage monitoring for Django anihpj services
# Analyzing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location resource consumption

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Resource monitoring context
resource_context = {
    "monitoring_id": "resource_mon_2026_01_14",
    "monitoring_type": "performance_analysis",
    "sample_period": "5_minutes"
}

# Service resource usage
resource_usage = {
    "web": {
        "cpu_percent": 45.2,
        "memory_mb": 512,
        "memory_limit_mb": 1024,
        "memory_percent": 50.0,
        "network_rx_mb": 234.5,
        "network_tx_mb": 456.7,
        "models_load": {
            "User": "medium",
            "JobPost": "high",
            "Skills": "medium",
            "Author": "low",
            "Location": "medium"
        }
    },
    "db": {
        "cpu_percent": 28.5,
        "memory_mb": 384,
        "memory_limit_mb": 2048,
        "memory_percent": 18.8,
        "disk_io_read_mb": 1234,
        "disk_io_write_mb": 567,
        "active_connections": 18
    },
    "elasticsearch": {
        "cpu_percent": 62.8,
        "memory_mb": 2048,
        "memory_limit_mb": 4096,
        "memory_percent": 50.0,
        "index_size_gb": 12.4,
        "search_queries_per_sec": 45
    },
    "worker": {
        "cpu_percent": 35.6,
        "memory_mb": 756,
        "memory_limit_mb": 1024,
        "memory_percent": 73.8,
        "tasks_processed": 14523,
        "queue_length": 42
    }
}

# Model performance metrics
model_metrics = {
    "User": {
        "requests_per_min": 234,
        "avg_response_ms": 45,
        "cache_hit_rate": 94.2,
        "resource_efficiency": "good"
    },
    "JobPost": {
        "requests_per_min": 567,
        "avg_response_ms": 120,
        "cache_hit_rate": 78.5,
        "resource_efficiency": "moderate"
    },
    "Skills": {
        "requests_per_min": 89,
        "avg_response_ms": 250,
        "cache_hit_rate": 85.3,
        "resource_efficiency": "good"
    },
    "Author": {
        "requests_per_min": 45,
        "avg_response_ms": 67,
        "cache_hit_rate": 91.2,
        "resource_efficiency": "excellent"
    },
    "Location": {
        "requests_per_min": 123,
        "avg_response_ms": 89,
        "cache_hit_rate": 88.7,
        "resource_efficiency": "good"
    }
}

# Print resource report
print("Django Resource Usage Monitoring - anihpj Performance:")
print("=" * 58)

print(f"\n💻 Service Resource Usage:")
for svc, usage in resource_usage.items():
    cpu = usage['cpu_percent']
    mem_pct = usage['memory_percent']
    
    cpu_icon = "🔴" if cpu > 80 else "🟡" if cpu > 60 else "🟢"
    mem_icon = "🔴" if mem_pct > 80 else "🟡" if mem_pct > 60 else "🟢"
    
    print(f"\n   {svc.title()}:")
    print(f"      {cpu_icon} CPU: {cpu}%")
    print(f"      {mem_icon} Memory: {mem_pct}%")

print(f"\n📊 Model Performance:")
for model, metrics in model_metrics.items():
    rpm = metrics['requests_per_min']
    response = metrics['avg_response_ms']
    efficiency = metrics['resource_efficiency'].title()
    
    icon = "🟢" if efficiency == "Excellent" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {model}:")
    print(f"      Requests: {rpm}/min")
    print(f"      Response: {response}ms")
    print(f"      Efficiency: {efficiency}")

print(f"\n✅ Resource Summary:")
print(f"   Overall system health: Good")
print(f"   All models performing within targets")
NAME SERVICE STATUS anihpj-web-1 web running anihpj-db-1 db running anihpj-redis-1 redis running anihpj-elasticsearch-1 elasticsearch running anihpj-worker-1 worker running Django Resource Usage Monitoring - anihpj Performance: ========================================================== 💻 Service Resource Usage: Web: 🟡 CPU: 45.2% 🟢 Memory: 50.0% Db: 🟢 CPU: 28.5% 🟢 Memory: 18.8% Elasticsearch: 🟡 CPU: 62.8% 🟢 Memory: 50.0% Worker: 🟢 CPU: 35.6% 🟡 Memory: 73.8% 📊 Model Performance: 🟡 User: Requests: 234/min Response: 45ms Efficiency: Good 🟡 Jobpost: Requests: 567/min Response: 120ms Efficiency: Moderate 🟢 Author: Requests: 45/min Response: 67ms Efficiency: Excellent ✅ Resource Summary: Overall system health: Good All models performing within targets

docker compose logs

Official Docs: docker compose logs | Docker CLI: docker compose logs

View output from containers for debugging, monitoring, and troubleshooting Django services.

Full Description

The docker compose logs command displays log output from containers in your anihpj Django application stack. This command is essential for debugging application issues, monitoring request patterns, tracking database queries, analyzing performance bottlenecks, and understanding user behavior in your jobpost application.

For Django development and production environments, logs provide crucial insights into application startup sequences, error conditions, database connection issues, authentication problems, and API request flows. The command supports real-time log following, filtering by service, and timestamp formatting for comprehensive Django application monitoring.

Syntax

# View all service logs
docker compose logs

# Follow logs in real-time
docker compose logs -f

# View specific service logs
docker compose logs web

# Show timestamps
docker compose logs -t web

# Limit output lines
docker compose logs --tail 100 web

# Follow logs for multiple services
docker compose logs -f web db

# Show logs since specific time
docker compose logs --since 2024-01-01T10:00:00

Parameters Description

  • -f, --follow: Follow log output in real-time. Essential for monitoring live Django application behavior during development and production operations.
  • -t, --timestamps: Show timestamps for each log entry. Crucial for Django debugging to understand event timing and sequence of operations.
  • --tail: Number of lines to show from end of logs. Limit Django log output to recent entries for focused troubleshooting.
  • --since: Show logs since timestamp or duration. Filter Django logs to specific time periods for incident analysis.
  • --until: Show logs before specific timestamp. Analyze Django application behavior during specific time windows.

Attributes

  • Multi-service Output: Aggregated logs from all Django services with color-coded service identification for easy debugging.
  • Real-time Streaming: Live log streaming for monitoring Django application behavior and immediate issue detection.
  • Timestamp Precision: Detailed timestamps for Django request timing analysis and performance optimization.
  • Service Filtering: Specific service log viewing for focused Django component debugging and monitoring.
  • Log Persistence: Access to historical log data for Django application trend analysis and incident investigation.

Methods

Production Error Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose logs -f --tail 50 web | grep -i error

Parameters: Real-time error filtering for Django application monitoring

Monitor Django production applications for errors and exceptions with immediate notification of critical issues.

Performance Analysis

Syntax: docker compose logs --since 1h web | grep -E "(slow|timeout|performance)"

Parameters: Time-based performance log analysis for Django optimization

Analyze Django application performance issues and identify slow queries or timeout problems.

Return Value

Returns formatted log output with service names, timestamps, and log messages from Django application containers for comprehensive debugging and monitoring.

  • Success (0): Successfully retrieved and displayed service logs
  • Error (1): Service access failure or log retrieval issues
  • Interactive Mode: Real-time log streaming until manual interruption

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: JobPost Application Debugging (Error Analysis and Performance Monitoring)

Analyze JobPost creation and application submission errors, monitor database performance, and track user interaction patterns.

# Monitor JobPost application logs in real-time
docker compose logs -f web

# Analyze recent JobPost errors
docker compose logs --tail 200 web | grep -i "error\|exception\|failed"

# Check database performance for JobPost queries
docker compose logs --since 30m db | grep -i "slow\|duration"

Django Models Context & Log Analysis:

# JobPost model for application debugging
class JobPost(models.Model):
    JOB_TYPES = [
        ('full_time', 'Full Time'),
        ('part_time', 'Part Time'),
        ('contract', 'Contract'),
        ('freelance', 'Freelance'),
        ('internship', 'Internship'),
        ('temporary', 'Temporary')
    ]
    
    EXPERIENCE_LEVELS = [
        ('entry', 'Entry Level'),
        ('mid', 'Mid Level'),
        ('senior', 'Senior Level'),
        ('lead', 'Lead/Principal'),
        ('executive', 'Executive')
    ]
    
    SALARY_RANGES = [
        ('30k-50k', '$30,000 - $50,000'),
        ('50k-75k', '$50,000 - $75,000'),
        ('75k-100k', '$75,000 - $100,000'),
        ('100k-150k', '$100,000 - $150,000'),
        ('150k-200k', '$150,000 - $200,000'),
        ('200k+', '$200,000+')
    ]
    
    job_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True, db_index=True)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    company_name = models.CharField(max_length=150, db_index=True)
    location = models.ForeignKey('Location', on_delete=models.CASCADE, db_index=True)
    job_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=JOB_TYPES, default='full_time')
    experience_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EXPERIENCE_LEVELS, default='mid')
    salary_range = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SALARY_RANGES, blank=True)
    description = models.TextField()
    requirements = models.TextField()
    benefits = models.TextField(blank=True)
    skills_required = models.ManyToManyField('Skills', related_name='job_posts')
    posted_by = models.ForeignKey('Author', on_delete=models.CASCADE, db_index=True)
    applications_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    views_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True, db_index=True)
    is_featured = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    application_deadline = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    created_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True, db_index=True)
    updated_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['job_type', 'experience_level']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_active', 'created_date']),
            models.Index(fields=['company_name', 'location']),
            models.Index(fields=['salary_range', 'job_type'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} at {self.company_name}"

# JobPost application log scenarios
job_posts_logs = [
    {
        "job_id": 1,
        "title": "Senior Python Django Developer",
        "company": "TechCorp Inc",
        "log_events": [
            "2024-01-11 14:30:15 [INFO] JobPost created successfully: Senior Python Django Developer",
            "2024-01-11 14:30:16 [INFO] Skills attached: Python Django, PostgreSQL, Docker",
            "2024-01-11 14:35:22 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: sarah_dev",
            "2024-01-11 14:40:33 [INFO] Application submitted for JobPost #1",
            "2024-01-11 14:40:34 [INFO] Email notification sent to TechCorp Inc"
        ],
        "applications": 3,
        "views": 45,
        "status": "active"
    },
    {
        "job_id": 2,
        "title": "DevOps Engineer",
        "company": "CloudSoft Solutions",
        "log_events": [
            "2024-01-11 15:15:10 [INFO] JobPost created successfully: DevOps Engineer",
            "2024-01-11 15:15:11 [ERROR] Failed to attach skill: Kubernetes (Skill not found)",
            "2024-01-11 15:16:05 [INFO] Skill issue resolved, JobPost updated",
            "2024-01-11 15:20:18 [WARNING] Slow query detected: JobPost skills lookup (2.3s)",
            "2024-01-11 15:25:44 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: alex_devops"
        ],
        "applications": 1,
        "views": 23,
        "status": "active"
    },
    {
        "job_id": 3,
        "title": "Frontend React Developer", 
        "company": "StartupXYZ",
        "log_events": [
            "2024-01-11 16:00:01 [INFO] JobPost created successfully: Frontend React Developer",
            "2024-01-11 16:05:15 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: emma_frontend",
            "2024-01-11 16:10:22 [ERROR] Application submission failed: Invalid email format",
            "2024-01-11 16:10:45 [INFO] Application resubmitted successfully",
            "2024-01-11 16:15:30 [INFO] JobPost featured status activated"
        ],
        "applications": 2,
        "views": 67,
        "status": "featured"
    }
]

# Print JobPost application log analysis
print("JobPost Application - Docker Compose Log Analysis:")
print(f"Total job posts being monitored: {len(job_posts_logs)}")

total_applications = sum(job['applications'] for job in job_posts_logs)
total_views = sum(job['views'] for job in job_posts_logs)
print(f"Total applications: {total_applications}, Total views: {total_views}")

# Log event analysis
log_levels = {"INFO": 0, "WARNING": 0, "ERROR": 0}
for job_post in job_posts_logs:
    for event in job_post["log_events"]:
        for level in log_levels:
            if f"[{level}]" in event:
                log_levels[level] += 1
                break

print(f"Log level distribution: {log_levels}")

for job_post in job_posts_logs:
    status_icon = "⭐" if job_post["status"] == "featured" else "✅" if job_post["status"] == "active" else "⏸"
    conversion_rate = (job_post["applications"] / job_post["views"] * 100) if job_post["views"] > 0 else 0
    
    print(f"\n{status_icon} JobPost #{job_post['job_id']}: {job_post['title']}")
    print(f"   Company: {job_post['company']} | Status: {job_post['status'].title()}")
    print(f"   Applications: {job_post['applications']}, Views: {job_post['views']} (Conversion: {conversion_rate:.1f}%)")
    
    print(f"   Recent Log Events:")
    for event in job_post["log_events"][-3:]:  # Show last 3 events
        timestamp = event.split()[0] + " " + event.split()[1]
        log_level = event.split('[')[1].split(']')[0]
        message = event.split('] ')[1]
        
        level_icon = "🔴" if log_level == "ERROR" else "🟡" if log_level == "WARNING" else "🟢"
        print(f"     {level_icon} {timestamp} [{log_level}] {message}")

# Error summary
error_count = log_levels["ERROR"]
warning_count = log_levels["WARNING"]
print(f"\n🚨 Issue Summary:")
print(f"   Errors detected: {error_count} events")
print(f"   Warnings detected: {warning_count} events")
print(f"   Most common issues: Skill attachment errors, slow queries, email validation")

print(f"\n📊 Performance Insights:")
avg_conversion = sum(job['applications'] / job['views'] if job['views'] > 0 else 0 for job in job_posts_logs) / len(job_posts_logs) * 100
print(f"   Average conversion rate: {avg_conversion:.1f}% (applications/views)")
print(f"   Peak activity time: 14:30-16:30 based on log timestamps")
print(f"   Database performance: 1 slow query detected (>2s)")
anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:30:15 [INFO] JobPost created successfully: Senior Python Django Developer anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:30:16 [INFO] Skills attached: Python Django, PostgreSQL, Docker anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:15:11 [ERROR] Failed to attach skill: Kubernetes (Skill not found) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:20:18 [WARNING] Slow query detected: JobPost skills lookup (2.3s) anihpj-db-1 | 2024-01-11 15:20:18 [SLOW] SELECT * FROM jobpost_skills WHERE skill_name LIKE '%kubernetes%' duration: 2300ms anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 16:10:22 [ERROR] Application submission failed: Invalid email format JobPost Application - Docker Compose Log Analysis: Total job posts being monitored: 3 Total applications: 6, Total views: 135 Log level distribution: {'INFO': 11, 'WARNING': 1, 'ERROR': 2} ⭐ JobPost #3: Frontend React Developer Company: StartupXYZ | Status: Featured Applications: 2, Views: 67 (Conversion: 3.0%) Recent Log Events: 🟢 2024-01-11 16:05:15 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: emma_frontend 🔴 2024-01-11 16:10:22 [ERROR] Application submission failed: Invalid email format 🟢 2024-01-11 16:15:30 [INFO] JobPost featured status activated ✅ JobPost #1: Senior Python Django Developer Company: TechCorp Inc | Status: Active Applications: 3, Views: 45 (Conversion: 6.7%) Recent Log Events: 🟢 2024-01-11 14:35:22 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: sarah_dev 🟢 2024-01-11 14:40:33 [INFO] Application submitted for JobPost #1 🟢 2024-01-11 14:40:34 [INFO] Email notification sent to TechCorp Inc ✅ JobPost #2: DevOps Engineer Company: CloudSoft Solutions | Status: Active Applications: 1, Views: 23 (Conversion: 4.3%) Recent Log Events: 🟡 2024-01-11 15:20:18 [WARNING] Slow query detected: JobPost skills lookup (2.3s) 🟢 2024-01-11 15:25:44 [INFO] JobPost viewed by user: alex_devops 🚨 Issue Summary: Errors detected: 2 events Warnings detected: 1 events Most common issues: Skill attachment errors, slow queries, email validation 📊 Performance Insights: Average conversion rate: 4.7% (applications/views) Peak activity time: 14:30-16:30 based on log timestamps Database performance: 1 slow query detected (>2s)
Example 2: Skills API Performance Monitoring (Real-time Log Streaming)

Monitor Skills API endpoint performance and database queries in real-time to optimize skill matching and search functionality.

# Follow Skills API logs in real-time
docker compose logs -f --tail=50 web | grep -i skills

# Monitor specific Skills service with timestamps
docker compose logs -f -t web --since="2024-01-11T15:00:00" | grep "skills_api"

# Check Skills database performance logs
docker compose logs db --tail=100 | grep -E "(SLOW|ERROR|skills)"

Django Skills Model Context & Log Analysis:

# Skills API monitoring and performance analysis
# Monitor Skills model operations and API endpoints

# Skills log analysis scenario
skills_performance_logs = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:45:12",
        "level": "INFO",
        "endpoint": "/api/skills/search/",
        "query": "python django",
        "response_time": "0.125s",
        "results_count": 23,
        "status": "success"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:45:45",
        "level": "WARNING", 
        "endpoint": "/api/skills/create/",
        "skill_name": "React Native",
        "duplicate_check_time": "1.2s",
        "status": "slow_response"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:46:10",
        "level": "ERROR",
        "endpoint": "/api/skills/bulk-import/",
        "error": "ValidationError: Skill 'Node.js++' contains invalid characters",
        "skills_rejected": 5,
        "skills_imported": 15
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:47:03",
        "level": "INFO",
        "endpoint": "/api/skills/trending/",
        "cache_hit": True,
        "response_time": "0.023s",
        "trending_skills": ["Python", "Docker", "Kubernetes", "Django", "React"]
    }
]

print("Skills API Performance - Docker Compose Log Analysis:")
print(f"Total API requests monitored: {len(skills_performance_logs)}")

# Response time analysis
response_times = []
for log_entry in skills_performance_logs:
    if "response_time" in log_entry:
        time_value = float(log_entry["response_time"].replace("s", ""))
        response_times.append(time_value)
        
avg_response_time = sum(response_times) / len(response_times) if response_times else 0
print(f"Average API response time: {avg_response_time:.3f}s")

# Log level analysis
log_levels = {"INFO": 0, "WARNING": 0, "ERROR": 0}
for log_entry in skills_performance_logs:
    log_levels[log_entry["level"]] += 1

print(f"Log distribution: {log_levels}")

print(f"\n🔍 Skills API Endpoint Analysis:")
for log_entry in skills_performance_logs:
    level_icon = "🔴" if log_entry["level"] == "ERROR" else "🟡" if log_entry["level"] == "WARNING" else "🟢"
    endpoint = log_entry["endpoint"]
    status = log_entry.get("status", "unknown")
    
    print(f"{level_icon} {log_entry['timestamp']} [{log_entry['level']}] {endpoint}")
    
    if "response_time" in log_entry:
        time_status = "🚀" if float(log_entry["response_time"].replace("s", "")) < 0.1 else "⚡" if float(log_entry["response_time"].replace("s", "")) < 0.5 else "🐌"
        print(f"     {time_status} Response Time: {log_entry['response_time']} | Status: {status}")
    
    if "results_count" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📊 Results Found: {log_entry['results_count']} skills")
    
    if "error" in log_entry:
        print(f"     🚫 Error: {log_entry['error']}")
        if "skills_imported" in log_entry:
            print(f"     ✅ Imported: {log_entry['skills_imported']}, ❌ Rejected: {log_entry['skills_rejected']}")
    
    if "trending_skills" in log_entry:
        trending = ", ".join(log_entry["trending_skills"][:3])
        cache_status = "💾 Cache Hit" if log_entry.get("cache_hit") else "🔄 Cache Miss"
        print(f"     {cache_status} | Top Skills: {trending}...")

# Performance insights
slow_requests = [log for log in skills_performance_logs if "response_time" in log and float(log["response_time"].replace("s", "")) > 0.5]
error_rate = log_levels["ERROR"] / len(skills_performance_logs) * 100

print(f"\n📈 Skills API Performance Metrics:")
print(f"   Slow requests (>0.5s): {len(slow_requests)} ({len(slow_requests)/len(skills_performance_logs)*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"   Error rate: {error_rate:.1f}%")
print(f"   Cache efficiency: High (trending endpoint cached)")
print(f"   Database optimization needed: Duplicate check queries")
anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:45:12 [INFO] GET /api/skills/search/?q=python+django 200 0.125s anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:45:45 [WARNING] POST /api/skills/create/ duplicate_check_duration=1.2s skill="React Native" anihpj-db-1 | 2024-01-11 15:45:45 [SLOW] SELECT COUNT(*) FROM skills WHERE name ILIKE 'react native' duration: 1200ms anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:46:10 [ERROR] POST /api/skills/bulk-import/ ValidationError: Invalid characters in skill name anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:47:03 [INFO] GET /api/skills/trending/ 200 0.023s cache_hit=True Skills API Performance - Docker Compose Log Analysis: Total API requests monitored: 4 Average API response time: 0.074s Log distribution: {'INFO': 2, 'WARNING': 1, 'ERROR': 1} 🔍 Skills API Endpoint Analysis: 🟢 2024-01-11 15:45:12 [INFO] /api/skills/search/ 🚀 Response Time: 0.125s | Status: success 📊 Results Found: 23 skills 🟡 2024-01-11 15:45:45 [WARNING] /api/skills/create/ 🐌 Response Time: 1.2s | Status: slow_response 🔴 2024-01-11 15:46:10 [ERROR] /api/skills/bulk-import/ 🚫 Error: ValidationError: Skill 'Node.js++' contains invalid characters ✅ Imported: 15, ❌ Rejected: 5 🟢 2024-01-11 15:47:03 [INFO] /api/skills/trending/ 🚀 Response Time: 0.023s | Status: success 💾 Cache Hit | Top Skills: Python, Docker, Kubernetes... 📈 Skills API Performance Metrics: Slow requests (>0.5s): 1 (25.0%) Error rate: 25.0% Cache efficiency: High (trending endpoint cached) Database optimization needed: Duplicate check queries
Example 3: Author Content Management Log Analysis (Error Investigation)

Investigate Author profile creation and content publishing errors with detailed log filtering and analysis for content moderation system.

# Filter Author-specific error logs
docker compose logs web --since="1h" | grep -E "(author|Author)" | grep -E "(ERROR|WARN)"

# Check Author content publishing pipeline
docker compose logs -f web | grep "author_content" --line-buffered

# Analyze Author database operations with time range
docker compose logs db --since="2024-01-11T14:00:00" --until="2024-01-11T16:00:00" | grep "author"

Django Author Model Context & Content Pipeline Logs:

# Author content management log analysis
# Track Author profile operations and content publishing workflow

# Author content pipeline logs
author_content_logs = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:15:30",
        "level": "INFO",
        "action": "profile_creation",
        "author_id": "author_001",
        "username": "sarah_writer",
        "email": "sarah@techwriter.com",
        "bio_length": 245,
        "status": "created"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:20:15",
        "level": "ERROR",
        "action": "portfolio_upload",
        "author_id": "author_001",
        "file_name": "portfolio_samples.pdf",
        "file_size": "15.5MB",
        "error": "File size exceeds 10MB limit",
        "status": "rejected"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:25:42",
        "level": "WARNING",
        "action": "content_moderation",
        "author_id": "author_002",
        "username": "alex_blogger",
        "flagged_content": "promotional_link",
        "confidence_score": 0.75,
        "action_taken": "manual_review_required"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:30:18",
        "level": "INFO",
        "action": "content_published",
        "author_id": "author_003", 
        "content_type": "blog_post",
        "title": "Django Best Practices for 2024",
        "word_count": 1250,
        "publication_status": "live"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:35:55",
        "level": "ERROR",
        "action": "author_verification",
        "author_id": "author_004",
        "verification_method": "email_domain",
        "domain": "suspicious-freelancer.tk",
        "error": "Domain flagged in security blacklist",
        "verification_status": "failed"
    }
]

print("Author Content Management - Docker Compose Log Analysis:")
print(f"Total author operations monitored: {len(author_content_logs)}")

# Action type analysis
action_types = {}
for log_entry in author_content_logs:
    action = log_entry["action"]
    action_types[action] = action_types.get(action, 0) + 1

print(f"Action distribution: {action_types}")

# Log level analysis
log_levels = {"INFO": 0, "WARNING": 0, "ERROR": 0}
for log_entry in author_content_logs:
    log_levels[log_entry["level"]] += 1

print(f"Log level distribution: {log_levels}")

print(f"\n📝 Author Content Pipeline Analysis:")
for log_entry in author_content_logs:
    level_icon = "🔴" if log_entry["level"] == "ERROR" else "🟡" if log_entry["level"] == "WARNING" else "🟢"
    action = log_entry["action"].replace("_", " ").title()
    author_id = log_entry["author_id"]
    
    print(f"{level_icon} {log_entry['timestamp']} [{log_entry['level']}] {action}")
    print(f"     👤 Author: {author_id}")
    
    if "username" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📛 Username: {log_entry['username']}")
    
    if "email" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📧 Email: {log_entry['email']}")
    
    if "error" in log_entry:
        print(f"     🚫 Error: {log_entry['error']}")
        print(f"     ❌ Status: {log_entry.get('status', 'failed')}")
    
    if "file_size" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📁 File: {log_entry['file_name']} ({log_entry['file_size']})")
    
    if "flagged_content" in log_entry:
        confidence = log_entry["confidence_score"]
        confidence_icon = "🔴" if confidence > 0.8 else "🟡" if confidence > 0.5 else "🟢"
        print(f"     {confidence_icon} Content Flag: {log_entry['flagged_content']} (confidence: {confidence})")
        print(f"     ⚠️ Action: {log_entry['action_taken']}")
    
    if "word_count" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📄 Content: {log_entry['title']} ({log_entry['word_count']} words)")
        print(f"     ✅ Status: {log_entry['publication_status']}")
    
    if "domain" in log_entry:
        print(f"     🌐 Domain: {log_entry['domain']}")
        print(f"     🛡️ Security: Domain blacklisted")

# Error analysis and recommendations
errors = [log for log in author_content_logs if log["level"] == "ERROR"]
warnings = [log for log in author_content_logs if log["level"] == "WARNING"]

print(f"\n🚨 Author System Issues Summary:")
print(f"   Critical errors: {len(errors)} ({len(errors)/len(author_content_logs)*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"   Warnings: {len(warnings)} ({len(warnings)/len(author_content_logs)*100:.1f}%)")

if errors:
    print(f"   Common error patterns:")
    for error_log in errors:
        print(f"     • {error_log['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()}: {error_log['error']}")

print(f"\n🔧 Recommended Actions:")
print(f"   • Increase file upload limit for author portfolios")
print(f"   • Enhance domain verification with reputation scoring")
print(f"   • Implement progressive content moderation alerts")
print(f"   • Add author verification status dashboard")
anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:15:30 [INFO] Author profile created: sarah_writer (bio: 245 chars) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:20:15 [ERROR] Author portfolio upload failed: portfolio_samples.pdf (15.5MB > 10MB limit) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:25:42 [WARNING] Content moderation flag: alex_blogger promotional_link detected (confidence: 0.75) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:30:18 [INFO] Content published: "Django Best Practices for 2024" by author_003 (1250 words) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 14:35:55 [ERROR] Author verification failed: suspicious-freelancer.tk domain blacklisted Author Content Management - Docker Compose Log Analysis: Total author operations monitored: 5 Action distribution: {'profile_creation': 1, 'portfolio_upload': 1, 'content_moderation': 1, 'content_published': 1, 'author_verification': 1} Log level distribution: {'INFO': 2, 'WARNING': 1, 'ERROR': 2} 📝 Author Content Pipeline Analysis: 🟢 2024-01-11 14:15:30 [INFO] Profile Creation 👤 Author: author_001 📛 Username: sarah_writer 📧 Email: sarah@techwriter.com 🔴 2024-01-11 14:20:15 [ERROR] Portfolio Upload 👤 Author: author_001 🚫 Error: File size exceeds 10MB limit ❌ Status: rejected 📁 File: portfolio_samples.pdf (15.5MB) 🟡 2024-01-11 14:25:42 [WARNING] Content Moderation 👤 Author: author_002 🟡 Content Flag: promotional_link (confidence: 0.75) ⚠️ Action: manual_review_required 🟢 2024-01-11 14:30:18 [INFO] Content Published 👤 Author: author_003 📄 Content: Django Best Practices for 2024 (1250 words) ✅ Status: live 🔴 2024-01-11 14:35:55 [ERROR] Author Verification 👤 Author: author_004 🚫 Error: Domain flagged in security blacklist ❌ Status: failed 🌐 Domain: suspicious-freelancer.tk 🛡️ Security: Domain blacklisted 🚨 Author System Issues Summary: Critical errors: 2 (40.0%) Warnings: 1 (20.0%) Common error patterns: • Portfolio Upload: File size exceeds 10MB limit • Author Verification: Domain flagged in security blacklist 🔧 Recommended Actions: • Increase file upload limit for author portfolios • Enhance domain verification with reputation scoring • Implement progressive content moderation alerts • Add author verification status dashboard
Example 4: Location Services Geospatial Log Monitoring (Performance Optimization)

Monitor Location-based services for geospatial queries, coordinate calculations, and address validation with performance optimization insights.

# Monitor Location service geospatial operations
docker compose logs -f web | grep -E "(location|geo|coordinates)" --line-buffered

# Check Location database spatial queries
docker compose logs db --tail=200 | grep -E "(ST_|PostGIS|spatial)"

# Filter Location service errors with context
docker compose logs web --since="30m" | grep -B2 -A2 -E "(location.*ERROR|coordinate.*ERROR)"

Django Location Model Context & Geospatial Operations:

# Location services geospatial log monitoring
# Track Location model operations, coordinate calculations, and spatial queries

# Geospatial operations log analysis
location_geo_logs = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:10:22",
        "level": "INFO",
        "operation": "address_geocoding",
        "location_id": "loc_001",
        "address": "123 Tech Street, San Francisco, CA",
        "coordinates": {"lat": 37.7749, "lng": -122.4194},
        "accuracy": "street_level",
        "response_time": "0.245s"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:12:15",
        "level": "WARNING",
        "operation": "proximity_search",
        "search_radius": "50km",
        "center_point": {"lat": 37.7749, "lng": -122.4194},
        "results_found": 2847,
        "query_time": "2.1s",
        "warning": "Large result set, consider narrowing search"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:15:33",
        "level": "ERROR",
        "operation": "reverse_geocoding",
        "coordinates": {"lat": 91.0000, "lng": -122.4194},
        "error": "Invalid latitude: 91.0000 (exceeds ±90 bounds)",
        "validation": "failed"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:18:45",
        "level": "INFO",
        "operation": "distance_calculation",
        "location_a": "San Francisco, CA",
        "location_b": "Los Angeles, CA", 
        "distance": "559.2 km",
        "calculation_method": "haversine",
        "processing_time": "0.012s"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:22:10",
        "level": "WARNING",
        "operation": "batch_geocoding",
        "batch_size": 500,
        "processed": 487,
        "failed": 13,
        "rate_limit_hit": True,
        "processing_time": "45.2s",
        "success_rate": "97.4%"
    }
]

print("Location Services Geospatial - Docker Compose Log Analysis:")
print(f"Total geospatial operations monitored: {len(location_geo_logs)}")

# Operation type analysis
operation_types = {}
for log_entry in location_geo_logs:
    operation = log_entry["operation"]
    operation_types[operation] = operation_types.get(operation, 0) + 1

print(f"Operation distribution: {operation_types}")

# Performance analysis
processing_times = []
for log_entry in location_geo_logs:
    if "response_time" in log_entry:
        time_value = float(log_entry["response_time"].replace("s", ""))
        processing_times.append(time_value)
    elif "processing_time" in log_entry:
        time_value = float(log_entry["processing_time"].replace("s", ""))
        processing_times.append(time_value)
    elif "query_time" in log_entry:
        time_value = float(log_entry["query_time"].replace("s", ""))
        processing_times.append(time_value)

avg_processing_time = sum(processing_times) / len(processing_times) if processing_times else 0
print(f"Average processing time: {avg_processing_time:.3f}s")

# Log level analysis  
log_levels = {"INFO": 0, "WARNING": 0, "ERROR": 0}
for log_entry in location_geo_logs:
    log_levels[log_entry["level"]] += 1

print(f"Log level distribution: {log_levels}")

print(f"\n🌍 Geospatial Operations Analysis:")
for log_entry in location_geo_logs:
    level_icon = "🔴" if log_entry["level"] == "ERROR" else "🟡" if log_entry["level"] == "WARNING" else "🟢"
    operation = log_entry["operation"].replace("_", " ").title()
    
    print(f"{level_icon} {log_entry['timestamp']} [{log_entry['level']}] {operation}")
    
    if "address" in log_entry:
        accuracy_icon = "🎯" if log_entry.get("accuracy") == "street_level" else "📍"
        print(f"     {accuracy_icon} Address: {log_entry['address']}")
        coords = log_entry.get("coordinates", {})
        print(f"     🗺️ Coordinates: ({coords.get('lat')}, {coords.get('lng')})")
        
    if "response_time" in log_entry:
        time_value = float(log_entry["response_time"].replace("s", ""))
        time_icon = "🚀" if time_value < 0.1 else "⚡" if time_value < 0.5 else "🐌"
        print(f"     {time_icon} Response Time: {log_entry['response_time']}")
    
    if "search_radius" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📏 Search Radius: {log_entry['search_radius']}")
        print(f"     📊 Results Found: {log_entry['results_found']:,}")
        if log_entry.get("warning"):
            print(f"     ⚠️ Warning: {log_entry['warning']}")
    
    if "error" in log_entry:
        print(f"     🚫 Error: {log_entry['error']}")
        print(f"     ❌ Validation: {log_entry.get('validation', 'failed')}")
    
    if "distance" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📍 Route: {log_entry['location_a']} → {log_entry['location_b']}")
        print(f"     📏 Distance: {log_entry['distance']}")
        print(f"     🧮 Method: {log_entry['calculation_method']}")
    
    if "batch_size" in log_entry:
        success_rate = float(log_entry["success_rate"].replace("%", ""))
        success_icon = "✅" if success_rate > 95 else "⚠️" if success_rate > 90 else "🔴"
        print(f"     📦 Batch Size: {log_entry['batch_size']}")
        print(f"     {success_icon} Success Rate: {log_entry['success_rate']} ({log_entry['processed']}/{log_entry['batch_size']})")
        if log_entry.get("rate_limit_hit"):
            print(f"     ⏱️ Rate Limit: Encountered during processing")

# Performance optimization insights
slow_operations = [log for log in location_geo_logs if "query_time" in log and float(log["query_time"].replace("s", "")) > 1.0]
error_operations = [log for log in location_geo_logs if log["level"] == "ERROR"]

print(f"\n📊 Geospatial Performance Analysis:")
print(f"   Slow operations (>1s): {len(slow_operations)} ({len(slow_operations)/len(location_geo_logs)*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"   Error rate: {len(error_operations)/len(location_geo_logs)*100:.1f}%")

print(f"\n🔧 Performance Optimization Recommendations:")
print(f"   • Add spatial indexing for proximity searches")
print(f"   • Implement coordinate validation before processing")
print(f"   • Add rate limiting for batch geocoding operations")
print(f"   • Cache frequently accessed geocoding results")
print(f"   • Consider result pagination for large proximity searches")
anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:10:22 [INFO] Geocoding success: "123 Tech Street, San Francisco, CA" -> (37.7749, -122.4194) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:12:15 [WARNING] Proximity search: 2847 results in 50km radius (query_time: 2.1s) anihpj-db-1 | 2024-01-11 15:12:15 [SLOW] SELECT * FROM location WHERE ST_DWithin(coordinates, ST_Point(-122.4194, 37.7749), 50000) duration: 2100ms anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:15:33 [ERROR] Invalid coordinates: latitude 91.0000 exceeds bounds (±90) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:18:45 [INFO] Distance calculated: SF->LA 559.2km using haversine (0.012s) anihpj-web-1 | 2024-01-11 15:22:10 [WARNING] Batch geocoding: 487/500 success (rate limit encountered) Location Services Geospatial - Docker Compose Log Analysis: Total geospatial operations monitored: 5 Operation distribution: {'address_geocoding': 1, 'proximity_search': 1, 'reverse_geocoding': 1, 'distance_calculation': 1, 'batch_geocoding': 1} Average processing time: 9.524s Log level distribution: {'INFO': 2, 'WARNING': 2, 'ERROR': 1} 🌍 Geospatial Operations Analysis: 🟢 2024-01-11 15:10:22 [INFO] Address Geocoding 🎯 Address: 123 Tech Street, San Francisco, CA 🗺️ Coordinates: (37.7749, -122.4194) ⚡ Response Time: 0.245s 🟡 2024-01-11 15:12:15 [WARNING] Proximity Search 📏 Search Radius: 50km 📊 Results Found: 2,847 ⚠️ Warning: Large result set, consider narrowing search 🔴 2024-01-11 15:15:33 [ERROR] Reverse Geocoding 🚫 Error: Invalid latitude: 91.0000 (exceeds ±90 bounds) ❌ Validation: failed 🟢 2024-01-11 15:18:45 [INFO] Distance Calculation 📍 Route: San Francisco, CA → Los Angeles, CA 📏 Distance: 559.2 km 🧮 Method: haversine 🟡 2024-01-11 15:22:10 [WARNING] Batch Geocoding 📦 Batch Size: 500 ✅ Success Rate: 97.4% (487/500) ⏱️ Rate Limit: Encountered during processing 📊 Geospatial Performance Analysis: Slow operations (>1s): 1 (20.0%) Error rate: 20.0% 🔧 Performance Optimization Recommendations: • Add spatial indexing for proximity searches • Implement coordinate validation before processing • Add rate limiting for batch geocoding operations • Cache frequently accessed geocoding results • Consider result pagination for large proximity searches
Example 5: User Authentication & Session Management Log Tracking (Security Analysis)

Track User authentication events, session management, and security incidents with comprehensive security analysis and fraud detection.

# Monitor User authentication and security events
docker compose logs -f web | grep -E "(auth|login|logout|session)" --line-buffered

# Track security incidents and suspicious activities
docker compose logs web --since="1h" | grep -E "(SECURITY|WARN|ERROR)" | grep -E "(user|User)"

# Analyze User session patterns and database activity
docker compose logs -t web db --since="2024-01-11T14:00:00" | grep -E "(session_key|user_id)" | head -50

Django User Authentication Context & Security Monitoring:

# User authentication and security log monitoring
# Track User model authentication, session management, and security events

# User authentication security logs
user_security_logs = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:45:12",
        "level": "INFO",
        "event": "login_success", 
        "user_id": "user_001",
        "username": "john_developer",
        "ip_address": "192.168.1.100",
        "user_agent": "Mozilla/5.0 Chrome/120.0",
        "session_duration": "2h 15m",
        "location": "San Francisco, CA"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 14:50:33",
        "level": "WARNING",
        "event": "failed_login_attempt",
        "attempted_username": "admin",
        "ip_address": "45.142.182.45",
        "failure_reason": "invalid_password",
        "attempt_count": 5,
        "threat_level": "medium"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:05:18",
        "level": "ERROR",
        "event": "session_hijack_detected",
        "user_id": "user_002",
        "username": "sarah_manager",
        "original_ip": "192.168.1.105",
        "suspicious_ip": "78.91.123.77",
        "session_invalidated": True,
        "security_action": "force_logout"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:12:45",
        "level": "INFO",
        "event": "password_changed",
        "user_id": "user_003",
        "username": "alex_designer",
        "change_method": "security_prompt",
        "previous_password_age": "45 days",
        "strength_score": 95
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:18:22",
        "level": "WARNING",
        "event": "suspicious_activity",
        "user_id": "user_004",
        "username": "emma_analyst",
        "activity": "rapid_api_calls",
        "requests_per_minute": 120,
        "normal_average": 15,
        "action_taken": "rate_limit_applied"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2024-01-11 15:25:10",
        "level": "INFO",
        "event": "logout_success",
        "user_id": "user_001", 
        "username": "john_developer",
        "session_duration": "40m 12s",
        "logout_method": "manual",
        "activity_summary": "viewed 15 job posts, applied to 2 positions"
    }
]

print("User Authentication & Security - Docker Compose Log Analysis:")
print(f"Total security events monitored: {len(user_security_logs)}")

# Event type analysis
event_types = {}
for log_entry in user_security_logs:
    event = log_entry["event"]
    event_types[event] = event_types.get(event, 0) + 1

print(f"Event distribution: {event_types}")

# Security level analysis
security_levels = {"INFO": 0, "WARNING": 0, "ERROR": 0}
for log_entry in user_security_logs:
    security_levels[log_entry["level"]] += 1

print(f"Security alert distribution: {security_levels}")

# Threat analysis
threat_indicators = {
    "failed_logins": len([log for log in user_security_logs if log["event"] == "failed_login_attempt"]),
    "session_attacks": len([log for log in user_security_logs if "hijack" in log.get("event", "")]),
    "suspicious_activities": len([log for log in user_security_logs if log["event"] == "suspicious_activity"]),
}

print(f"Threat indicators: {threat_indicators}")

print(f"\n🔐 User Security Events Analysis:")
for log_entry in user_security_logs:
    level_icon = "🔴" if log_entry["level"] == "ERROR" else "🟡" if log_entry["level"] == "WARNING" else "🟢"
    event = log_entry["event"].replace("_", " ").title()
    
    print(f"{level_icon} {log_entry['timestamp']} [{log_entry['level']}] {event}")
    
    if "username" in log_entry:
        print(f"     👤 User: {log_entry['username']} (ID: {log_entry.get('user_id', 'N/A')})")
    elif "attempted_username" in log_entry:
        print(f"     👤 Attempted User: {log_entry['attempted_username']}")
    
    if "ip_address" in log_entry:
        ip = log_entry["ip_address"]
        location_info = log_entry.get("location", "Unknown")
        
        # Classify IP as internal/external
        if ip.startswith("192.168.") or ip.startswith("10.") or ip.startswith("172."):
            ip_icon = "🏠"
            ip_type = "Internal"
        else:
            ip_icon = "🌐"
            ip_type = "External"
        
        print(f"     {ip_icon} IP: {ip} ({ip_type}) | Location: {location_info}")
    
    if "session_duration" in log_entry:
        duration = log_entry["session_duration"]
        duration_icon = "⏰" if "h" in duration else "⏱️"
        print(f"     {duration_icon} Session: {duration}")
    
    if "attempt_count" in log_entry:
        attempts = log_entry["attempt_count"]
        threat = log_entry.get("threat_level", "low")
        threat_icon = "🚨" if threat == "high" else "⚠️" if threat == "medium" else "🔸"
        print(f"     {threat_icon} Failed Attempts: {attempts} | Threat Level: {threat.title()}")
        print(f"     🚫 Reason: {log_entry['failure_reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    
    if "suspicious_ip" in log_entry:
        print(f"     🚨 Security Alert: Session IP changed")
        print(f"     🏠 Original IP: {log_entry['original_ip']}")
        print(f"     🌐 Suspicious IP: {log_entry['suspicious_ip']}")
        if log_entry.get("session_invalidated"):
            print(f"     🔒 Action: Session invalidated, user force-logged out")
    
    if "strength_score" in log_entry:
        score = log_entry["strength_score"]
        strength_icon = "💪" if score >= 90 else "🔒" if score >= 70 else "⚠️"
        print(f"     🔑 Password Change: {log_entry['change_method'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
        print(f"     {strength_icon} New Password Strength: {score}/100")
        print(f"     📅 Previous Password Age: {log_entry['previous_password_age']}")
    
    if "requests_per_minute" in log_entry:
        rpm = log_entry["requests_per_minute"]
        normal = log_entry["normal_average"]
        ratio = rpm / normal
        activity_icon = "🚨" if ratio > 10 else "⚠️" if ratio > 5 else "🔸"
        print(f"     {activity_icon} API Activity: {rpm} req/min (normal: {normal})")
        print(f"     📊 Activity Ratio: {ratio:.1f}x above normal")
        print(f"     🛡️ Action: {log_entry['action_taken'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    
    if "activity_summary" in log_entry:
        print(f"     📋 Session Summary: {log_entry['activity_summary']}")
        print(f"     🚪 Logout Method: {log_entry['logout_method'].title()}")

# Security insights and recommendations
failed_logins = [log for log in user_security_logs if log["event"] == "failed_login_attempt"]
successful_logins = [log for log in user_security_logs if log["event"] == "login_success"]
security_incidents = [log for log in user_security_logs if log["level"] == "ERROR"]

print(f"\n🛡️ Security Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   Successful logins: {len(successful_logins)}")
print(f"   Failed login attempts: {len(failed_logins)}")
print(f"   Security incidents: {len(security_incidents)}")

if failed_logins:
    login_success_rate = len(successful_logins) / (len(successful_logins) + len(failed_logins)) * 100
    print(f"   Login success rate: {login_success_rate:.1f}%")

print(f"\n🚨 Security Recommendations:")
print(f"   • Implement account lockout after 3 failed attempts")
print(f"   • Add geolocation validation for suspicious logins")
print(f"   • Enable two-factor authentication for all users")
print(f"   • Monitor API rate limiting and implement progressive delays")
print(f"   • Set up automated alerts for session hijacking attempts")
print(f"   • Require password changes every 60 days for security")
