print(f"   🚀 Clean workspace ready for development")
Removing anihpj_web_1 ... done Removing anihpj_web_2 ... done Removing anihpj_worker_1 ... done ... (15 containers removed) Django Development Workspace - Reset Analysis: ================================================== 🎯 Workspace Reset Summary: ✅ 15 stopped containers removed 💾 4.2GB storage space recovered 📊 All data volumes preserved 🔧 Running services protected 🚀 Clean workspace ready for development
Example 5: Selective Service Cleanup (Targeted Container Removal)

Remove specific stopped Django services for targeted cleanup while maintaining Skills processing containers, JobPost search services, and User authentication containers for selective environment management.

# Remove specific stopped services only
docker compose rm -f web worker

# Keep database and cache containers for data continuity  
docker compose ps | grep -E "(db|redis|elasticsearch)"

# Verify selective cleanup results
docker compose rm --dry-run

Django Models Context & Selective Cleanup:

# Selective service cleanup analysis
# Targeted removal with strategic service preservation

selective_cleanup = {
    "removed_services": ["web", "worker"],
    "preserved_services": ["db", "redis", "elasticsearch"], 
    "cleanup_strategy": "selective_application_layer",
    "data_impact": "zero_data_loss",
    "service_continuity": "data_layer_maintained"
}

print("Django Selective Service - Cleanup Analysis:")
print("=" * 48)
print(f"🎯 Selective Cleanup Summary:")
print(f"   🗑️ Application containers removed: {selective_cleanup['removed_services']}")
print(f"   🔒 Data services preserved: {selective_cleanup['preserved_services']}")
print(f"   📊 Zero data loss achieved")
print(f"   🔧 Data layer continuity maintained")
print(f"   🚀 Selective cleanup completed successfully")
Removing anihpj_web_1 ... done Removing anihpj_worker_1 ... done Django Selective Service - Cleanup Analysis: ================================================ 🎯 Selective Cleanup Summary: 🗑️ Application containers removed: ['web', 'worker'] 🔒 Data services preserved: ['db', 'redis', 'elasticsearch'] 📊 Zero data loss achieved 🔧 Data layer continuity maintained 🚀 Selective cleanup completed successfully

docker compose config

Official Docs: docker compose config | Docker CLI: docker compose config

Parse and validate Django Compose file configuration displaying resolved service definitions.

Full Description

The docker compose config command parses your anihpj Django compose file and outputs the resolved configuration, showing how services, networks, volumes, and environment variables are interpreted. This validates syntax, resolves variable substitutions, and displays the final configuration that Docker Compose will use.

For Django applications, this command is essential for debugging configuration issues, validating environment-specific settings, confirming service dependencies, and understanding how compose files merge together. It helps ensure your anihpj jobpost application configuration is correct before deployment.

Syntax

# Show complete resolved configuration
docker compose config

# Show only service names
docker compose config --services

# Show configuration for specific service
docker compose config web

# Show in JSON format
docker compose config --format json

# Validate without output
docker compose config --quiet

# Show volumes configuration
docker compose config --volumes

# Show configuration with secrets
docker compose config --no-interpolate

# Show profiles information
docker compose config --profiles

Parameters Description

  • --services: List only Django service names defined in compose file. Quick overview of available services.
  • --volumes: List only volume names for Django data persistence. Verify volume configurations and names.
  • --format json: Output Django configuration in JSON format. Machine-readable format for automated processing.
  • --quiet: Validate Django compose file without output. Silent syntax and configuration validation.
  • --no-interpolate: Show raw Django configuration without variable substitution. Debug environment variable issues.
  • Service Name: Show configuration for specific Django service. Focus on individual service settings.

Attributes

  • Configuration Validation: Validates Django compose file syntax and structure, ensuring proper YAML format.
  • Variable Resolution: Resolves environment variables and substitutions in Django configuration for final values.
  • Service Dependencies: Shows resolved Django service dependencies and network connections between components.
  • Environment Merging: Displays merged configuration from multiple Django compose files and overrides.
  • Output Formatting: Supports multiple output formats for Django configuration analysis and automation.

Methods

Configuration Validation

Syntax: docker compose config --quiet

Parameters: --quiet for Django compose file syntax validation without output

Validate Django compose file syntax and configuration correctness before deployment or testing.

Service Configuration Analysis

Syntax: docker compose config web

Parameters: service name for focused Django service configuration review

Examine specific Django service configuration including ports, volumes, environment variables, and dependencies.

Return Value

Returns resolved Django configuration in YAML or JSON format showing final service definitions and settings.

  • Success (0): Valid configuration displayed or validated successfully
  • Validation Error (1): Invalid syntax or configuration errors found
  • File Error (2): Compose file not found or not readable

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Configuration Validation (Environment-Specific Settings)

Validate production Django configuration for anihpj deployment including User authentication settings, JobPost processing queues, Skills verification services, and Location-based search with proper environment variable resolution.

# Validate complete production configuration
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml config --quiet

# Show resolved production configuration
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml config

# Verify environment-specific settings
docker compose config --format json | jq '.services.web.environment'

Django Models Context & Configuration Validation:

# Production configuration validation for Django anihpj application
# Validating User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model configurations

import yaml
import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Production configuration analysis for anihpj Django application
production_config_analysis = {
    "compose_files": [
        "docker-compose.yml",  # Base configuration
        "docker-compose.prod.yml"  # Production overrides
    ],
    "environment": "production",
    "validation_target": "anihpj_production_deployment"
}

# Expected Django services configuration
expected_services = {
    "web": {
        "image": "anihpj:production",
        "port_mapping": "80:8000",
        "environment_vars": [
            "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production",
            "DATABASE_URL",
            "REDIS_URL", 
            "SECRET_KEY",
            "ALLOWED_HOSTS"
        ],
        "volumes": [
            "./static:/app/static",
            "./media:/app/media"
        ],
        "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "critical_paths": [
            "/api/users/auth/",
            "/api/jobs/",
            "/api/skills/verify/",
            "/api/locations/search/"
        ]
    },
    "db": {
        "image": "postgres:15",
        "port_mapping": "internal:5432",
        "environment_vars": [
            "POSTGRES_DB=anihpj",
            "POSTGRES_USER", 
            "POSTGRES_PASSWORD"
        ],
        "volumes": [
            "db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"
        ],
        "models_stored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "performance_targets": {
            "User_table": "1M+ records",
            "JobPost_table": "500K+ records", 
            "Skills_table": "50K+ records"
        }
    },
    "redis": {
        "image": "redis:7-alpine",
        "port_mapping": "internal:6379",
        "purpose": "session_cache_and_job_queue",
        "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
        "queue_operations": [
            "job_notifications",
            "skill_verification",
            "location_indexing"
        ]
    },
    "worker": {
        "image": "anihpj:production", 
        "command": "celery worker",
        "environment_vars": [
            "CELERY_BROKER_URL",
            "CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND"
        ],
        "task_types": [
            "email_notifications",
            "job_post_processing",
            "skills_verification",
            "location_geocoding"
        ]
    },
    "nginx": {
        "image": "nginx:alpine",
        "port_mapping": "443:443",
        "ssl_enabled": True,
        "static_files": True,
        "django_proxy": "web:8000"
    }
}

# Environment variable validation for Django models
environment_validation = {
    "database_config": {
        "DATABASE_URL": {
            "required": True,
            "format": "postgresql://user:pass@host:port/anihpj",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "validation": "connection_string_format"
        },
        "DB_CONNECTION_POOL": {
            "required": True,
            "value": "20",
            "purpose": "handle_concurrent_user_jobpost_operations"
        }
    },
    "cache_config": {
        "REDIS_URL": {
            "required": True,
            "format": "redis://host:port/db",
            "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
            "cache_operations": [
                "user_session_storage",
                "skill_verification_cache",
                "jobpost_search_results"
            ]
        },
        "CACHE_TTL_USER": {
            "required": True,
            "value": "3600",
            "purpose": "User_model_cache_duration"
        },
        "CACHE_TTL_SKILLS": {
            "required": True, 
            "value": "7200",
            "purpose": "Skills_verification_cache"
        }
    },
    "django_config": {
        "SECRET_KEY": {
            "required": True,
            "length": 50,
            "security": "cryptographic_operations",
            "models_protected": ["User", "Author"]
        },
        "ALLOWED_HOSTS": {
            "required": True,
            "value": ["anihpj.com", "www.anihpj.com"],
            "purpose": "production_domain_security"
        },
        "DEBUG": {
            "required": True,
            "value": "False",
            "security": "production_safety"
        }
    },
    "model_specific_config": {
        "USER_AUTH_BACKEND": {
            "required": True,
            "value": "django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend",
            "model": "User",
            "purpose": "user_authentication_system"
        },
        "JOBPOST_PAGINATION": {
            "required": True,
            "value": "25",
            "model": "JobPost", 
            "purpose": "api_response_optimization"
        },
        "SKILLS_VERIFICATION_API": {
            "required": True,
            "model": "Skills",
            "purpose": "external_verification_service"
        },
        "LOCATION_GEOCODING_KEY": {
            "required": True,
            "model": "Location",
            "purpose": "address_to_coordinates_conversion"
        }
    }
}

# Configuration validation results simulation
config_validation_results = {
    "syntax_validation": {
        "yaml_syntax": "valid",
        "service_definitions": "valid",
        "volume_declarations": "valid",
        "network_configurations": "valid",
        "environment_variables": "resolved"
    },
    "service_validation": {
        "web_service": {
            "image_available": True,
            "port_conflicts": False,
            "environment_complete": True,
            "volume_mounts_valid": True,
            "django_models_accessible": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        "db_service": {
            "image_available": True,
            "data_persistence": True,
            "connection_string_valid": True,
            "performance_tuned": True,
            "models_schema_ready": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        "redis_service": {
            "image_available": True,
            "memory_allocation": "512MB",
            "persistence_enabled": True,
            "cache_optimization": "enabled"
        }
    },
    "dependency_validation": {
        "web_depends_on_db": "verified",
        "web_depends_on_redis": "verified", 
        "worker_depends_on_redis": "verified",
        "nginx_depends_on_web": "verified",
        "startup_order": "correct"
    },
    "volume_validation": {
        "db_data_volume": {
            "type": "named",
            "persistence": "guaranteed",
            "backup_ready": True,
            "models_data": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        "static_files_volume": {
            "type": "bind_mount",
            "path": "./static",
            "django_static_files": True
        },
        "media_files_volume": {
            "type": "bind_mount", 
            "path": "./media",
            "user_uploads": True,
            "models_files": ["User", "Author", "Skills"]
        }
    }
}

# Security validation for production Django deployment
security_validation = {
    "environment_security": {
        "secret_key_exposure": "protected",
        "database_credentials": "env_file",
        "api_keys": "secrets_management",
        "debug_mode": "disabled"
    },
    "network_security": {
        "ssl_termination": "nginx",
        "internal_communication": "docker_network",
        "port_exposure": "minimal_required",
        "firewall_rules": "configured"
    },
    "data_security": {
        "volume_encryption": "enabled",
        "backup_encryption": "enabled", 
        "model_data_protection": {
            "User": "personal_data_encrypted",
            "Author": "profile_data_protected",
            "JobPost": "business_data_secured"
        }
    }
}

# Model-specific configuration verification
model_config_verification = {
    "User_model": {
        "authentication_backend": "configured",
        "session_management": "redis_backed",
        "password_hashing": "PBKDF2",
        "profile_images": "media_volume_mapped",
        "api_endpoints": ["auth", "profile", "sessions"],
        "configuration_status": "production_ready"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "search_indexing": "elasticsearch_ready",
        "application_queue": "celery_configured",
        "file_attachments": "media_volume_mapped",
        "api_endpoints": ["create", "search", "apply", "manage"],
        "configuration_status": "production_ready"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "verification_service": "external_api_configured",
        "demand_analysis": "data_pipeline_ready",
        "cache_strategy": "redis_ttl_configured",
        "api_endpoints": ["verify", "search", "trends"],
        "configuration_status": "production_ready"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "portfolio_storage": "media_volume_configured",
        "rating_system": "database_optimized",
        "availability_tracking": "real_time_updates",
        "api_endpoints": ["profile", "portfolio", "availability"],
        "configuration_status": "production_ready"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "geocoding_service": "api_key_configured",
        "search_optimization": "geographic_indexing",
        "timezone_handling": "automated",
        "api_endpoints": ["search", "coordinates", "markets"],
        "configuration_status": "production_ready"
    }
}

# Print production configuration validation
print("Django Production Configuration Validation - anihpj Deployment:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Environment: {production_config_analysis['environment'].title()}")
print(f"Configuration files: {', '.join(production_config_analysis['compose_files'])}")

print(f"\n🔍 Syntax Validation Results:")
for check, status in config_validation_results['syntax_validation'].items():
    check_name = check.replace('_', ' ').title()
    status_icon = "✅" if status == "valid" or status == "resolved" else "❌"
    print(f"   {status_icon} {check_name}: {status.title()}")

print(f"\n🏗️ Service Configuration Validation:")
for service, details in config_validation_results['service_validation'].items():
    service_name = service.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name}:")
    
    for check, result in details.items():
        if check == 'django_models_accessible' or check == 'models_schema_ready':
            check_name = check.replace('_', ' ').title()
            models = ', '.join(result) if isinstance(result, list) else str(result)
            print(f"      ✅ {check_name}: {models}")
        else:
            check_name = check.replace('_', ' ').title()
            status_icon = "✅" if result is True or result == "enabled" else "❌" if result is False else "ℹ️"
            print(f"      {status_icon} {check_name}: {result}")

print(f"\n🔗 Service Dependencies:")
for dependency, status in config_validation_results['dependency_validation'].items():
    dep_name = dependency.replace('_', ' ').title()
    status_icon = "✅" if status == "verified" or status == "correct" else "❌"
    print(f"   {status_icon} {dep_name}: {status.title()}")

print(f"\n💾 Volume Configuration:")
for volume, details in config_validation_results['volume_validation'].items():
    volume_name = volume.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   📂 {volume_name}:")
    
    for attr, value in details.items():
        if attr == 'models_data' or attr == 'models_files':
            attr_name = attr.replace('_', ' ').title()
            models = ', '.join(value) if isinstance(value, list) else str(value)
            print(f"      📊 {attr_name}: {models}")
        else:
            attr_name = attr.replace('_', ' ').title()
            status_icon = "✅" if value is True or value == "guaranteed" else "ℹ️"
            print(f"      {status_icon} {attr_name}: {value}")

print(f"\n🔒 Security Validation:")
for category, checks in security_validation.items():
    category_name = category.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   🛡️ {category_name}:")
    
    for check, status in checks.items():
        check_name = check.replace('_', ' ').title()
        if isinstance(status, dict):
            print(f"      📋 {check_name}:")
            for model, protection in status.items():
                print(f"        - {model}: {protection.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
        else:
            status_icon = "✅" if "enabled" in status or "configured" in status or "protected" in status else "ℹ️"
            print(f"      {status_icon} {check_name}: {status.replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Configuration Status:")
for model, config in model_config_verification.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    status = config['configuration_status']
    status_icon = "✅" if "ready" in status else "🔧"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Status: {status.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    
    endpoints = config.get('api_endpoints', [])
    if endpoints:
        print(f"      API Endpoints: {', '.join(endpoints)}")
    
    # Show specific configuration details
    for key, value in config.items():
        if key not in ['api_endpoints', 'configuration_status']:
            key_name = key.replace('_', ' ').title()
            value_display = value.replace('_', ' ').title() if isinstance(value, str) else str(value)
            print(f"      {key_name}: {value_display}")

print(f"\n🎯 Production Deployment Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ Syntax validation: All checks passed")
print(f"   ✅ Service configuration: 5 services validated")
print(f"   ✅ Environment variables: All required vars resolved")
print(f"   ✅ Volume mounts: Data persistence configured")
print(f"   ✅ Security settings: Production-hardened")
print(f"   ✅ Django models: All 5 models production-ready")
print(f"   ✅ API endpoints: All model endpoints configured")

print(f"\n🚀 Ready for Production Deployment:")
print(f"   - Docker Compose configuration validated")
print(f"   - All Django models properly configured") 
print(f"   - Database connections and volumes verified")
print(f"   - Security settings production-hardened")
print(f"   - Service dependencies correctly defined")
version: '3.8' services: web: build: . image: anihpj:production ports: - "80:8000" environment: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.production DATABASE_URL: postgresql://anihpj_user:secure_pass@db:5432/anihpj REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0 SECRET_KEY: your-production-secret-key-50-chars-long ALLOWED_HOSTS: anihpj.com,www.anihpj.com DEBUG: "False" volumes: - ./static:/app/static - ./media:/app/media depends_on: - db - redis db: image: postgres:15 environment: POSTGRES_DB: anihpj POSTGRES_USER: anihpj_user POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secure_database_password volumes: - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data redis: image: redis:7-alpine worker: image: anihpj:production command: celery worker -A anihpj --loglevel=info environment: CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0 CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/0 depends_on: - redis nginx: image: nginx:alpine ports: - "443:443" volumes: - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf - ./ssl:/etc/ssl/certs depends_on: - web volumes: db_data: Django Production Configuration Validation - anihpj Deployment: ================================================================= Environment: Production Configuration files: docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.prod.yml 🔍 Syntax Validation Results: ✅ Yaml Syntax: Valid ✅ Service Definitions: Valid ✅ Volume Declarations: Valid ✅ Network Configurations: Valid ✅ Environment Variables: Resolved 🏗️ Service Configuration Validation: 📋 Web Service: ✅ Image Available: True ✅ Port Conflicts: False ✅ Environment Complete: True ✅ Volume Mounts Valid: True ✅ Django Models Accessible: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📋 Db Service: ✅ Image Available: True ✅ Data Persistence: True ✅ Connection String Valid: True ✅ Performance Tuned: True ✅ Models Schema Ready: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 🔗 Service Dependencies: ✅ Web Depends On Db: Verified ✅ Web Depends On Redis: Verified ✅ Worker Depends On Redis: Verified ✅ Nginx Depends On Web: Verified ✅ Startup Order: Correct 🔒 Security Validation: 🛡️ Environment Security: ✅ Secret Key Exposure: Protected ✅ Database Credentials: Env File ✅ Api Keys: Secrets Management ✅ Debug Mode: Disabled 📊 Django Model Configuration Status: ✅ User Model: Status: Production Ready API Endpoints: auth, profile, sessions Authentication Backend: Configured Session Management: Redis Backed Password Hashing: PBKDF2 ✅ Jobpost Model: Status: Production Ready API Endpoints: create, search, apply, manage Search Indexing: Elasticsearch Ready Application Queue: Celery Configured 🎯 Production Deployment Summary: ✅ Syntax validation: All checks passed ✅ Service configuration: 5 services validated ✅ Environment variables: All required vars resolved ✅ Volume mounts: Data persistence configured ✅ Security settings: Production-hardened ✅ Django models: All 5 models production-ready
Example 2: Development Environment Debugging (Configuration Troubleshooting)

Debug Django development configuration issues for Skills integration testing, JobPost search optimization, and User session handling with detailed environment variable and service dependency analysis.

# Debug development configuration with all files
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml config --quiet

# Show service dependencies for debugging
docker compose config --services && docker compose config --volumes

# Export configuration for analysis
docker compose config --format json > config-debug.json

Django Models Context & Configuration Debugging:

# Development configuration debugging for Django anihpj application
# Troubleshooting Skills, JobPost, User model integration issues

debug_analysis = {
    "environment": "development",
    "debug_scope": ["service_dependencies", "environment_variables", "volume_mounts"],
    "models_affected": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User"],
    "configuration_issues": "integration_testing_setup"
}

print("Django Development Configuration Debug - anihpj Analysis:")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"🔍 Configuration Debugging Summary:")
print(f"   🐛 Environment: {debug_analysis['environment']}")
print(f"   📊 Models affected: {', '.join(debug_analysis['models_affected'])}")
print(f"   🔧 Debug scope: {', '.join(debug_analysis['debug_scope'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Configuration validated for development")
Docker Compose configuration is valid. Django Development Configuration Debug - anihpj Analysis: ============================================================ 🔍 Configuration Debugging Summary: 🐛 Environment: development 📊 Models affected: Skills, JobPost, User 🔧 Debug scope: service_dependencies, environment_variables, volume_mounts ✅ Configuration validated for development
Example 3: Multi-Environment Comparison (Configuration Differences)

Compare Django configuration differences between development, staging, and production environments for Skills processing, JobPost indexing, and User authentication settings.

# Generate configurations for comparison
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml config > base-config.yml
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml config > prod-config.yml

# Compare environment differences
diff base-config.yml prod-config.yml

Django Models Context & Environment Comparison:

# Multi-environment configuration comparison
# Analyzing Skills, JobPost, User settings across environments

environment_comparison = {
    "base_environment": "development_defaults",
    "production_overrides": ["database_settings", "cache_config", "security_settings"],
    "model_configurations": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User"],
    "differences_identified": "production_optimizations"
}

print("Django Multi-Environment Configuration Comparison:")
print("=" * 50)
print(f"🎯 Environment Comparison Summary:")
print(f"   📊 Base: {environment_comparison['base_environment']}")
print(f"   🔧 Production overrides: {len(environment_comparison['production_overrides'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Configuration differences analyzed")
Django Multi-Environment Configuration Comparison: ================================================== 🎯 Environment Comparison Summary: 📊 Base: development_defaults 🔧 Production overrides: 3 ✅ Configuration differences analyzed
Example 4: Service Configuration Analysis (Individual Component Review)

Analyze specific Django service configurations focusing on Skills verification service settings, JobPost search indexing configuration, and User authentication service parameters.

# Analyze specific service configurations
docker compose config web
docker compose config worker  
docker compose config db

# Export service-specific configuration
docker compose config --format json | jq '.services.web'

Django Models Context & Service Analysis:

# Individual service configuration analysis
# Detailed review of Skills, JobPost, User model service settings

service_analysis = {
    "web_service": {
        "django_models": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"],
        "api_endpoints": ["auth", "jobs", "skills", "profiles"],
        "configuration_status": "analyzed"
    },
    "worker_service": {
        "background_tasks": ["skill_verification", "job_processing", "user_notifications"],
        "queue_management": "celery_configured",
        "configuration_status": "analyzed"
    }
}

print("Django Service Configuration Analysis:")
print("=" * 40)
print(f"🎯 Service Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   🔧 Web service: {len(service_analysis['web_service']['django_models'])} models configured")
print(f"   ⚙️ Worker service: {len(service_analysis['worker_service']['background_tasks'])} task types")
print(f"   ✅ Individual service configurations validated")
Django Service Configuration Analysis: ======================================== 🎯 Service Analysis Summary: 🔧 Web service: 5 models configured ⚙️ Worker service: 3 task types ✅ Individual service configurations validated
Example 5: CI/CD Pipeline Integration (Automated Configuration Validation)

Integrate Django configuration validation into CI/CD pipeline for automated Skills processing setup, JobPost deployment verification, and User authentication configuration testing.

# CI/CD configuration validation pipeline
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.ci.yml config --quiet || exit 1

# Generate deployment-ready configuration
docker compose config --format json > deployment-config.json

# Validate configuration against schema
schema-validator deployment-config.json django-compose-schema.json

Django Models Context & CI/CD Integration:

# CI/CD pipeline configuration validation
# Automated validation for Skills, JobPost, User deployment configurations

cicd_validation = {
    "pipeline_stage": "configuration_validation",
    "validation_scope": ["syntax", "dependencies", "environment", "security"],
    "models_validated": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"],
    "deployment_readiness": "verified",
    "automation_status": "integrated"
}

print("Django CI/CD Configuration Validation:")
print("=" * 40)
print(f"🎯 CI/CD Validation Summary:")
print(f"   🤖 Pipeline stage: {cicd_validation['pipeline_stage']}")
print(f"   📊 Models validated: {len(cicd_validation['models_validated'])}")
print(f"   🔧 Validation scope: {len(cicd_validation['validation_scope'])} checks")
print(f"   ✅ Deployment configuration verified")
Django CI/CD Configuration Validation: ======================================== 🎯 CI/CD Validation Summary: 🤖 Pipeline stage: configuration_validation 📊 Models validated: 5 🔧 Validation scope: 4 checks ✅ Deployment configuration verified

docker compose top

Official Docs: docker compose top | Docker CLI: docker compose top

Display running processes in Django service containers showing system resource usage and activity.

Full Description

The docker compose top command displays the running processes within your anihpj Django service containers, showing process IDs, CPU usage, memory consumption, and command details. This provides real-time insight into what's happening inside each container and helps monitor resource utilization.

For Django applications, this command is essential for performance monitoring, debugging application behavior, identifying resource bottlenecks, and understanding process activity in your anihpj jobpost application. It helps track Django workers, database connections, cache processes, and background tasks.

Syntax

# Show processes for all services
docker compose top

# Show processes for specific service
docker compose top web

# Show processes with extended information
docker compose top -e

# Monitor specific Django services
docker compose top web db redis

# Show processes in specific format
docker compose top --format table

# Continuous monitoring (with watch)
watch -n 2 "docker compose top"

# Show worker processes only
docker compose top worker

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to monitor. Focus on specific containers like web, worker, or db.
  • -e, --extended: Show extended process information for Django containers including environment and arguments.
  • --format: Output format for Django process display. Control how process information is presented.
  • Multiple Services: Monitor several Django services simultaneously. View processes across web, database, and cache services.
  • Watch Integration: Combine with watch command for continuous Django process monitoring and real-time updates.

Attributes

  • Process Monitoring: Real-time view of Django application processes including web servers, workers, and background tasks.
  • Resource Tracking: Shows CPU and memory usage for Django processes, helping identify performance bottlenecks.
  • Service-Specific View: Filter Django process display by service type for focused monitoring of specific components.
  • System Integration: Leverages host system process table to show Django container process activity and resource consumption.
  • Debugging Support: Identifies running Django processes for troubleshooting, performance analysis, and optimization.

Methods

Performance Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose top web

Parameters: service name for focused Django web server process monitoring

Monitor Django web server processes including Gunicorn workers, request handlers, and resource utilization patterns.

Background Task Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose top worker

Parameters: worker service for Django Celery task and background job monitoring

Track Django background task execution including job processing, queue management, and worker activity.

Return Value

Returns formatted table of Django service processes showing PID, CPU%, MEM%, command, and process status information.

  • Success (0): Process information displayed successfully
  • No Processes (0): Services running but no processes to display
  • Service Error (1): Specified service not found or not running

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Performance Monitoring (High-Load Process Analysis)

Monitor production Django processes during peak traffic handling User authentication, JobPost search operations, Skills verification requests, and Location-based filtering with real-time performance analysis.

# Monitor all Django services during peak load
docker compose top

# Focus on web server processes
docker compose top web

# Monitor background workers
docker compose top worker

# Extended process information
docker compose top -e web worker

Django Models Context & Process Monitoring:

# Production process monitoring for Django anihpj application
# Monitoring User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model operations

import datetime
import statistics
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Peak traffic process monitoring analysis
peak_traffic_analysis = {
    "monitoring_period": {
        "start_time": "2026-01-11 14:00:00",
        "end_time": "2026-01-11 16:00:00", 
        "duration": "2 hours",
        "traffic_type": "weekend_job_search_peak"
    },
    "traffic_patterns": {
        "concurrent_users": 2847,
        "requests_per_minute": 1234,
        "job_searches_per_minute": 445,
        "skill_verifications_per_minute": 156,
        "new_registrations_per_minute": 23
    }
}

# Django service process monitoring results
service_process_monitoring = {
    "web_service": {
        "container_name": "anihpj_web_1",
        "process_count": 8,
        "processes": [
            {
                "pid": 1,
                "command": "python manage.py runserver",
                "cpu_percent": 15.2,
                "memory_mb": 245,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "main_django_application"
            },
            {
                "pid": 23,
                "command": "gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application",
                "cpu_percent": 22.8,
                "memory_mb": 198,
                "status": "running", 
                "purpose": "wsgi_server_master"
            },
            {
                "pid": 24,
                "command": "gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 1",
                "cpu_percent": 45.6,
                "memory_mb": 187,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "user_auth_requests",
                "models_served": ["User"]
            },
            {
                "pid": 25,
                "command": "gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 2", 
                "cpu_percent": 38.4,
                "memory_mb": 203,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "jobpost_search_api",
                "models_served": ["JobPost", "Location"]
            },
            {
                "pid": 26,
                "command": "gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 3",
                "cpu_percent": 29.7,
                "memory_mb": 176,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "skills_verification",
                "models_served": ["Skills", "Author"]
            }
        ],
        "performance_metrics": {
            "total_cpu_usage": 151.7,
            "total_memory_mb": 1009,
            "average_response_time": "245ms",
            "request_queue_length": 12
        },
        "model_operations": {
            "User": {
                "auth_requests": 234,
                "profile_updates": 67,
                "session_validations": 445
            },
            "JobPost": {
                "search_queries": 445,
                "creation_requests": 34,
                "application_submissions": 123
            },
            "Skills": {
                "verification_requests": 156,
                "search_filtering": 289,
                "demand_analysis": 45
            }
        }
    },
    "db_service": {
        "container_name": "anihpj_db_1",
        "process_count": 12,
        "processes": [
            {
                "pid": 1,
                "command": "postgres",
                "cpu_percent": 8.3,
                "memory_mb": 512,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "postgresql_master"
            },
            {
                "pid": 45,
                "command": "postgres: autovacuum launcher",
                "cpu_percent": 2.1,
                "memory_mb": 23,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "database_maintenance"
            },
            {
                "pid": 67,
                "command": "postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT",
                "cpu_percent": 12.4,
                "memory_mb": 89,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "user_model_queries",
                "table_focus": "auth_user"
            },
            {
                "pid": 68,
                "command": "postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT",
                "cpu_percent": 18.7,
                "memory_mb": 134,
                "status": "running", 
                "purpose": "jobpost_search_queries",
                "table_focus": "jobpost_jobpost"
            },
            {
                "pid": 69,
                "command": "postgres: anihpj_user anihpj UPDATE",
                "cpu_percent": 6.9,
                "memory_mb": 67,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "skills_verification_updates",
                "table_focus": "jobpost_skills"
            }
        ],
        "database_metrics": {
            "active_connections": 45,
            "connection_pool_usage": "67%",
            "query_cache_hit_rate": "94.2%",
            "table_lock_count": 3,
            "slow_query_count": 2
        },
        "model_performance": {
            "User_table": {
                "size": "125MB",
                "queries_per_minute": 445,
                "index_usage": "optimal",
                "cache_hit_rate": "96.1%"
            },
            "JobPost_table": {
                "size": "456MB", 
                "queries_per_minute": 234,
                "index_usage": "good",
                "cache_hit_rate": "92.4%"
            },
            "Skills_table": {
                "size": "78MB",
                "queries_per_minute": 156,
                "index_usage": "optimal",
                "cache_hit_rate": "97.8%"
            }
        }
    },
    "worker_service": {
        "container_name": "anihpj_worker_1",
        "process_count": 6,
        "processes": [
            {
                "pid": 1,
                "command": "celery worker -A anihpj",
                "cpu_percent": 14.6,
                "memory_mb": 234,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "celery_main_worker"
            },
            {
                "pid": 23,
                "command": "python -c import anihpj.tasks",
                "cpu_percent": 8.2,
                "memory_mb": 89,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "task_import_validation"
            },
            {
                "pid": 34,
                "command": "celery worker: job_notification_task",
                "cpu_percent": 11.4,
                "memory_mb": 145,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "jobpost_notifications",
                "models_involved": ["JobPost", "User"]
            },
            {
                "pid": 35,
                "command": "celery worker: skill_verification_task",
                "cpu_percent": 7.8,
                "memory_mb": 123,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "skills_verification_processing", 
                "models_involved": ["Skills", "Author"]
            },
            {
                "pid": 36,
                "command": "celery worker: location_geocoding_task",
                "cpu_percent": 5.3,
                "memory_mb": 98,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "location_processing",
                "models_involved": ["Location"]
            }
        ],
        "task_metrics": {
            "active_tasks": 23,
            "completed_tasks_hour": 1234,
            "failed_tasks_hour": 3,
            "queue_length": 45,
            "average_task_time": "4.2s"
        },
        "model_task_breakdown": {
            "User_tasks": {
                "email_notifications": 67,
                "profile_updates": 23,
                "authentication_cleanup": 12
            },
            "JobPost_tasks": {
                "notification_sending": 89,
                "status_updates": 34,
                "search_indexing": 56
            },
            "Skills_tasks": {
                "verification_processing": 45,
                "demand_calculation": 12,
                "cache_updates": 78
            }
        }
    },
    "redis_service": {
        "container_name": "anihpj_redis_1",
        "process_count": 3,
        "processes": [
            {
                "pid": 1,
                "command": "redis-server *:6379",
                "cpu_percent": 6.7,
                "memory_mb": 156,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "redis_main_server"
            },
            {
                "pid": 12,
                "command": "redis-server: saving DB",
                "cpu_percent": 3.2,
                "memory_mb": 45,
                "status": "running",
                "purpose": "persistence_backup"
            }
        ],
        "cache_metrics": {
            "memory_usage": "67%",
            "keyspace_hits": 9456,
            "keyspace_misses": 234,
            "hit_rate": "97.6%",
            "connected_clients": 23
        },
        "model_cache_usage": {
            "User_sessions": {
                "keys": 2847,
                "memory": "45MB",
                "ttl_average": "3600s"
            },
            "JobPost_search": {
                "keys": 1234,
                "memory": "78MB", 
                "ttl_average": "1800s"
            },
            "Skills_verification": {
                "keys": 567,
                "memory": "23MB",
                "ttl_average": "7200s"
            }
        }
    }
}

# Resource utilization analysis
resource_analysis = {
    "system_totals": {
        "total_cpu_usage": 198.4,
        "total_memory_mb": 2156,
        "total_processes": 29,
        "system_load_average": 2.84
    },
    "performance_thresholds": {
        "cpu_warning": 80.0,
        "memory_warning": 85.0,
        "current_cpu_status": "normal",
        "current_memory_status": "normal"
    },
    "bottleneck_identification": {
        "primary_bottleneck": "database_query_optimization",
        "secondary_concern": "worker_queue_length",
        "recommendations": [
            "optimize_jobpost_search_queries",
            "increase_worker_pool_size",
            "implement_skills_cache_warming"
        ]
    }
}

# Performance optimization recommendations
optimization_recommendations = {
    "User_model_optimization": {
        "current_performance": "good",
        "recommendations": [
            "implement_session_clustering",
            "optimize_authentication_queries",
            "add_profile_caching"
        ],
        "expected_improvement": "15% response time reduction"
    },
    "JobPost_model_optimization": {
        "current_performance": "moderate",
        "recommendations": [
            "add_search_result_caching",
            "optimize_location_join_queries", 
            "implement_pagination_improvements"
        ],
        "expected_improvement": "25% search performance boost"
    },
    "Skills_model_optimization": {
        "current_performance": "excellent",
        "recommendations": [
            "maintain_current_cache_strategy",
            "consider_demand_calculation_optimization"
        ],
        "expected_improvement": "5% minor improvements"
    }
}

# Print peak traffic process monitoring analysis
print("Django Peak Traffic Process Monitoring - anihpj Production:")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"Monitoring Period: {peak_traffic_analysis['monitoring_period']['start_time']} → {peak_traffic_analysis['monitoring_period']['end_time']}")
print(f"Traffic Type: {peak_traffic_analysis['monitoring_period']['traffic_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

traffic = peak_traffic_analysis['traffic_patterns']
print(f"\n📊 Current Traffic Load:")
print(f"   👥 Concurrent Users: {traffic['concurrent_users']:,}")
print(f"   🔄 Requests/Min: {traffic['requests_per_minute']:,}")
print(f"   🔍 Job Searches/Min: {traffic['job_searches_per_minute']:,}")
print(f"   ✅ Skill Verifications/Min: {traffic['skill_verifications_per_minute']:,}")
print(f"   📝 New Registrations/Min: {traffic['new_registrations_per_minute']:,}")

print(f"\n🐳 Service Process Analysis:")
for service, details in service_process_monitoring.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    container = details['container_name']
    process_count = details['process_count']
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name} Service ({container}):")
    print(f"      Process Count: {process_count}")
    
    if 'performance_metrics' in details:
        metrics = details['performance_metrics']
        print(f"      Total CPU: {metrics['total_cpu_usage']:.1f}%")
        print(f"      Total Memory: {metrics['total_memory_mb']}MB")
        print(f"      Avg Response: {metrics['average_response_time']}")
    
    print(f"      Key Processes:")
    for process in details['processes'][:3]:  # Show first 3 processes
        purpose = process['purpose'].replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"        - PID {process['pid']}: {purpose}")
        print(f"          CPU: {process['cpu_percent']:.1f}% | Memory: {process['memory_mb']}MB")
        if 'models_served' in process:
            models = ', '.join(process['models_served'])
            print(f"          Django Models: {models}")

    if 'model_operations' in details:
        print(f"      Model Operations:")
        for model, ops in details['model_operations'].items():
            total_ops = sum(ops.values())
            print(f"        📊 {model}: {total_ops} operations")

print(f"\n💾 Database Performance:")
db_metrics = service_process_monitoring['db_service']['database_metrics']
print(f"   Active Connections: {db_metrics['active_connections']}")
print(f"   Connection Pool Usage: {db_metrics['connection_pool_usage']}")
print(f"   Cache Hit Rate: {db_metrics['query_cache_hit_rate']}")
print(f"   Slow Queries: {db_metrics['slow_query_count']}")

db_perf = service_process_monitoring['db_service']['model_performance']
for model, perf in db_perf.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_table', '').replace('_', ' ')
    print(f"\n   📊 {model_name.title()} Table:")
    print(f"      Size: {perf['size']} | Queries/Min: {perf['queries_per_minute']}")
    print(f"      Index Usage: {perf['index_usage'].title()} | Hit Rate: {perf['cache_hit_rate']}")

print(f"\n🔄 Background Workers:")
worker_metrics = service_process_monitoring['worker_service']['task_metrics']
print(f"   Active Tasks: {worker_metrics['active_tasks']}")
print(f"   Completed/Hour: {worker_metrics['completed_tasks_hour']:,}")
print(f"   Failed/Hour: {worker_metrics['failed_tasks_hour']}")
print(f"   Queue Length: {worker_metrics['queue_length']}")

print(f"\n   Task Breakdown:")
worker_breakdown = service_process_monitoring['worker_service']['model_task_breakdown']
for model, tasks in worker_breakdown.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_tasks', '').replace('_', ' ')
    total_tasks = sum(tasks.values())
    print(f"     📊 {model_name.title()}: {total_tasks} tasks")

print(f"\n⚡ Redis Cache Performance:")
cache_metrics = service_process_monitoring['redis_service']['cache_metrics']
print(f"   Memory Usage: {cache_metrics['memory_usage']}")
print(f"   Hit Rate: {cache_metrics['hit_rate']}")
print(f"   Connected Clients: {cache_metrics['connected_clients']}")

cache_usage = service_process_monitoring['redis_service']['model_cache_usage']
for cache_type, usage in cache_usage.items():
    cache_name = cache_type.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"   📊 {cache_name}: {usage['keys']} keys, {usage['memory']}")

print(f"\n🎯 System Resource Summary:")
totals = resource_analysis['system_totals']
print(f"   Total CPU Usage: {totals['total_cpu_usage']:.1f}%")
print(f"   Total Memory: {totals['total_memory_mb']:,}MB")
print(f"   Total Processes: {totals['total_processes']}")
print(f"   System Load: {totals['system_load_average']}")

print(f"\n🔧 Performance Recommendations:")
for model, rec in optimization_recommendations.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_optimization', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    performance = rec['current_performance'].title()
    improvement = rec['expected_improvement']
    
    print(f"\n   📊 {model_name} Model ({performance} Performance):")
    print(f"      Expected Improvement: {improvement}")
    for recommendation in rec['recommendations'][:2]:  # Show first 2 recommendations
        rec_text = recommendation.replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"        - {rec_text}")

print(f"\n✅ Peak Traffic Handling Status:")
print(f"   🚀 All services responding normally")
print(f"   📊 Resource utilization within acceptable ranges")
print(f"   🔄 Background tasks processing efficiently")
print(f"   💾 Database performance optimized")
print(f"   ⚡ Cache hit rates exceeding targets")
print(f"   👥 {traffic['concurrent_users']:,} users served successfully")
anihpj_web_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 root 0:00 python manage.py runserver 23 root 0:02 gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application 24 www 0:45 gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 1 25 www 0:38 gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 2 26 www 0:29 gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --worker 3 anihpj_db_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 postgres 0:12 postgres 45 postgres 0:00 postgres: autovacuum launcher 67 postgres 0:04 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT 68 postgres 0:07 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT 69 postgres 0:02 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj UPDATE anihpj_worker_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 celery 0:15 celery worker -A anihpj 34 celery 0:11 celery worker: job_notification_task 35 celery 0:07 celery worker: skill_verification_task 36 celery 0:05 celery worker: location_geocoding_task anihpj_redis_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 redis 0:06 redis-server *:6379 12 redis 0:03 redis-server: saving DB Django Peak Traffic Process Monitoring - anihpj Production: ============================================================ Monitoring Period: 2026-01-11 14:00:00 → 2026-01-11 16:00:00 Traffic Type: Weekend Job Search Peak 📊 Current Traffic Load: 👥 Concurrent Users: 2,847 🔄 Requests/Min: 1,234 🔍 Job Searches/Min: 445 ✅ Skill Verifications/Min: 156 📝 New Registrations/Min: 23 🐳 Service Process Analysis: 📋 Web Service (anihpj_web_1): Process Count: 8 Total CPU: 151.7% Total Memory: 1009MB Avg Response: 245ms Key Processes: - PID 24: User Auth Requests CPU: 45.6% | Memory: 187MB Django Models: User - PID 25: Jobpost Search Api CPU: 38.4% | Memory: 203MB Django Models: JobPost, Location - PID 26: Skills Verification CPU: 29.7% | Memory: 176MB Django Models: Skills, Author 💾 Database Performance: Active Connections: 45 Connection Pool Usage: 67% Cache Hit Rate: 94.2% Slow Queries: 2 📊 User Table: Size: 125MB | Queries/Min: 445 Index Usage: Optimal | Hit Rate: 96.1% 📊 Jobpost Table: Size: 456MB | Queries/Min: 234 Index Usage: Good | Hit Rate: 92.4% 🔄 Background Workers: Active Tasks: 23 Completed/Hour: 1,234 Failed/Hour: 3 Queue Length: 45 🎯 System Resource Summary: Total CPU Usage: 198.4% Total Memory: 2,156MB Total Processes: 29 System Load: 2.84
Example 2: Background Worker Analysis (Task Processing Performance)

Monitor Django background worker processes handling Skills verification queues, JobPost notification delivery, User email processing, and Location geocoding tasks with detailed task performance metrics.

# Monitor Celery worker processes
docker compose top worker

# Extended worker information
docker compose top -e worker

# Monitor worker with continuous updates
watch -n 5 "docker compose top worker"

Django Models Context & Worker Analysis:

# Background worker process analysis for Django task processing
# Monitoring Skills, JobPost, User, Location model background tasks

worker_analysis = {
    "monitoring_focus": "background_task_processing",
    "worker_processes": 12,
    "active_tasks": 67,
    "models_processed": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Location"],
    "queue_performance": "optimal"
}

print("Django Background Worker Analysis - Task Processing:")
print("=" * 52)
print(f"🎯 Worker Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   ⚙️ Active worker processes: {worker_analysis['worker_processes']}")
print(f"   📊 Current tasks: {worker_analysis['active_tasks']}")
print(f"   🔧 Models processed: {', '.join(worker_analysis['models_processed'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Queue performance: {worker_analysis['queue_performance']}")
anihpj_worker_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 celery 0:15 celery worker -A anihpj --loglevel=info 34 celery 0:11 celery worker: skill_verification_task 35 celery 0:07 celery worker: job_notification_task 36 celery 0:05 celery worker: user_email_task 37 celery 0:08 celery worker: location_geocoding_task Django Background Worker Analysis - Task Processing: ==================================================== 🎯 Worker Analysis Summary: ⚙️ Active worker processes: 12 📊 Current tasks: 67 🔧 Models processed: Skills, JobPost, User, Location ✅ Queue performance: optimal
Example 3: Database Performance Monitoring (Query Process Analysis)

Analyze Django database processes executing Skills search queries, JobPost filtering operations, User authentication checks, and Location-based searches with connection and performance tracking.

# Monitor database processes
docker compose top db

# Extended database process information
docker compose top -e db

# Monitor database with Redis together
docker compose top db redis

Django Models Context & Database Analysis:

# Database process monitoring for Django model operations
# Analyzing Skills, JobPost, User, Location database performance

db_analysis = {
    "database_processes": 18,
    "active_connections": 45,
    "query_types": ["SELECT", "INSERT", "UPDATE", "DELETE"],
    "models_queried": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"],
    "performance_status": "excellent"
}

print("Django Database Performance - Process Analysis:")
print("=" * 48)
print(f"🎯 Database Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   💾 Database processes: {db_analysis['database_processes']}")
print(f"   🔗 Active connections: {db_analysis['active_connections']}")
print(f"   📊 Models queried: {', '.join(db_analysis['models_queried'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Performance: {db_analysis['performance_status']}")
anihpj_db_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 postgres 0:12 postgres 45 postgres 0:00 postgres: autovacuum launcher 67 postgres 0:04 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT 68 postgres 0:07 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj SELECT 69 postgres 0:02 postgres: anihpj_user anihpj UPDATE Django Database Performance - Process Analysis: ================================================ 🎯 Database Analysis Summary: 💾 Database processes: 18 🔗 Active connections: 45 📊 Models queried: Skills, JobPost, User, Author, Location ✅ Performance: excellent
Example 4: Development Debugging (Service Process Troubleshooting)

Debug Django development processes for Skills verification issues, JobPost search problems, User authentication debugging, and Location service troubleshooting with detailed process inspection.

# Debug specific service processes
docker compose top web db worker

# Get detailed process arguments
docker compose top -e web

# Monitor processes for debugging
watch -n 1 "docker compose top web | grep python"

Django Models Context & Development Debugging:

# Development debugging process analysis
# Troubleshooting Skills, JobPost, User model development issues

debug_analysis = {
    "debugging_scope": "development_environment",
    "services_monitored": ["web", "db", "worker"],
    "debug_focus": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User"],
    "issues_identified": "performance_bottlenecks",
    "resolution_status": "in_progress"
}

print("Django Development Debugging - Process Analysis:")
print("=" * 50)
print(f"🎯 Debug Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   🐛 Debug scope: {debug_analysis['debugging_scope']}")
print(f"   📊 Focus models: {', '.join(debug_analysis['debug_focus'])}")
print(f"   🔧 Services monitored: {', '.join(debug_analysis['services_monitored'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Resolution: {debug_analysis['resolution_status']}")
anihpj_web_1 PID USER TIME COMMAND 1 root 0:00 python manage.py runserver --settings=anihpj.settings.dev 24 www 0:45 python manage.py shell 25 www 0:38 python manage.py test jobpost.tests Django Development Debugging - Process Analysis: ================================================== 🎯 Debug Analysis Summary: 🐛 Debug scope: development_environment 📊 Focus models: Skills, JobPost, User 🔧 Services monitored: web, db, worker ✅ Resolution: in_progress
Example 5: Multi-Service Resource Monitoring (Complete System Analysis)

Monitor all Django services simultaneously for comprehensive Skills processing, JobPost management, User operations, Author workflows, and Location services with complete resource utilization tracking.

# Monitor all services with extended info
docker compose top -e

# Continuous monitoring of all services
watch -n 3 "docker compose top"

# Export process monitoring data
docker compose top > process-monitoring-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).txt

    "monitoring_scope": "complete_system_analysis",
    "total_services": 6,
    "total_processes": 42,
    "models_covered": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"],
    "resource_utilization": "within_limits",
    "system_health": "excellent"
}
