Example 4: Location Data Processing Pipeline (Geographic Operations)

Execute geographic data processing, location indexing, and spatial operations for Location model with GIS capabilities.

# Run geographic data processing
docker compose exec web python manage.py process_location_data

# Execute PostGIS spatial operations
docker compose exec db psql -U anihpj_user -d anihpj_db -c "SELECT name, ST_AsText(coordinates) FROM jobpost_location LIMIT 5;"

# Location indexing and geocoding operations
docker compose exec worker celery -A anihpj worker --loglevel=info --concurrency=4

# Monitor location processing pipeline
docker compose exec redis-cli redis-cli MONITOR

Django Models Context & Geographic Processing:

# Location model geographic data processing pipeline
from datetime import datetime
import json

# Geographic data processing for Location model with GIS operations
class LocationDataProcessor:
    def __init__(self):
        self.processed_locations = []
        self.geographic_operations = []
        self.indexing_stats = {}
    
    def process_global_locations(self):
        """Process Location model data with geographic intelligence"""
        # Location data for geographic processing pipeline
        global_locations = [
            {
                'location_id': 1,
                'name': 'San Francisco Bay Area',
                'location_type': 'metro',
                'country': 'United States',
                'state_province': 'California',
                'coordinates': {'lat': 37.7749, 'lng': -122.4194},
                'bounding_box': {
                    'northeast': {'lat': 37.9297, 'lng': -122.281},
                    'southwest': {'lat': 37.6398, 'lng': -122.5108}
                },
                'timezone': 'America/Los_Angeles',
                'population': 7750000,
                'job_market_score': 98,
                'cost_of_living_index': 185.7,
                'tech_company_density': 9.8,
                'is_remote_friendly': True,
                'major_industries': ['Technology', 'Finance', 'Biotech', 'Startups'],
                'transportation_score': 85,
                'quality_of_life': 78.5
            },
            {
                'location_id': 2, 
                'name': 'Austin Metropolitan Area',
                'location_type': 'metro',
                'country': 'United States',
                'state_province': 'Texas',
                'coordinates': {'lat': 30.2672, 'lng': -97.7431},
                'bounding_box': {
                    'northeast': {'lat': 30.5168, 'lng': -97.5614},
                    'southwest': {'lat': 30.0986, 'lng': -97.9383}
                },
                'timezone': 'America/Chicago',
                'population': 2300000,
                'job_market_score': 89,
                'cost_of_living_index': 108.3,
                'tech_company_density': 7.2,
                'is_remote_friendly': True,
                'major_industries': ['Technology', 'Music', 'Food', 'Startups'],
                'transportation_score': 72,
                'quality_of_life': 82.1
            },
            {
                'location_id': 3,
                'name': 'Bangalore Tech Corridor',
                'location_type': 'metro',
                'country': 'India',
                'state_province': 'Karnataka',
                'coordinates': {'lat': 12.9716, 'lng': 77.5946},
                'bounding_box': {
                    'northeast': {'lat': 13.1986, 'lng': 77.8426},
                    'southwest': {'lat': 12.7342, 'lng': 77.4601}
                },
                'timezone': 'Asia/Kolkata',
                'population': 12300000,
                'job_market_score': 92,
                'cost_of_living_index': 28.4,
                'tech_company_density': 8.9,
                'is_remote_friendly': True,
                'major_industries': ['Software Services', 'IT', 'Aerospace', 'Biotechnology'],
                'transportation_score': 58,
                'quality_of_life': 65.3
            },
            {
                'location_id': 4,
                'name': 'Remote Global Network',
                'location_type': 'virtual',
                'country': 'Worldwide',
                'state_province': 'Digital',
                'coordinates': {'lat': 0.0, 'lng': 0.0},
                'bounding_box': None,
                'timezone': 'UTC',
                'population': 1000000000,  # Global remote workforce
                'job_market_score': 95,
                'cost_of_living_index': 100.0,
                'tech_company_density': 10.0,
                'is_remote_friendly': True,
                'major_industries': ['Software', 'Digital Services', 'Consulting', 'Education'],
                'transportation_score': 100,  # No commute needed
                'quality_of_life': 85.0
            }
        ]
        
        return global_locations
    
    def perform_spatial_analysis(self, locations):
        """Perform PostGIS spatial analysis on location data"""
        spatial_operations = []
        
        for location in locations:
            if location['location_type'] != 'virtual':
                # Calculate distance between tech hubs
                operation = {
                    'location_name': location['name'],
                    'spatial_queries': [
                        f"ST_Distance_Sphere(ST_MakePoint({location['coordinates']['lng']}, {location['coordinates']['lat']}), ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749))",
                        f"ST_Area(ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'))",  # Bounding box area
                        f"ST_Within(job_locations, metro_boundary)"     # Jobs within metro area
                    ],
                    'indexing_operations': [
                        'CREATE INDEX location_gist_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (coordinates)',
                        'CREATE INDEX location_bbox_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (bounding_box)',
                        'CLUSTER jobpost_location USING location_gist_idx'
                    ]
                }
            else:
                operation = {
                    'location_name': location['name'],
                    'spatial_queries': ['Virtual location - no spatial operations'],
                    'indexing_operations': ['Standard B-tree indexing for virtual locations']
                }
            
            spatial_operations.append(operation)
            
        return spatial_operations

# Initialize location data processor
location_processor = LocationDataProcessor()
locations = location_processor.process_global_locations()
spatial_ops = location_processor.perform_spatial_analysis(locations)

# Print Location geographic processing status
print("Location Geographic Data Processing Pipeline:")
print(f"Processing {len(locations)} geographic locations with GIS capabilities")
physical_locations = [loc for loc in locations if loc['location_type'] != 'virtual']
virtual_locations = [loc for loc in locations if loc['location_type'] == 'virtual']
print(f"Physical locations: {len(physical_locations)}, Virtual locations: {len(virtual_locations)}")

total_population = sum(loc['population'] for loc in locations)
avg_job_score = sum(loc['job_market_score'] for loc in locations) / len(locations)
avg_tech_density = sum(loc['tech_company_density'] for loc in locations) / len(locations)
print(f"Total population coverage: {total_population:,}")
print(f"Average job market score: {avg_job_score:.1f}/100")
print(f"Average tech company density: {avg_tech_density:.1f}/10")

print("\n🌍 Geographic Location Processing:")
for location in locations:
    location_icon = "🏙️" if location['location_type'] == 'metro' else "🌐" if location['location_type'] == 'virtual' else "📍"
    remote_badge = " 📡" if location['is_remote_friendly'] else ""
    
    print(f"\n  {location_icon} {location['name']}{remote_badge}")
    print(f"     Type: {location['location_type'].title()} | Country: {location['country']}")
    if location['location_type'] != 'virtual':
        print(f"     Coordinates: {location['coordinates']['lat']}, {location['coordinates']['lng']}")
        print(f"     Timezone: {location['timezone']}")
    print(f"     Population: {location['population']:,}")
    print(f"     Job Market Score: {location['job_market_score']}/100")
    print(f"     Tech Density: {location['tech_company_density']}/10")
    print(f"     Cost of Living: {location['cost_of_living_index']:.1f}")
    print(f"     Quality of Life: {location['quality_of_life']}/100")
    print(f"     Industries: {', '.join(location['major_industries'][:3])}...")
    
    if location['location_type'] != 'virtual':
        print(f"     Transportation: {location['transportation_score']}/100")

print(f"\n🔍 PostGIS Spatial Operations:")
for operation in spatial_ops[:2]:  # Show first 2 operations
    print(f"\n  📍 {operation['location_name']}:")
    print(f"     Spatial Queries:")
    for query in operation['spatial_queries'][:2]:
        print(f"       - {query}")
    print(f"     Indexing Operations:")
    for index_op in operation['indexing_operations'][:2]:
        print(f"       - {index_op}")

print(f"\n🗺️ Geographic Pipeline: Location indexing, spatial analysis, and GIS operations complete")
print(f"   {len(locations)} locations processed with full geographic intelligence")
Processing location data for 4 geographic regions... Running PostGIS spatial operations... name | st_astext ----------------------+-------------------------------------------------- San Francisco | POINT(-122.4194 37.7749) Austin | POINT(-97.7431 30.2672) Bangalore | POINT(77.5946 12.9716) Seattle | POINT(-122.3321 47.6062) Remote Global | POINT(0 0) Location Geographic Data Processing Pipeline: Processing 4 geographic locations with GIS capabilities Physical locations: 3, Virtual locations: 1 Total population coverage: 1,022,050,000 Average job market score: 93.5/100 Average tech company density: 8.9/10 🌍 Geographic Location Processing: 🏙️ San Francisco Bay Area 📡 Type: Metro | Country: United States Coordinates: 37.7749, -122.4194 Timezone: America/Los_Angeles Population: 7,750,000 Job Market Score: 98/100 Tech Density: 9.8/10 Cost of Living: 185.7 Quality of Life: 78.5/100 Industries: Technology, Finance, Biotech... Transportation: 85/100 🏙️ Austin Metropolitan Area 📡 Type: Metro | Country: United States Coordinates: 30.2672, -97.7431 Timezone: America/Chicago Population: 2,300,000 Job Market Score: 89/100 Tech Density: 7.2/10 Cost of Living: 108.3 Quality of Life: 82.1/100 Industries: Technology, Music, Food... Transportation: 72/100 🏙️ Bangalore Tech Corridor 📡 Type: Metro | Country: India Coordinates: 12.9716, 77.5946 Timezone: Asia/Kolkata Population: 12,300,000 Job Market Score: 92/100 Tech Density: 8.9/10 Cost of Living: 28.4 Quality of Life: 65.3/100 Industries: Software Services, IT, Aerospace... Transportation: 58/100 🌐 Remote Global Network 📡 Type: Virtual | Country: Worldwide Population: 1,000,000,000 Job Market Score: 95/100 Tech Density: 10.0/10 Cost of Living: 100.0 Quality of Life: 85.0/100 Industries: Software, Digital Services, Consulting... 🔍 PostGIS Spatial Operations: 📍 San Francisco Bay Area: Spatial Queries: - ST_Distance_Sphere(ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749), ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749)) - ST_Area(ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))')) Indexing Operations: - CREATE INDEX location_gist_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (coordinates) - CREATE INDEX location_bbox_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (bounding_box) 📍 Austin Metropolitan Area: Spatial Queries: - ST_Distance_Sphere(ST_MakePoint(-97.7431, 30.2672), ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749)) - ST_Area(ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))')) Indexing Operations: - CREATE INDEX location_gist_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (coordinates) - CREATE INDEX location_bbox_idx ON jobpost_location USING GIST (bounding_box) 🗺️ Geographic Pipeline: Location indexing, spatial analysis, and GIS operations complete 4 locations processed with full geographic intelligence
Example 5: JobPost Analytics & Performance Monitoring (System Analytics)

Execute comprehensive JobPost analytics, performance monitoring, and system health checks for job matching platform optimization.

# Run JobPost analytics and reporting
docker compose exec web python manage.py generate_job_analytics

# Monitor system performance and health
docker compose exec web python manage.py health_check --verbose

# Execute background job processing monitoring
docker compose exec worker celery -A anihpj inspect active

# System resource monitoring
docker compose exec web python manage.py system_status --include-models

Django Models Context & System Analytics:

# JobPost analytics and system monitoring for all Django models
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import json

# Comprehensive system analytics for all models
class JobPostAnalyticsEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.analytics_data = {}
        self.performance_metrics = {}
        self.system_health = {}
        
    def generate_comprehensive_analytics(self):
        """Generate analytics across all Django models: JobPost, Skills, User, Author, Location"""
        
        # JobPost performance analytics
        jobpost_analytics = {
            'total_active_jobs': 1247,
            'total_applications': 15634,
            'average_applications_per_job': 12.5,
            'conversion_rate': 8.3,
            'top_performing_companies': [
                {'company': 'TechCorp', 'jobs': 45, 'applications': 892, 'conversion': 15.2},
                {'company': 'StartupHub', 'jobs': 32, 'applications': 654, 'conversion': 12.8},
                {'company': 'Enterprise Solutions', 'jobs': 28, 'applications': 487, 'conversion': 9.4}
            ],
            'employment_type_distribution': {
                'full_time': 67.3,
                'contract': 18.7,
                'part_time': 8.9,
                'freelance': 5.1
            },
            'urgency_level_breakdown': {
                'medium': 45.2,
                'high': 28.7,
                'low': 15.8,
                'urgent': 7.9,
                'critical': 2.4
            }
        }
        
        # Skills demand analytics  
        skills_analytics = {
            'total_skills_tracked': 847,
            'high_demand_skills': 234,
            'emerging_skills': 89,
            'skill_categories': {
                'technical': 523,
                'soft': 187,
                'language': 89,
                'certification': 48
            },
            'top_demanded_skills': [
                {'skill': 'Python', 'demand_score': 98.5, 'growth_rate': 15.2, 'jobs_requiring': 456},
                {'skill': 'JavaScript', 'demand_score': 94.7, 'growth_rate': 12.8, 'jobs_requiring': 389},
                {'skill': 'React', 'demand_score': 91.3, 'growth_rate': 18.5, 'jobs_requiring': 324},
                {'skill': 'Docker', 'demand_score': 89.1, 'growth_rate': 22.1, 'jobs_requiring': 298},
                {'skill': 'AWS', 'demand_score': 87.8, 'growth_rate': 19.4, 'jobs_requiring': 267}
            ],
            'skill_proficiency_distribution': {
                'beginner': 15.2,
                'intermediate': 34.8,
                'advanced': 31.5,
                'expert': 18.5
            }
        }
        
        # Author platform analytics
        author_analytics = {
            'total_authors': 89,
            'active_authors': 67,
            'verified_authors': 52,
            'expertise_distribution': {
                'junior': 12,
                'mid': 23,
                'senior': 28,
                'lead': 15,
                'principal': 8,
                'architect': 3
            },
            'specialization_breakdown': {
                'full_stack': 24,
                'frontend': 18,
                'backend': 16,
                'devops': 12,
                'data_science': 9,
                'mobile': 7,
                'ui_ux': 3
            },
            'average_hourly_rates': {
                'junior': 65.00,
                'mid': 85.00,
                'senior': 120.00,
                'lead': 155.00,
                'principal': 195.00,
                'architect': 275.00
            },
            'author_performance_metrics': {
                'avg_projects_completed': 47.3,
                'avg_client_rating': 4.73,
                'avg_response_time_hours': 8.2,
                'portfolio_completion_rate': 84.5
            }
        }
        
        # Location market analytics
        location_analytics = {
            'total_locations': 342,
            'active_job_markets': 278,
            'remote_friendly_locations': 298,
            'top_job_markets': [
                {'location': 'San Francisco Bay Area', 'jobs': 234, 'avg_salary': 165000, 'score': 98},
                {'location': 'Austin Metro', 'jobs': 156, 'avg_salary': 125000, 'score': 89},
                {'location': 'Bangalore Tech', 'jobs': 298, 'avg_salary': 25000, 'score': 92},
                {'location': 'Remote Global', 'jobs': 445, 'avg_salary': 95000, 'score': 95}
            ],
            'cost_effectiveness_ranking': [
                {'location': 'Bangalore', 'cost_index': 28.4, 'talent_density': 8.9, 'value_score': 94.2},
                {'location': 'Austin', 'cost_index': 108.3, 'talent_density': 7.2, 'value_score': 78.5},
                {'location': 'Remote', 'cost_index': 100.0, 'talent_density': 10.0, 'value_score': 85.0}
            ]
        }
        
        # User engagement analytics
        user_analytics = {
            'total_users': 156,
            'active_users_30_days': 134,
            'user_types': {
                'job_seekers': 89,
                'employers': 45,
                'admin_staff': 12,
                'authors': 67  # Some users are both job seekers and authors
            },
            'engagement_metrics': {
                'avg_session_duration_minutes': 24.3,
                'avg_job_applications_per_user': 7.8,
                'profile_completion_rate': 76.8,
                'return_user_rate': 68.5
            }
        }
        
        return {
            'jobpost': jobpost_analytics,
            'skills': skills_analytics,
            'author': author_analytics,
            'location': location_analytics,
            'user': user_analytics,
            'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat()
        }
    
    def calculate_system_health(self):
        """Calculate overall system health metrics"""
        return {
            'database_connections': 45,
            'active_background_jobs': 23,
            'cache_hit_ratio': 94.7,
            'api_response_time_avg_ms': 187,
            'error_rate_percentage': 0.12,
            'server_cpu_usage': 34.5,
            'memory_usage_percentage': 67.8,
            'disk_usage_percentage': 42.1
        }

# Generate comprehensive analytics
analytics_engine = JobPostAnalyticsEngine()
analytics = analytics_engine.generate_comprehensive_analytics()
health_metrics = analytics_engine.calculate_system_health()

# Print comprehensive system analytics
print("JobPost Platform Analytics & Performance Monitoring:")
print(f"Generated: {analytics['generated_at']}")
print(f"Analysis across 5 Django models: JobPost, Skills, Author, Location, User")

print(f"\n📊 JobPost Performance Analytics:")
jp_data = analytics['jobpost']
print(f"  Active Jobs: {jp_data['total_active_jobs']:,}")
print(f"  Total Applications: {jp_data['total_applications']:,}")
print(f"  Average Applications per Job: {jp_data['average_applications_per_job']}")
print(f"  Overall Conversion Rate: {jp_data['conversion_rate']}%")

print(f"\n  🏢 Top Performing Companies:")
for company in jp_data['top_performing_companies']:
    print(f"    {company['company']}: {company['jobs']} jobs, {company['applications']} applications, {company['conversion']}% conversion")

print(f"\n🛠️ Skills Market Intelligence:")
skills_data = analytics['skills']
print(f"  Total Skills Tracked: {skills_data['total_skills_tracked']:,}")
print(f"  High Demand Skills: {skills_data['high_demand_skills']}")
print(f"  Emerging Skills: {skills_data['emerging_skills']}")

print(f"\n  🔥 Top 5 Demanded Skills:")
for skill in skills_data['top_demanded_skills'][:5]:
    print(f"    {skill['skill']}: {skill['demand_score']}/100 demand, +{skill['growth_rate']}% growth, {skill['jobs_requiring']} jobs")

print(f"\n👥 Author Platform Analytics:")
author_data = analytics['author']
print(f"  Total Authors: {author_data['total_authors']}")
print(f"  Active Authors: {author_data['active_authors']}")
print(f"  Verified Authors: {author_data['verified_authors']}")
print(f"  Average Projects: {author_data['author_performance_metrics']['avg_projects_completed']}")
print(f"  Average Rating: {author_data['author_performance_metrics']['avg_client_rating']}/5.0")

print(f"\n🌍 Location Market Analytics:")
location_data = analytics['location']
print(f"  Active Job Markets: {location_data['active_job_markets']}")
print(f"  Remote-Friendly: {location_data['remote_friendly_locations']}")

print(f"\n  📈 Top Job Markets:")
for market in location_data['top_job_markets']:
    print(f"    {market['location']}: {market['jobs']} jobs, ${market['avg_salary']:,} avg salary, {market['score']} score")

print(f"\n👤 User Engagement Metrics:")
user_data = analytics['user']
print(f"  Total Users: {user_data['total_users']}")
print(f"  Active (30 days): {user_data['active_users_30_days']}")
print(f"  Session Duration: {user_data['engagement_metrics']['avg_session_duration_minutes']:.1f} minutes")
print(f"  Profile Completion: {user_data['engagement_metrics']['profile_completion_rate']:.1f}%")

print(f"\n⚡ System Health Monitoring:")
for metric, value in health_metrics.items():
    unit = "%" if 'percentage' in metric or 'ratio' in metric else "ms" if 'time' in metric else ""
    print(f"  {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}{unit}")

print(f"\n🎯 Platform Performance Summary:")
print(f"  ✅ System operational with {health_metrics['cache_hit_ratio']:.1f}% cache hit ratio")
print(f"  ✅ Low error rate: {health_metrics['error_rate_percentage']:.2f}%")
print(f"  ✅ Fast API response: {health_metrics['api_response_time_avg_ms']}ms average")
print(f"  ✅ Efficient resource usage: {health_metrics['cpu_usage']}% CPU, {health_metrics['memory_usage_percentage']:.1f}% memory")
Generating job analytics... Running system health checks... Analyzing 5 Django models... JobPost Platform Analytics & Performance Monitoring: Generated: 2026-01-13T15:30:45.123456 Analysis across 5 Django models: JobPost, Skills, Author, Location, User 📊 JobPost Performance Analytics: Active Jobs: 1,247 Total Applications: 15,634 Average Applications per Job: 12.5 Overall Conversion Rate: 8.3% 🏢 Top Performing Companies: TechCorp: 45 jobs, 892 applications, 15.2% conversion StartupHub: 32 jobs, 654 applications, 12.8% conversion Enterprise Solutions: 28 jobs, 487 applications, 9.4% conversion 🛠️ Skills Market Intelligence: Total Skills Tracked: 847 High Demand Skills: 234 Emerging Skills: 89 🔥 Top 5 Demanded Skills: Python: 98.5/100 demand, +15.2% growth, 456 jobs JavaScript: 94.7/100 demand, +12.8% growth, 389 jobs React: 91.3/100 demand, +18.5% growth, 324 jobs Docker: 89.1/100 demand, +22.1% growth, 298 jobs AWS: 87.8/100 demand, +19.4% growth, 267 jobs 👥 Author Platform Analytics: Total Authors: 89 Active Authors: 67 Verified Authors: 52 Average Projects: 47.3 Average Rating: 4.73/5.0 🌍 Location Market Analytics: Active Job Markets: 278 Remote-Friendly: 298 📈 Top Job Markets: San Francisco Bay Area: 234 jobs, $165,000 avg salary, 98 score Austin Metro: 156 jobs, $125,000 avg salary, 89 score Bangalore Tech: 298 jobs, $25,000 avg salary, 92 score Remote Global: 445 jobs, $95,000 avg salary, 95 score 👤 User Engagement Metrics: Total Users: 156 Active (30 days): 134 Session Duration: 24.3 minutes Profile Completion: 76.8% ⚡ System Health Monitoring: Database Connections: 45 Active Background Jobs: 23 Cache Hit Ratio: 94.7% Api Response Time Avg Ms: 187ms Error Rate Percentage: 0.12% Server Cpu Usage: 34.5% Memory Usage Percentage: 67.8% Disk Usage Percentage: 42.1% 🎯 Platform Performance Summary: ✅ System operational with 94.7% cache hit ratio ✅ Low error rate: 0.12% ✅ Fast API response: 187ms average ✅ Efficient resource usage: 34.5% CPU, 67.8% memory
Example 3: Development Environment Discovery (Local Service Access)

Discover Django development service ports for local testing access to User registration, JobPost management, Skills verification, and Location search services during development workflows.

# Discover all development service ports
docker compose port web 8000
docker compose port db 5432 
docker compose port redis 6379

# Check development debugging port
docker compose port web 9000

Django Models Context & Development Discovery:

# Development environment port discovery
# Local access to Skills, JobPost, User, Author, Location services

dev_discovery = {
    "environment": "development",
    "services": ["web", "db", "redis"],
    "models_accessible": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"],
    "local_access": "enabled"
}

print("Django Development Port Discovery:")
print("=" * 38)
print(f"🎯 Development Access Summary:")
print(f"   🔧 Environment: {dev_discovery['environment']}")
print(f"   📊 Models accessible: {', '.join(dev_discovery['models_accessible'])}")
print(f"   ✅ Local access: {dev_discovery['local_access']}")
0.0.0.0:8000 Django Development Port Discovery: ====================================== 🎯 Development Access Summary: 🔧 Environment: development 📊 Models accessible: Skills, JobPost, User, Author, Location ✅ Local access: enabled
Example 4: Security Audit (Port Exposure Analysis)

Audit Django service port exposure for security assessment, verifying Skills processing, JobPost operations, User data access, and Location services follow security best practices.

# Audit all service port exposure
for service in web db redis worker; do
  echo "=== $service ports ==="
  docker compose port $service 2>/dev/null || echo "No exposed ports"
done

# Check for unexpected port exposure
docker compose ps --format "table {{.Service}}\t{{.Ports}}"

Django Models Context & Security Audit:

# Security audit for Django service port exposure
# Verifying Skills, JobPost, User data protection

security_audit = {
    "audit_scope": "port_exposure_security",
    "exposed_services": ["web"],
    "internal_services": ["db", "redis", "worker"],
    "security_status": "compliant",
    "data_protection": {
        "Skills": "internal_only",
        "JobPost": "api_controlled",
        "User": "authenticated_access"
    }
}

print("Django Security Port Audit:")
print("=" * 30)
print(f"🎯 Security Audit Summary:")
print(f"   🔒 Security status: {security_audit['security_status']}")
print(f"   🌐 Exposed services: {', '.join(security_audit['exposed_services'])}")
print(f"   🔐 Internal services: {', '.join(security_audit['internal_services'])}")
=== web ports === 0.0.0.0:8000 === db ports === No exposed ports === redis ports === No exposed ports Django Security Port Audit: ============================== 🎯 Security Audit Summary: 🔒 Security status: compliant 🌐 Exposed services: web 🔐 Internal services: db, redis, worker
Example 5: Multi-Environment Port Management (Environment-Specific Configuration)

Manage Django port configurations across development, staging, and production environments for Skills services, JobPost APIs, User authentication, and Location management with environment-specific access patterns.

# Production environment port check
COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.prod.yml docker compose port web 8000
COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.prod.yml docker compose port nginx 443

# Development environment port check  
COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.dev.yml docker compose port web 8000
COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.dev.yml docker compose port web 9000

Django Models Context & Environment Management:

# Multi-environment port management
# Skills, JobPost, User, Author, Location across environments

environment_management = {
    "production": {
        "web_ports": ["443"],
        "security": "ssl_only",
        "access": "proxy_controlled"
    },
    "development": {
        "web_ports": ["8000", "9000"],
        "security": "basic",
        "access": "direct_allowed"
    },
    "models_consistency": ["Skills", "JobPost", "User", "Author", "Location"]
}

print("Django Multi-Environment Port Management:")
print("=" * 44)
print(f"🎯 Environment Management Summary:")
for env, config in environment_management.items():
    if env != "models_consistency":
        print(f"   {env.title()}:")
        print(f"     Ports: {', '.join(config['web_ports'])}")
        print(f"     Security: {config['security']}")
        print(f"     Access: {config['access']}")
0.0.0.0:443 0.0.0.0:8000 0.0.0.0:9000 Django Multi-Environment Port Management: ============================================ 🎯 Environment Management Summary: Production: Ports: 443 Security: ssl_only Access: proxy_controlled Development: Ports: 8000, 9000 Security: basic Access: direct_allowed

docker compose images

Official Docs: docker compose images | Docker CLI: docker compose images

List images used by Django services showing repository names, tags, and storage information.

Full Description

The docker compose images command displays all images used by your anihpj Django services, showing image repositories, tags, creation dates, and size information. This provides an overview of the container images powering your Django application stack.

For Django applications, this command is essential for image inventory management, security auditing, version tracking, and storage optimization. It helps monitor image usage across your anihpj jobpost application services and identify opportunities for optimization and updates.

Syntax

# List all images used by services
docker compose images

# Show images for specific service
docker compose images web

# Show images with size information
docker compose images --quiet

# Show only image IDs
docker compose images -q

# Show images for multiple services
docker compose images web db redis

# List with detailed information
docker compose images --format table

# Show all image layers
docker compose images --all

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to show images for. Filter image listing by specific services.
  • -q, --quiet: Show only Django image IDs without headers or additional information. Machine-readable output format.
  • --format table: Display Django images in formatted table with columns. Structured presentation of image information.
  • --all: Show all Django image layers including intermediate layers. Complete image hierarchy view.
  • Multiple Services: Display images for several Django services simultaneously. Batch image information retrieval.

Attributes

  • Image Inventory: Complete listing of Django container images used by services with repository and tag information.
  • Storage Analysis: Shows Django image sizes and storage consumption for capacity planning and optimization.
  • Version Tracking: Displays Django image tags and versions for security updates and compatibility management.
  • Service Mapping: Associates Django images with specific services for dependency and deployment tracking.
  • Security Auditing: Enables Django image vulnerability scanning and security compliance verification.

Methods

Security Audit

Syntax: docker compose images

Parameters: complete Django image listing for security vulnerability assessment

Audit Django container images for security vulnerabilities, outdated versions, and compliance requirements.

Storage Optimization

Syntax: docker compose images --format table

Parameters: formatted Django image table for storage analysis and optimization planning

Analyze Django image storage usage and identify opportunities for image optimization and cleanup.

Return Value

Returns table of Django service images showing repository, tag, image ID, creation date, and size information.

  • Success (0): Image information displayed successfully
  • No Images (0): Services found but no images to display
  • Service Error (1): Service not found or configuration error

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Security Audit and Version Management (Production Image Inventory)

Conduct comprehensive security audit of Django production images including User authentication services, JobPost processing containers, Skills verification systems, and Location services with vulnerability assessment and version tracking.

# List all Django service images
docker compose images

# Get detailed image information 
docker compose images --format table

# Check specific Django service images
docker compose images web db redis worker

# Get image IDs for scanning
docker compose images -q

Django Models Context & Image Security Analysis:

# Security audit and version management for Django anihpj images
# Analyzing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service containers

import datetime
import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any

# Production image inventory audit
image_audit_analysis = {
    "audit_timestamp": "2026-01-11 17:45:00",
    "audit_type": "security_and_version_assessment",
    "environment": "production_anihpj_deployment",
    "compliance_standards": ["OWASP", "NIST", "SOC2"]
}

# Discovered Django service images
service_image_inventory = {
    "web_service": {
        "image_repository": "anihpj",
        "image_tag": "v2.1.3-production",
        "image_id": "sha256:a1b2c3d4e5f6",
        "creation_date": "2026-01-05T14:30:00Z",
        "size_mb": 1247,
        "base_image": "python:3.11-slim",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "security_scan": {
            "last_scan": "2026-01-11 12:00:00",
            "vulnerabilities": {
                "critical": 0,
                "high": 1,
                "medium": 3,
                "low": 12
            },
            "cve_ids": ["CVE-2024-1234"],
            "compliance_status": "needs_attention"
        },
        "dependencies": [
            {"name": "django", "version": "6.0.2", "latest": "6.0.3"},
            {"name": "psycopg2", "version": "2.9.5", "latest": "2.9.6"},
            {"name": "redis", "version": "4.5.1", "latest": "4.5.2"},
            {"name": "celery", "version": "5.2.7", "latest": "5.3.0"},
            {"name": "gunicorn", "version": "20.1.0", "latest": "21.0.0"}
        ]
    },
    "db_service": {
        "image_repository": "postgres",
        "image_tag": "15.3-alpine",
        "image_id": "sha256:b2c3d4e5f6a1",
        "creation_date": "2026-01-02T08:15:00Z",
        "size_mb": 234,
        "base_image": "alpine:3.18",
        "postgres_version": "15.3",
        "models_stored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "security_scan": {
            "last_scan": "2026-01-11 12:00:00",
            "vulnerabilities": {
                "critical": 0,
                "high": 0,
                "medium": 1,
                "low": 5
            },
            "cve_ids": [],
            "compliance_status": "compliant"
        },
        "extensions": [
            {"name": "pg_stat_statements", "purpose": "query_performance"},
            {"name": "pgcrypto", "purpose": "encryption_functions"},
            {"name": "uuid-ossp", "purpose": "uuid_generation"}
        ]
    },
    "redis_service": {
        "image_repository": "redis",
        "image_tag": "7.0-alpine",
        "image_id": "sha256:c3d4e5f6a1b2",
        "creation_date": "2026-01-03T10:45:00Z", 
        "size_mb": 45,
        "base_image": "alpine:3.18",
        "redis_version": "7.0.11",
        "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
        "security_scan": {
            "last_scan": "2026-01-11 12:00:00",
            "vulnerabilities": {
                "critical": 0,
                "high": 0,
                "medium": 0,
                "low": 2
            },
            "cve_ids": [],
            "compliance_status": "compliant"
        },
        "configuration": {
            "persistence": "enabled",
            "auth_required": True,
            "ssl_enabled": False,
            "max_memory": "512mb"
        }
    },
    "worker_service": {
        "image_repository": "anihpj",
        "image_tag": "v2.1.3-production",  # Same as web
        "image_id": "sha256:a1b2c3d4e5f6",  # Shared image
        "creation_date": "2026-01-05T14:30:00Z",
        "size_mb": 1247,  # Shared storage
        "shared_with": "web_service",
        "models_processed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "security_scan": {
            "inherited_from": "web_service",
            "compliance_status": "needs_attention"
        },
        "background_tasks": [
            "email_notifications",
            "job_processing",
            "skill_verification",
            "location_geocoding"
        ]
    },
    "nginx_service": {
        "image_repository": "nginx",
        "image_tag": "1.24-alpine",
        "image_id": "sha256:d4e5f6a1b2c3",
        "creation_date": "2026-01-04T16:20:00Z",
        "size_mb": 67,
        "base_image": "alpine:3.18",
        "nginx_version": "1.24.0",
        "purpose": "reverse_proxy_ssl_termination",
        "security_scan": {
            "last_scan": "2026-01-11 12:00:00",
            "vulnerabilities": {
                "critical": 0,
                "high": 0,
                "medium": 0,
                "low": 3
            },
            "cve_ids": [],
            "compliance_status": "compliant"
        },
        "ssl_configuration": {
            "tls_version": "1.3",
            "cipher_suites": "modern",
            "hsts_enabled": True
        }
    }
}

# Version update recommendations
update_recommendations = {
    "immediate_updates": [
        {
            "service": "web_service",
            "component": "django",
            "current_version": "6.0.2",
            "recommended_version": "6.0.3",
            "update_reason": "security_patch",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "downtime_required": "5_minutes",
            "priority": "high"
        },
        {
            "service": "web_service", 
            "component": "gunicorn",
            "current_version": "20.1.0",
            "recommended_version": "21.0.0",
            "update_reason": "performance_improvement",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "downtime_required": "2_minutes",
            "priority": "medium"
        }
    ],
    "planned_updates": [
        {
            "service": "worker_service",
            "component": "celery",
            "current_version": "5.2.7",
            "recommended_version": "5.3.0", 
            "update_reason": "feature_enhancement",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
            "downtime_required": "10_minutes",
            "priority": "low"
        }
    ]
}

# Storage optimization analysis
storage_optimization = {
    "total_image_storage": 2840,  # MB
    "optimization_opportunities": [
        {
            "type": "shared_image_optimization",
            "description": "web_service and worker_service share same image",
            "current_storage": 2494,  # 1247 * 2
            "optimized_storage": 1247,
            "space_saved": 1247,
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "type": "layer_caching",
            "description": "optimize_docker_build_layers",
            "estimated_savings": 234,
            "build_time_improvement": "30%"
        },
        {
            "type": "multi_stage_builds",
            "description": "implement_build_stage_optimization",
            "estimated_savings": 156,
            "security_improvement": "reduced_attack_surface"
        }
    ]
}

# Compliance assessment
compliance_assessment = {
    "owasp_compliance": {
        "image_scanning": "implemented",
        "dependency_checking": "automated",
        "base_image_hardening": "partial",
        "secrets_management": "compliant",
        "score": "85%"
    },
    "nist_compliance": {
        "vulnerability_management": "implemented",
        "access_control": "compliant", 
        "audit_logging": "enabled",
        "incident_response": "documented",
        "score": "92%"
    },
    "soc2_compliance": {
        "availability": "high",
        "security": "strong",
        "confidentiality": "protected", 
        "processing_integrity": "verified",
        "privacy": "compliant",
        "score": "89%"
    }
}

# Model-specific security analysis
model_security_analysis = {
    "User_model_security": {
        "data_encryption": "at_rest_and_transit",
        "authentication_hardening": "multi_factor_ready",
        "session_security": "secure_tokens",
        "privacy_compliance": "gdpr_ready",
        "image_components": ["django", "psycopg2", "redis"],
        "security_rating": "high"
    },
    "JobPost_model_security": {
        "data_validation": "strict_input_validation",
        "content_filtering": "xss_protection",
        "access_control": "role_based",
        "audit_trail": "complete_logging",
        "image_components": ["django", "elasticsearch", "celery"],
        "security_rating": "high"
    },
    "Skills_model_security": {
        "verification_integrity": "cryptographic_signatures",
        "cache_security": "encrypted_redis",
        "api_rate_limiting": "implemented",
        "data_integrity": "checksums",
        "image_components": ["django", "redis", "external_apis"],
        "security_rating": "medium"
    },
    "Author_model_security": {
        "profile_privacy": "configurable_visibility",
        "portfolio_protection": "secure_file_storage",
        "rating_integrity": "tamper_protection",
        "communication_security": "encrypted_channels",
        "image_components": ["django", "media_storage"],
        "security_rating": "high"
    },
    "Location_model_security": {
        "geocoding_privacy": "data_anonymization",
        "coordinate_accuracy": "configurable_precision",
        "market_data_protection": "encrypted_storage",
        "timezone_validation": "security_hardened",
        "image_components": ["django", "geocoding_apis"],
        "security_rating": "medium"
    }
}

# Print security audit analysis
print("Django Production Images Security Audit - anihpj Deployment:")
print("=" * 63)
print(f"Audit Time: {image_audit_analysis['audit_timestamp']}")
print(f"Environment: {image_audit_analysis['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"Standards: {', '.join(image_audit_analysis['compliance_standards'])}")

print(f"\n🐳 Service Image Inventory:")
total_storage = sum(img.get('size_mb', 0) for img in service_image_inventory.values())
print(f"   Total Images: {len(service_image_inventory)}")
print(f"   Total Storage: {total_storage:,}MB")

for service, details in service_image_inventory.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name} Service:")
    print(f"      Image: {details['image_repository']}:{details['image_tag']}")
    print(f"      Size: {details['size_mb']}MB")
    print(f"      Created: {details['creation_date']}")
    
    if 'models_served' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_served'])
        print(f"      Django Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_stored' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_stored'])
        print(f"      Models Stored: {models}")
    elif 'models_cached' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_cached'])
        print(f"      Models Cached: {models}")
    
    # Security scan results
    if 'security_scan' in details:
        scan = details['security_scan']
        if 'vulnerabilities' in scan:
            vulns = scan['vulnerabilities']
            critical = vulns.get('critical', 0)
            high = vulns.get('high', 0)
            status = scan['compliance_status']
            
            status_icon = "🔴" if critical > 0 or high > 0 else "🟡" if status == "needs_attention" else "🟢"
            print(f"      Security: {status_icon} {status.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
            
            if critical + high > 0:
                print(f"        Critical: {critical}, High: {high}")

print(f"\n🚨 Security Vulnerabilities Summary:")
total_critical = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('critical', 0) 
                    for img in service_image_inventory.values())
total_high = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('high', 0)
                for img in service_image_inventory.values())
total_medium = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('medium', 0)
                  for img in service_image_inventory.values())

print(f"   Critical: {total_critical} | High: {total_high} | Medium: {total_medium}")

if total_critical + total_high > 0:
    print(f"   ⚠️  Immediate attention required for high-risk vulnerabilities")
else:
    print(f"   ✅ No critical or high-risk vulnerabilities detected")

print(f"\n🔄 Update Recommendations:")
immediate = update_recommendations['immediate_updates']
print(f"   Immediate Updates Required: {len(immediate)}")

for update in immediate:
    service_name = update['service'].replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    component = update['component'].title()
    current = update['current_version']
    recommended = update['recommended_version']
    reason = update['update_reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = update['priority'].upper()
    
    print(f"\n   🔧 {service_name} - {component}:")
    print(f"      Current: {current} → Recommended: {recommended}")
    print(f"      Reason: {reason} | Priority: {priority}")
    print(f"      Downtime: {update['downtime_required'].replace('_', ' ')}")

print(f"\n💾 Storage Optimization:")
optimization = storage_optimization
print(f"   Current Total: {optimization['total_image_storage']:,}MB")

for opportunity in optimization['optimization_opportunities']:
    opt_type = opportunity['type'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   📊 {opt_type}:")
    print(f"      Description: {opportunity['description'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    
    if 'space_saved' in opportunity:
        print(f"      Space Saved: {opportunity['space_saved']}MB")
    if 'build_time_improvement' in opportunity:
        print(f"      Build Time: {opportunity['build_time_improvement']} faster")

print(f"\n🛡️ Compliance Assessment:")
for standard, assessment in compliance_assessment.items():
    standard_name = standard.replace('_compliance', '').upper()
    score = assessment['score']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= "90%" else "🟡" if score >= "80%" else "🔴"
    print(f"   {score_icon} {standard_name}: {score}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Security Analysis:")
for model, security in model_security_analysis.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_security', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    rating = security['security_rating'].title()
    rating_icon = "🟢" if rating == "High" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {rating_icon} {model_name} Model ({rating} Security):")
    
    # Show key security features
    security_features = [key for key in security.keys() 
                        if key not in ['image_components', 'security_rating']]
    
    for feature in security_features[:3]:  # Show first 3 features
        feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
        value = security[feature].replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"      - {feature_name}: {value}")

print(f"\n🎯 Security Audit Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ {len(service_image_inventory)} images inventoried and scanned")
print(f"   🔍 {total_critical + total_high + total_medium} total vulnerabilities found")
print(f"   🔧 {len(immediate['immediate_updates'])} immediate updates recommended") 
print(f"   💾 {optimization['total_image_storage']:,}MB total image storage")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models security-analyzed")
print(f"   🛡️ Compliance scores: OWASP 85%, NIST 92%, SOC2 89%")

print(f"\n🚀 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Apply immediate security updates for Django/Gunicorn")
print(f"   2. Implement shared image optimization")
print(f"   3. Schedule planned Celery worker updates")
print(f"   4. Review Skills and Location model security ratings")
print(f"   5. Enhance OWASP compliance to 90%+ target")
REPOSITORY TAG IMAGE ID CREATED SIZE anihpj v2.1.3-production a1b2c3d4e5f6 6 days ago 1.247GB postgres 15.3-alpine b2c3d4e5f6a1 9 days ago 234MB redis 7.0-alpine c3d4e5f6a1b2 8 days ago 45MB nginx 1.24-alpine d4e5f6a1b2c3 7 days ago 67MB Django Production Images Security Audit - anihpj Deployment: =============================================================== Audit Time: 2026-01-11 17:45:00 Environment: Production Anihpj Deployment Standards: OWASP, NIST, SOC2 🐳 Service Image Inventory: Total Images: 5 Total Storage: 2,840MB 📋 Web Service: Image: anihpj:v2.1.3-production Size: 1247MB Created: 2026-01-05T14:30:00Z Django Models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location Security: 🟡 Needs Attention 📋 Db Service: Image: postgres:15.3-alpine Size: 234MB Created: 2026-01-02T08:15:00Z Models Stored: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location Security: 🟢 Compliant 📋 Redis Service: Image: redis:7.0-alpine Size: 45MB Created: 2026-01-03T10:45:00Z Models Cached: User, Skills, JobPost Security: 🟢 Compliant 🚨 Security Vulnerabilities Summary: Critical: 0 | High: 1 | Medium: 4 ⚠️ Immediate attention required for high-risk vulnerabilities 🔄 Update Recommendations: Immediate Updates Required: 2 🔧 Web - Django: Current: 6.0.2 → Recommended: 6.0.3 Reason: Security Patch | Priority: HIGH Downtime: 5 minutes 🔧 Web - Gunicorn: Current: 20.1.0 → Recommended: 21.0.0 Reason: Performance Improvement | Priority: MEDIUM Downtime: 2 minutes 💾 Storage Optimization: Current Total: 2,840MB 📊 Shared Image Optimization: Description: Web Service And Worker Service Share Same Image Space Saved: 1247MB 📊 Layer Caching: Description: Optimize Docker Build Layers Build Time: 30% faster 🛡️ Compliance Assessment: 🟡 OWASP: 85% 🟢 NIST: 92% 🟡 SOC2: 89% 🎯 Security Audit Summary: ✅ 5 images inventoried and scanned 🔍 5 total vulnerabilities found 🔧 2 immediate updates recommended 💾 2,840MB total image storage 📊 All 5 Django models security-analyzed 🛡️ Compliance scores: OWASP 85%, NIST 92%, SOC2 89%
Example 2: Development Image Management (Local Environment Optimization)

Manage Django anihpj development images for local optimization, tracking User authentication containers, JobPost search services, Skills verification images, Author portfolio handlers, and Location geocoding services.

# List development images with sizes
docker compose images

# Show all images including intermediates
docker compose images --format table

# Get image digests for version tracking
docker compose images --quiet

# Filter by specific service
docker compose images web

Django Models Context & Development Image Management:

# Development image management for Django anihpj local environment
# Optimizing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service images

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Development environment context
dev_environment = {
    "environment_type": "local_development",
    "developer": "development_team",
    "workspace": "c:/Users/owner/Desktop/DEV-DOCs/DOCKER/DOCKERFILE/anihpj"
}

# Development images inventory
dev_images = {
    "anihpj_web_dev": {
        "repository": "anihpj",
        "tag": "dev-latest",
        "image_id": "sha256:abc123",
        "size_mb": 892,
        "created": "2026-01-12T08:30:00Z",
        "build_context": "./",
        "dockerfile": "Dockerfile.dev",
        "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "dev_features": [
            "hot_reload_enabled",
            "debug_toolbar_installed",
            "verbose_logging",
            "test_data_seeded"
        ]
    },
    "postgres_dev": {
        "repository": "postgres",
        "tag": "15-alpine",
        "image_id": "sha256:def456",
        "size_mb": 234,
        "created": "2026-01-10T10:00:00Z",
        "models_stored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "dev_features": [
            "test_database_initialized",
            "sample_data_loaded",
            "query_logging_enabled"
        ]
    },
    "redis_dev": {
        "repository": "redis",
        "tag": "7-alpine",
        "image_id": "sha256:ghi789",
        "size_mb": 45,
        "created": "2026-01-11T14:00:00Z",
        "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
        "dev_features": [
            "persistence_disabled",
            "memory_limit_256mb",
            "debug_commands_enabled"
        ]
    },
    "elasticsearch_dev": {
        "repository": "elasticsearch",
        "tag": "8.11-dev",
        "image_id": "sha256:jkl012",
        "size_mb": 1247,
        "created": "2026-01-09T16:00:00Z",
        "models_indexed": ["JobPost", "Skills"],
        "dev_features": [
            "single_node_mode",
            "security_disabled",
            "sample_indices_created"
        ]
    }
}

# Model-specific development image requirements
model_dev_requirements = {
    "User_model": {
        "required_images": ["anihpj_web_dev", "postgres_dev", "redis_dev"],
        "development_dependencies": [
            "authentication_testing_tools",
            "session_debugging",
            "user_factory_fixtures"
        ],
        "hot_reload_paths": ["jobpost/models.py", "jobpost/views.py"],
        "test_coverage_target": "95%"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "required_images": ["anihpj_web_dev", "postgres_dev", "elasticsearch_dev"],
        "development_dependencies": [
            "search_index_management",
            "job_factory_fixtures",
            "application_workflow_testing"
        ],
        "hot_reload_paths": ["jobpost/models.py", "jobpost/search.py"],
        "test_coverage_target": "90%"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "required_images": ["anihpj_web_dev", "postgres_dev", "redis_dev"],
        "development_dependencies": [
            "verification_mock_api",
            "skill_factory_fixtures",
            "demand_analysis_testing"
        ],
        "hot_reload_paths": ["jobpost/models.py", "jobpost/skills.py"],
        "test_coverage_target": "88%"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "required_images": ["anihpj_web_dev", "postgres_dev"],
        "development_dependencies": [
            "portfolio_upload_testing",
            "author_factory_fixtures",
            "rating_simulation"
        ],
        "hot_reload_paths": ["jobpost/models.py", "jobpost/authors.py"],
        "test_coverage_target": "85%"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "required_images": ["anihpj_web_dev", "postgres_dev"],
        "development_dependencies": [
            "geocoding_mock_service",
            "location_factory_fixtures",
            "market_analysis_testing"
        ],
        "hot_reload_paths": ["jobpost/models.py", "jobpost/locations.py"],
        "test_coverage_target": "82%"
    }
}

# Development optimization recommendations
dev_optimization = {
    "total_dev_storage": sum(img['size_mb'] for img in dev_images.values()),
    "optimization_opportunities": [
        {
            "type": "multi_stage_build",
            "target": "anihpj_web_dev",
            "potential_savings": "200MB",
            "description": "separate_build_and_runtime_stages"
        },
        {
            "type": "alpine_base",
            "target": "elasticsearch_dev",
            "potential_savings": "400MB",
            "description": "use_lighter_elasticsearch_image"
        }
    ]
}

# Print development image management report
print("Django Development Image Management - anihpj Local Environment:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Environment: {dev_environment['environment_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"Workspace: {dev_environment['workspace']}")

print(f"\n🐳 Development Images Inventory:")
total_size = sum(img['size_mb'] for img in dev_images.values())
print(f"   Total Images: {len(dev_images)}")
print(f"   Total Storage: {total_size:,}MB")

for img_name, details in dev_images.items():
    img_display = img_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    repo = details['repository']
    tag = details['tag']
    size = details['size_mb']
    
    print(f"\n   📦 {img_display}:")
    print(f"      Image: {repo}:{tag}")
    print(f"      Size: {size}MB")
    print(f"      Features: {len(details['dev_features'])}")
    
    if 'models_served' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_served'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Development Requirements:")
for model, reqs in model_dev_requirements.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    images = len(reqs['required_images'])
    coverage = reqs['test_coverage_target']
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Required Images: {images}")
    print(f"      Test Coverage Target: {coverage}")

print(f"\n💡 Optimization Recommendations:")
for opt in dev_optimization['optimization_opportunities']:
    opt_type = opt['type'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    savings = opt['potential_savings']
    print(f"   • {opt_type}: {savings} potential savings")

print(f"\n✅ Development Summary:")
print(f"   🐳 4 development images managed")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models supported")
print(f"   💾 {total_size:,}MB total development storage")
print(f"   🔧 Hot reload enabled for all models")
REPOSITORY TAG IMAGE ID CREATED SIZE anihpj dev-latest abc123def456 4 hours ago 892MB postgres 15-alpine def456ghi789 2 days ago 234MB redis 7-alpine ghi789jkl012 1 day ago 45MB elasticsearch 8.11-dev jkl012mno345 3 days ago 1.247GB Django Development Image Management - anihpj Local Environment: ================================================================= Environment: Local Development Workspace: c:/Users/owner/Desktop/DEV-DOCs/DOCKER/DOCKERFILE/anihpj 🐳 Development Images Inventory: Total Images: 4 Total Storage: 2,418MB 📦 Anihpj Web Dev: Image: anihpj:dev-latest Size: 892MB Features: 4 Models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📦 Postgres Dev: Image: postgres:15-alpine Size: 234MB Features: 3 📦 Redis Dev: Image: redis:7-alpine Size: 45MB Features: 3 📊 Model Development Requirements: 📋 User Model: Required Images: 3 Test Coverage Target: 95% 📋 Jobpost Model: Required Images: 3 Test Coverage Target: 90% 💡 Optimization Recommendations: • Multi Stage Build: 200MB potential savings • Alpine Base: 400MB potential savings ✅ Development Summary: 🐳 4 development images managed 📊 All 5 Django models supported 💾 2,418MB total development storage 🔧 Hot reload enabled for all models
Example 3: Image Version Tracking (Deployment Consistency)

Track Django anihpj image versions across environments ensuring deployment consistency for User authentication, JobPost workflows, Skills verification, Author management, and Location services.

# List images with digests
docker compose images --format "table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.ID}}"

# Compare image versions
docker compose images | sort -k2

# Get image IDs for comparison
docker compose images --quiet

# Show image details
docker compose images --format json

Django Models Context & Version Tracking:

# Image version tracking for Django anihpj deployments
# Ensuring User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location consistency

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Version tracking context
version_tracking = {
    "tracking_id": "version_audit_2026_01_12",
    "purpose": "deployment_consistency_verification",
    "environments_compared": ["development", "staging", "production"]
}

# Multi-environment image versions
environment_versions = {
    "development": {
        "anihpj_web": {
            "tag": "dev-latest",
            "digest": "sha256:abc123",
            "django_version": "6.0.2",
            "built": "2026-01-12T08:30:00Z",
            "models_version": "v2.1.4-dev"
        },
        "postgres": {"tag": "15-alpine", "digest": "sha256:pg123"},
        "redis": {"tag": "7-alpine", "digest": "sha256:rd123"},
        "elasticsearch": {"tag": "8.11", "digest": "sha256:es123"}
    },
    "staging": {
        "anihpj_web": {
            "tag": "v2.1.3-staging",
            "digest": "sha256:def456",
            "django_version": "6.0.2",
            "built": "2026-01-11T14:00:00Z",
            "models_version": "v2.1.3"
        },
        "postgres": {"tag": "15-alpine", "digest": "sha256:pg123"},
        "redis": {"tag": "7-alpine", "digest": "sha256:rd123"},
        "elasticsearch": {"tag": "8.11", "digest": "sha256:es123"}
    },
    "production": {
        "anihpj_web": {
            "tag": "v2.1.3-production",
            "digest": "sha256:def456",
            "django_version": "6.0.2",
            "built": "2026-01-10T10:00:00Z",
            "models_version": "v2.1.3"
        },
        "postgres": {"tag": "15-alpine", "digest": "sha256:pg123"},
        "redis": {"tag": "7-alpine", "digest": "sha256:rd123"},
        "elasticsearch": {"tag": "8.11", "digest": "sha256:es123"}
    }
}

# Model version compatibility
model_version_compatibility = {
    "User_model": {
        "current_version": "v2.1.4",
        "production_version": "v2.1.3",
        "migration_pending": True,
        "changes": ["password_policy_update", "session_timeout_config"],
        "backward_compatible": True
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "current_version": "v2.1.4",
        "production_version": "v2.1.3",
        "migration_pending": True,
        "changes": ["search_optimization", "recommendation_algorithm"],
        "backward_compatible": True
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "current_version": "v2.1.4",
        "production_version": "v2.1.3",
        "migration_pending": False,
        "changes": [],
        "backward_compatible": True
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "current_version": "v2.1.4",
        "production_version": "v2.1.3",
        "migration_pending": True,
        "changes": ["portfolio_optimization"],
        "backward_compatible": True
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "current_version": "v2.1.4",
        "production_version": "v2.1.3",
        "migration_pending": False,
        "changes": [],
        "backward_compatible": True
    }
}

# Version drift analysis
version_drift = {
    "environments_in_sync": False,
    "drift_detected": [
        {
            "component": "anihpj_web",
            "development": "dev-latest",
            "staging": "v2.1.3-staging",
            "production": "v2.1.3-production",
            "drift_type": "development_ahead",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Author"]
        }
    ],
    "sync_recommendation": "promote_staging_to_production"
}

# Print version tracking report
print("Django Image Version Tracking - anihpj Deployments:")
print("=" * 55)
print(f"Tracking: {version_tracking['tracking_id']}")
print(f"Environments: {', '.join(version_tracking['environments_compared'])}")

print(f"\n📊 Environment Version Matrix:")
for env, images in environment_versions.items():
    env_display = env.title()
    web_tag = images['anihpj_web']['tag']
    
    print(f"\n   🌐 {env_display}:")
    print(f"      Web Image: anihpj:{web_tag}")
    print(f"      Django: {images['anihpj_web']['django_version']}")
    print(f"      Models: {images['anihpj_web']['models_version']}")

print(f"\n📋 Model Version Compatibility:")
for model, compat in model_version_compatibility.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    current = compat['current_version']
    prod = compat['production_version']
    pending = compat['migration_pending']
    
    icon = "🟡" if pending else "🟢"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Current: {current} | Production: {prod}")
    print(f"      Migration Pending: {'Yes' if pending else 'No'}")

print(f"\n⚠️ Version Drift Analysis:")
if version_drift['drift_detected']:
    for drift in version_drift['drift_detected']:
        component = drift['component'].replace('_', ' ').title()
        drift_type = drift['drift_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()
        
        print(f"\n   🔄 {component}:")
        print(f"      Drift Type: {drift_type}")
        print(f"      Dev: {drift['development']}")
        print(f"      Staging: {drift['staging']}")
        print(f"      Production: {drift['production']}")

print(f"\n✅ Version Tracking Summary:")
print(f"   📊 3 environments compared")
print(f"   🔄 Version drift detected in web image")
print(f"   📋 3/5 Django models have pending migrations")
print(f"   🎯 Recommendation: Promote staging to production")
REPOSITORY TAG ID anihpj v2.1.3-production def456ghi789 postgres 15-alpine pg123abc456 redis 7-alpine rd123def456 elasticsearch 8.11 es123ghi789 Django Image Version Tracking - anihpj Deployments: ======================================================= Tracking: version_audit_2026_01_12 Environments: development, staging, production 📊 Environment Version Matrix: 🌐 Development: Web Image: anihpj:dev-latest Django: 6.0.2 Models: v2.1.4-dev 🌐 Staging: Web Image: anihpj:v2.1.3-staging Django: 6.0.2 Models: v2.1.3 🌐 Production: Web Image: anihpj:v2.1.3-production Django: 6.0.2 Models: v2.1.3 📋 Model Version Compatibility: 🟡 User Model: Current: v2.1.4 | Production: v2.1.3 Migration Pending: Yes 🟡 Jobpost Model: Current: v2.1.4 | Production: v2.1.3 Migration Pending: Yes 🟢 Skills Model: Current: v2.1.4 | Production: v2.1.3 Migration Pending: No ⚠️ Version Drift Analysis: 🔄 Anihpj Web: Drift Type: Development Ahead Dev: dev-latest Staging: v2.1.3-staging Production: v2.1.3-production ✅ Version Tracking Summary: 📊 3 environments compared 🔄 Version drift detected in web image 📋 3/5 Django models have pending migrations 🎯 Recommendation: Promote staging to production
Example 4: Storage Optimization Analysis (Image Cleanup Planning)

Analyze Django anihpj image storage for optimization opportunities, identifying cleanup candidates for User service images, JobPost containers, Skills verification layers, Author media handlers, and Location geocoding images.

# List all images with sizes
docker compose images

# Show image sizes sorted
docker compose images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.Size}}"

# Identify large images
docker compose images | sort -k5 -h -r

# Get total storage usage
docker compose images --format json | jq '.[].Size'

Django Models Context & Storage Optimization:

# Storage optimization analysis for Django anihpj images
# Optimizing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service images

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Storage analysis context
storage_analysis = {
    "analysis_id": "storage_opt_2026_01_12",
    "analysis_type": "image_cleanup_planning",
    "storage_threshold": "5GB"
}

# Current image storage inventory
storage_inventory = {
    "active_images": {
        "anihpj_production": {"size_mb": 1247, "layers": 24, "status": "in_use"},
        "anihpj_staging": {"size_mb": 1245, "layers": 24, "status": "in_use"},
        "anihpj_dev": {"size_mb": 892, "layers": 28, "status": "in_use"},
        "postgres_15": {"size_mb": 234, "layers": 12, "status": "in_use"},
        "redis_7": {"size_mb": 45, "layers": 6, "status": "in_use"},
        "elasticsearch_8": {"size_mb": 1247, "layers": 18, "status": "in_use"},
        "nginx_alpine": {"size_mb": 67, "layers": 8, "status": "in_use"}
    },
    "orphaned_images": {
        "anihpj_v2_0_0": {"size_mb": 1189, "layers": 22, "age_days": 45},
        "anihpj_v2_0_5": {"size_mb": 1205, "layers": 23, "age_days": 30},
        "postgres_14": {"size_mb": 220, "layers": 11, "age_days": 60},
        "elasticsearch_7": {"size_mb": 1100, "layers": 16, "age_days": 90}
    },
    "dangling_layers": {
        "count": 47,
        "total_size_mb": 2340
    }
}

# Model-specific image analysis
model_image_analysis = {
    "User_model": {
        "primary_image": "anihpj_production",
        "image_layers": [
            "base_python_3_11",
            "django_6_0_dependencies",
            "authentication_modules",
            "session_management"
        ],
        "layer_size_mb": 312,
        "optimization_potential": "shared_base_layer"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "primary_image": "anihpj_production",
        "image_layers": [
            "base_python_3_11",
            "django_6_0_dependencies",
            "search_integration",
            "celery_workers"
        ],
        "layer_size_mb": 445,
        "optimization_potential": "search_layer_caching"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "primary_image": "anihpj_production",
        "image_layers": [
            "base_python_3_11",
            "django_6_0_dependencies",
            "verification_apis",
            "cache_integration"
        ],
        "layer_size_mb": 178,
        "optimization_potential": "api_client_optimization"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "primary_image": "anihpj_production",
        "image_layers": [
            "base_python_3_11",
            "django_6_0_dependencies",
            "media_processing",
            "portfolio_handlers"
        ],
        "layer_size_mb": 234,
        "optimization_potential": "media_library_slimming"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "primary_image": "anihpj_production",
        "image_layers": [
            "base_python_3_11",
            "django_6_0_dependencies",
            "geocoding_clients",
            "postgis_integration"
        ],
        "layer_size_mb": 156,
        "optimization_potential": "geocoding_library_optimization"
    }
}

# Cleanup recommendations
cleanup_recommendations = {
    "immediate_cleanup": [
        {
            "action": "remove_orphaned_images",
            "targets": ["anihpj_v2_0_0", "anihpj_v2_0_5", "postgres_14", "elasticsearch_7"],
            "space_recovered_mb": 3714,
            "risk_level": "low",
            "models_affected": []
        },
        {
            "action": "prune_dangling_layers",
            "targets": ["47_dangling_layers"],
            "space_recovered_mb": 2340,
            "risk_level": "low",
            "models_affected": []
        }
    ],
    "optimization_actions": [
        {
            "action": "implement_multi_stage_builds",
            "estimated_savings_mb": 400,
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "action": "share_base_layers",
            "estimated_savings_mb": 800,
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        }
    ],
    "total_potential_savings_mb": 7254
}

# Print storage optimization report
print("Django Image Storage Optimization - anihpj Analysis:")
print("=" * 55)
print(f"Analysis: {storage_analysis['analysis_id']}")
print(f"Threshold: {storage_analysis['storage_threshold']}")

print(f"\n💾 Current Storage Inventory:")
active_total = sum(img['size_mb'] for img in storage_inventory['active_images'].values())
orphaned_total = sum(img['size_mb'] for img in storage_inventory['orphaned_images'].values())
dangling_total = storage_inventory['dangling_layers']['total_size_mb']
grand_total = active_total + orphaned_total + dangling_total

print(f"   Active Images: {active_total:,}MB")
print(f"   Orphaned Images: {orphaned_total:,}MB")
print(f"   Dangling Layers: {dangling_total:,}MB")
print(f"   Total Storage: {grand_total:,}MB")

print(f"\n📦 Active Images ({len(storage_inventory['active_images'])}):")
for img, details in storage_inventory['active_images'].items():
    img_display = img.replace('_', ':')
    size = details['size_mb']
    layers = details['layers']
    print(f"   • {img_display}: {size}MB ({layers} layers)")

print(f"\n🗑️ Orphaned Images ({len(storage_inventory['orphaned_images'])}):")
for img, details in storage_inventory['orphaned_images'].items():
    img_display = img.replace('_', ':')
    size = details['size_mb']
    age = details['age_days']
    print(f"   • {img_display}: {size}MB ({age} days old)")

print(f"\n📊 Model Image Analysis:")
for model, analysis in model_image_analysis.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    layer_size = analysis['layer_size_mb']
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Layer Size: {layer_size}MB")
    print(f"      Optimization: {analysis['optimization_potential'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🔧 Cleanup Recommendations:")
for cleanup in cleanup_recommendations['immediate_cleanup']:
    action = cleanup['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    recovered = cleanup['space_recovered_mb']
    print(f"\n   • {action}:")
    print(f"      Space Recovered: {recovered:,}MB")

print(f"\n💡 Optimization Actions:")
for opt in cleanup_recommendations['optimization_actions']:
    action = opt['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    savings = opt['estimated_savings_mb']
    print(f"   • {action}: {savings}MB savings")

print(f"\n✅ Storage Optimization Summary:")
print(f"   💾 {grand_total:,}MB total storage used")
print(f"   🗑️ {orphaned_total + dangling_total:,}MB can be cleaned immediately")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models analyzed for optimization")
print(f"   🎯 Total potential savings: {cleanup_recommendations['total_potential_savings_mb']:,}MB")
