Example 5: Registry Synchronization (Image Distribution)

Manage Django anihpj image distribution to container registries, ensuring User authentication, JobPost search, Skills verification, Author profiles, and Location services are available across deployment targets.

# List local images for push
docker compose images

# Check image tags for registry
docker compose images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}"

# Verify image availability
docker compose images --quiet

# Get image digests for registry comparison
docker compose images --format json | jq '.[].ID'

Django Models Context & Registry Distribution:

# Registry synchronization for Django anihpj images
# Distributing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service images

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Registry sync context
registry_context = {
    "sync_id": "registry_sync_2026_01_12",
    "source": "local_build",
    "target_registries": ["docker_hub", "aws_ecr", "azure_acr"]
}

# Local images for distribution
local_images = {
    "anihpj_web": {
        "local_tag": "anihpj:v2.1.4-production",
        "local_digest": "sha256:abc123def456",
        "size_mb": 1247,
        "models_included": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "build_date": "2026-01-12T10:00:00Z"
    },
    "anihpj_worker": {
        "local_tag": "anihpj-worker:v2.1.4",
        "local_digest": "sha256:def456ghi789",
        "size_mb": 1189,
        "models_included": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
        "build_date": "2026-01-12T10:15:00Z"
    },
    "anihpj_nginx": {
        "local_tag": "anihpj-nginx:v2.1.4",
        "local_digest": "sha256:ghi789jkl012",
        "size_mb": 67,
        "models_included": [],
        "build_date": "2026-01-12T10:20:00Z"
    }
}

# Registry synchronization status
registry_sync_status = {
    "docker_hub": {
        "registry_url": "docker.io/anihpj",
        "sync_status": "synchronized",
        "images_synced": 3,
        "last_sync": "2026-01-12T11:00:00Z",
        "bandwidth_used_mb": 2503
    },
    "aws_ecr": {
        "registry_url": "123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj",
        "sync_status": "pending",
        "images_synced": 2,
        "last_sync": "2026-01-11T14:00:00Z",
        "bandwidth_used_mb": 1892
    },
    "azure_acr": {
        "registry_url": "anihpj.azurecr.io",
        "sync_status": "synchronized",
        "images_synced": 3,
        "last_sync": "2026-01-12T10:45:00Z",
        "bandwidth_used_mb": 2503
    }
}

# Model distribution requirements
model_distribution_requirements = {
    "User_model": {
        "required_registries": ["docker_hub", "aws_ecr", "azure_acr"],
        "deployment_regions": ["us_east", "eu_west", "ap_southeast"],
        "replication_priority": "critical",
        "max_sync_delay": "15_minutes"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "required_registries": ["docker_hub", "aws_ecr", "azure_acr"],
        "deployment_regions": ["us_east", "eu_west", "ap_southeast"],
        "replication_priority": "critical",
        "max_sync_delay": "15_minutes"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "required_registries": ["docker_hub", "aws_ecr"],
        "deployment_regions": ["us_east", "eu_west"],
        "replication_priority": "high",
        "max_sync_delay": "30_minutes"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "required_registries": ["docker_hub", "azure_acr"],
        "deployment_regions": ["us_east", "eu_west"],
        "replication_priority": "medium",
        "max_sync_delay": "60_minutes"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "required_registries": ["docker_hub", "aws_ecr"],
        "deployment_regions": ["us_east", "ap_southeast"],
        "replication_priority": "medium",
        "max_sync_delay": "60_minutes"
    }
}

# Sync actions needed
sync_actions = {
    "pending_syncs": [
        {
            "image": "anihpj_web",
            "target_registry": "aws_ecr",
            "action": "push_latest_version",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "estimated_time": "5_minutes"
        }
    ],
    "verification_needed": [
        {
            "image": "anihpj_worker",
            "registry": "azure_acr",
            "verification": "digest_comparison",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"]
        }
    ]
}

# Print registry sync report
print("Django Registry Synchronization - anihpj Distribution:")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"Sync ID: {registry_context['sync_id']}")
print(f"Registries: {', '.join(registry_context['target_registries'])}")

print(f"\n📦 Local Images for Distribution:")
for img_name, details in local_images.items():
    img_display = img_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    tag = details['local_tag']
    size = details['size_mb']
    
    print(f"\n   🐳 {img_display}:")
    print(f"      Tag: {tag}")
    print(f"      Size: {size}MB")
    
    if details['models_included']:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_included'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n🌐 Registry Synchronization Status:")
for registry, status in registry_sync_status.items():
    registry_display = registry.replace('_', ' ').title()
    sync_status = status['sync_status'].title()
    images = status['images_synced']
    
    icon = "🟢" if sync_status == "Synchronized" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {registry_display}:")
    print(f"      Status: {sync_status}")
    print(f"      Images Synced: {images}")
    print(f"      Bandwidth: {status['bandwidth_used_mb']}MB")

print(f"\n📊 Model Distribution Requirements:")
for model, reqs in model_distribution_requirements.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    registries = len(reqs['required_registries'])
    regions = len(reqs['deployment_regions'])
    priority = reqs['replication_priority'].title()
    
    priority_icon = "🔴" if priority == "Critical" else "🟡" if priority == "High" else "🟢"
    
    print(f"\n   {priority_icon} {model_name} Model ({priority}):")
    print(f"      Registries: {registries}")
    print(f"      Regions: {regions}")

print(f"\n🔄 Pending Sync Actions:")
for action in sync_actions['pending_syncs']:
    image = action['image'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    target = action['target_registry'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_est = action['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    
    print(f"\n   📤 {image} → {target}:")
    print(f"      Action: {action['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Time: {time_est}")

print(f"\n✅ Registry Sync Summary:")
print(f"   🐳 3 images ready for distribution")
print(f"   🌐 3 registries configured")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models distribution-mapped")
print(f"   🔄 1 pending sync action to complete")
REPOSITORY TAG IMAGE ID SIZE anihpj v2.1.4-production abc123def456 1.247GB anihpj-worker v2.1.4 def456ghi789 1.189GB anihpj-nginx v2.1.4 ghi789jkl012 67MB Django Registry Synchronization - anihpj Distribution: ========================================================== Sync ID: registry_sync_2026_01_12 Registries: docker_hub, aws_ecr, azure_acr 📦 Local Images for Distribution: 🐳 Anihpj Web: Tag: anihpj:v2.1.4-production Size: 1247MB Models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 🐳 Anihpj Worker: Tag: anihpj-worker:v2.1.4 Size: 1189MB Models: User, JobPost, Skills 🐳 Anihpj Nginx: Tag: anihpj-nginx:v2.1.4 Size: 67MB 🌐 Registry Synchronization Status: 🟢 Docker Hub: Status: Synchronized Images Synced: 3 Bandwidth: 2503MB 🟡 Aws Ecr: Status: Pending Images Synced: 2 Bandwidth: 1892MB 🟢 Azure Acr: Status: Synchronized Images Synced: 3 Bandwidth: 2503MB 📊 Model Distribution Requirements: 🔴 User Model (Critical): Registries: 3 Regions: 3 🔴 Jobpost Model (Critical): Registries: 3 Regions: 3 🔄 Pending Sync Actions: 📤 Anihpj Web → Aws Ecr: Action: Push Latest Version Time: 5 minutes ✅ Registry Sync Summary: 🐳 3 images ready for distribution 🌐 3 registries configured 📊 All 5 Django models distribution-mapped 🔄 1 pending sync action to complete

docker compose version

Official Docs: docker compose version | Docker CLI: docker compose version

Display Docker Compose version information for Django environment compatibility verification.

Full Description

The docker compose version command displays version information for Docker Compose, including the compose version, build details, and platform information. This is essential for troubleshooting, compatibility verification, and ensuring your anihpj Django development environment uses compatible tooling.

For Django applications, this command helps verify Docker Compose compatibility with your anihpj jobpost application requirements, troubleshoot version-specific issues, and ensure consistent deployment environments across development, staging, and production systems.

Syntax

# Show version information
docker compose version

# Show short version only
docker compose version --short

# Output in JSON format
docker compose version --format json

# Show version with build details
docker compose --version

# Check version compatibility
docker compose version && docker --version

# Version for automation scripts
docker compose version --short --format json

Parameters Description

  • --short: Display only Django Compose version number without additional details. Compact output for scripts.
  • --format json: Output Django Compose version information in JSON format. Machine-readable format for automation.
  • --version: Alternative Django Compose version display format with build information included.
  • Combined Commands: Chain version checks with Docker engine version for complete Django environment verification.
  • Script Integration: Use version output in Django deployment scripts for compatibility verification.

Attributes

  • Version Verification: Displays Django Compose version for compatibility checking with compose file syntax and features.
  • Build Information: Shows Django Compose build details including commit hash and build date for debugging.
  • Platform Details: Indicates Django development platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) for environment-specific troubleshooting.
  • Compatibility Checking: Enables Django deployment verification across different development and production environments.
  • Troubleshooting Support: Provides Django environment information essential for issue diagnosis and support requests.

Methods

Environment Verification

Syntax: docker compose version

Parameters: complete Django environment version information display

Verify Django development environment Docker Compose version for feature compatibility and troubleshooting.

Deployment Validation

Syntax: docker compose version --format json

Parameters: JSON format for Django deployment script automation and validation

Automate Django deployment version checking in CI/CD pipelines and deployment scripts.

Return Value

Returns Docker Compose version information including version number, build details, and platform information for Django environment.

  • Success (0): Version information displayed successfully
  • Command Error (1): Docker Compose not found or installation issue
  • Format Error (2): Invalid output format specified

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Environment Compatibility Audit (Multi-Platform Django Development)

Verify Docker Compose version compatibility across Django development team environments for User authentication, JobPost management, Skills verification, and Location services with consistent deployment capabilities.

# Check current Compose version
docker compose version

# Verify Docker engine compatibility
docker --version

# Get JSON version data for automation
docker compose version --format json

# Short version for quick checks
docker compose version --short

Django Models Context & Environment Compatibility:

# Environment compatibility audit for Django anihpj development
# Verifying User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model deployment consistency

import json
import subprocess
import platform
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Environment compatibility audit
environment_audit = {
    "audit_timestamp": "2026-01-12 09:15:00",
    "audit_purpose": "multi_platform_django_development_consistency",
    "project_name": "anihpj_jobpost_application"
}

# Team development environments
development_environments = {
    "dev_lead_workstation": {
        "developer": "Sarah Chen",
        "platform": "Windows 11 Pro",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.3",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.7",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
        "environment_status": "primary_development",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-10",
        "compatibility_score": 100
    },
    "backend_developer": {
        "developer": "Alex Rodriguez", 
        "platform": "macOS Ventura 13.6",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.21.0",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.5",
        "python_version": "3.11.4",
        "django_version": "6.0.1",
        "models_developed": ["Author", "Location", "Skills"],
        "environment_status": "feature_development",
        "last_updated": "2025-12-28",
        "compatibility_score": 85
    },
    "frontend_developer": {
        "developer": "Maria Santos",
        "platform": "Ubuntu 22.04 LTS",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.20.2", 
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.4",
        "python_version": "3.11.3",
        "django_version": "5.2.8",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "api_integration",
        "last_updated": "2025-12-15",
        "compatibility_score": 70
    },
    "qa_engineer": {
        "developer": "James Wilson",
        "platform": "Windows 10 Pro",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.1",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.6",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "testing_environment",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-08",
        "compatibility_score": 95
    },
    "production_server": {
        "developer": "Production Team",
        "platform": "Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Server",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.3",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.7",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_deployed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "production_deployment",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-11",
        "compatibility_score": 100
    }
}

# Version compatibility matrix
compatibility_matrix = {
    "docker_compose_requirements": {
        "minimum_version": "v2.20.0",
        "recommended_version": "v2.23.3",
        "features_required": [
            "compose_file_v3_8_support",
            "build_context_support",
            "secret_management",
            "profile_support",
            "health_check_support"
        ],
        "django_features_enabled": [
            "multi_service_orchestration",
            "database_container_management", 
            "redis_cache_integration",
            "worker_service_coordination",
            "nginx_reverse_proxy"
        ]
    },
    "django_version_compatibility": {
        "target_version": "6.0.2",
        "minimum_supported": "5.2.0",
        "model_migration_compatibility": {
            "User": "all_versions_compatible",
            "JobPost": "django_6_0_optimized", 
            "Skills": "requires_django_6_0",
            "Author": "all_versions_compatible",
            "Location": "django_6_0_geospatial_features"
        },
        "api_compatibility": {
            "rest_framework": "3.14.0",
            "authentication": "session_and_token",
            "serialization": "json_and_xml",
            "pagination": "cursor_and_page"
        }
    }
}

# Environment compatibility analysis
compatibility_analysis = {
    "version_inconsistencies": [
        {
            "environment": "backend_developer",
            "issue": "outdated_docker_compose",
            "current": "v2.21.0",
            "required": "v2.23.3",
            "impact": "missing_profile_support",
            "models_affected": ["Author", "Location", "Skills"],
            "resolution": "update_docker_compose"
        },
        {
            "environment": "frontend_developer",
            "issue": "outdated_django_version",
            "current": "5.2.8",
            "required": "6.0.2",
            "impact": "model_compatibility_issues",
            "models_affected": ["Skills", "Location"],
            "resolution": "upgrade_django_update_models"
        },
        {
            "environment": "frontend_developer",
            "issue": "compose_feature_limitations",
            "current": "v2.20.2",
            "required": "v2.23.3",
            "impact": "deployment_script_failures",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Location"],
            "resolution": "update_compose_and_scripts"
        }
    ],
    "feature_compatibility": {
        "compose_profiles": {
            "supported_environments": 4,
            "unsupported_environments": 1,
            "impact": "conditional_service_deployment",
            "models_requiring_profiles": ["Skills", "Author"]
        },
        "health_checks": {
            "supported_environments": 5,
            "unsupported_environments": 0,
            "impact": "service_availability_monitoring",
            "models_monitored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        "secrets_management": {
            "supported_environments": 4,
            "unsupported_environments": 1,
            "impact": "secure_credential_handling",
            "models_requiring_secrets": ["User", "Author"]
        }
    }
}

# Model-specific version dependencies
model_version_dependencies = {
    "User_model": {
        "django_minimum": "5.2.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2",
        "compose_features": ["health_checks", "secrets"],
        "database_requirements": "postgresql_15+",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "enhanced_authentication_backends",
            "improved_session_management",
            "gdpr_compliance_tools"
        ]
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "django_minimum": "6.0.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2", 
        "compose_features": ["profiles", "health_checks", "build_context"],
        "search_requirements": "elasticsearch_8+",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "advanced_search_indexing",
            "job_recommendation_engine",
            "application_workflow_automation"
        ]
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "django_minimum": "6.0.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2",
        "compose_features": ["profiles", "secrets", "external_networks"],
        "cache_requirements": "redis_7+",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "verification_api_integration",
            "demand_analysis_pipeline",
            "skill_matching_algorithms"
        ]
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "django_minimum": "5.2.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2",
        "compose_features": ["secrets", "volume_management"],
        "storage_requirements": "s3_compatible_storage",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "portfolio_media_handling",
            "rating_aggregation_system",
            "availability_calendar_integration"
        ]
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "django_minimum": "6.0.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2",
        "compose_features": ["external_networks", "health_checks"],
        "geospatial_requirements": "postgis_3+",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "advanced_geocoding_apis",
            "market_analysis_tools",
            "timezone_automation"
        ]
    }
}

# Upgrade recommendations
upgrade_recommendations = {
    "immediate_upgrades": [
        {
            "environment": "backend_developer",
            "component": "docker_compose",
            "action": "upgrade_to_v2_23_3",
            "reason": "enable_profile_support_for_author_location_models",
            "estimated_time": "15_minutes",
            "risk_level": "low",
            "testing_required": "author_location_service_testing"
        },
        {
            "environment": "frontend_developer", 
            "component": "django",
            "action": "upgrade_to_6_0_2",
            "reason": "skills_location_model_compatibility",
            "estimated_time": "2_hours",
            "risk_level": "medium",
            "testing_required": "full_model_migration_testing"
        }
    ],
    "planned_upgrades": [
        {
            "environment": "all_development",
            "component": "docker_engine",
            "action": "standardize_on_24_0_7",
            "reason": "consistent_container_runtime_behavior",
            "estimated_time": "1_hour_per_environment",
            "risk_level": "low",
            "coordination_required": True
        }
    ]
}

# Print environment compatibility analysis
print("Django Multi-Platform Environment Compatibility Audit - anihpj Team:")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"Audit Time: {environment_audit['audit_timestamp']}")
print(f"Project: {environment_audit['project_name'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n👥 Development Team Environments:")
compatible_envs = sum(1 for env in development_environments.values() if env['compatibility_score'] >= 90)
total_envs = len(development_environments)

print(f"   Compatible Environments: {compatible_envs}/{total_envs}")
print(f"   Environment Types: Development, Testing, Production")

for env_name, details in development_environments.items():
    env_display = env_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    developer = details['developer']
    platform = details['platform']
    compose_ver = details['docker_compose_version']
    django_ver = details['django_version']
    score = details['compatibility_score']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= 90 else "🟡" if score >= 80 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {score_icon} {env_display} ({developer}):")
    print(f"      Platform: {platform}")
    print(f"      Compose: {compose_ver} | Django: {django_ver}")
    print(f"      Compatibility: {score}%")
    
    if 'models_developed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_developed'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_deployed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_deployed'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n⚙️ Version Requirements:")
requirements = compatibility_matrix['docker_compose_requirements']
print(f"   Minimum Compose: {requirements['minimum_version']}")
print(f"   Recommended: {requirements['recommended_version']}")

django_compat = compatibility_matrix['django_version_compatibility']
print(f"   Target Django: {django_compat['target_version']}")
print(f"   Minimum Django: {django_compat['minimum_supported']}")

print(f"\n🚨 Compatibility Issues:")
issues = compatibility_analysis['version_inconsistencies']
print(f"   Total Issues: {len(issues)}")

for issue in issues:
    env_name = issue['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    problem = issue['issue'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    current = issue['current']
    required = issue['required']
    
    print(f"\n   ❌ {env_name}:")
    print(f"      Issue: {problem}")
    print(f"      Current: {current} → Required: {required}")
    print(f"      Models Affected: {', '.join(issue['models_affected'])}")
    print(f"      Resolution: {issue['resolution'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Feature Compatibility Matrix:")
feature_compat = compatibility_analysis['feature_compatibility']

for feature, details in feature_compat.items():
    feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
    supported = details['supported_environments']
    unsupported = details['unsupported_environments']
    
    status_icon = "🟢" if unsupported == 0 else "🟡" if unsupported == 1 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {feature_name}:")
    print(f"      Supported: {supported}/{supported + unsupported} environments")
    
    if 'models_requiring_profiles' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_requiring_profiles'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_monitored' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_monitored'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n📋 Django Model Version Dependencies:")
for model, deps in model_version_dependencies.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    django_min = deps['django_minimum']
    django_rec = deps['django_recommended']
    
    print(f"\n   📊 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Django: {django_min}+ (Recommended: {django_rec})")
    print(f"      Compose Features: {', '.join(deps['compose_features'])}")
    
    features = deps['version_specific_features']
    if features:
        feature_text = features[0].replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"      Key Feature: {feature_text}")

print(f"\n🔧 Upgrade Recommendations:")
immediate = upgrade_recommendations['immediate_upgrades']
print(f"   Immediate Actions: {len(immediate)}")

for upgrade in immediate:
    env_name = upgrade['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    component = upgrade['component'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    action = upgrade['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_est = upgrade['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    risk = upgrade['risk_level'].title()
    
    print(f"\n   🔧 {env_name} - {component}:")
    print(f"      Action: {action}")
    print(f"      Time: {time_est} | Risk: {risk}")
    print(f"      Testing: {upgrade['testing_required'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🎯 Compatibility Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ {compatible_envs}/{total_envs} environments fully compatible")
print(f"   🔧 {len(issues)} compatibility issues identified")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models version-analyzed")
print(f"   🚀 Production environment: 100% compatible")
print(f"   👥 Team coordination required for upgrades")

print(f"\n🚀 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Upgrade Alex's Docker Compose to v2.23.3")
print(f"   2. Upgrade Maria's Django to 6.0.2 with model testing") 
print(f"   3. Standardize Docker Engine across all environments")
print(f"   4. Implement automated version checking in CI/CD")
print(f"   5. Create environment setup documentation")

Docker Compose: Action: Upgrade To V2 23 3 Time: 15 minutes | Risk: Low Testing: Author Location Service Testing 🔧 Frontend Developer - Django: Action: Upgrade To 6 0 2 Time: 2 hours | Risk: Medium Testing: Full Model Migration Testing 🎯 Compatibility Summary: ✅ 3/5 environments fully compatible 🔧 3 compatibility issues identified 📊 All 5 Django models version-analyzed 🚀 Production environment: 100% compatible 👥 Team coordination required for upgrades
Example 2: CI/CD Pipeline Version Validation (Automated Deployment Gating)

Implement automated version checking in CI/CD pipelines for Django anihpj deployments, ensuring Docker Compose compatibility for User authentication, JobPost workflow, Skills processing, Author management, and Location services before deployment execution.

# Get JSON version for script parsing
docker compose version --format json

# Extract version number only
docker compose version --short

# Verify minimum version requirements
docker compose version --format json | jq '.version'

# Check both Docker and Compose versions
docker --version && docker compose version

Django Models Context & CI/CD Version Gating:

# CI/CD pipeline version validation for Django anihpj deployment
# Gating User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model deployments

import json
import subprocess
import sys
from typing import Dict, List, Any, Tuple
from packaging import version

# CI/CD version validation configuration
cicd_validation_config = {
    "pipeline_id": "anihpj_deploy_2026_01_12_build_456",
    "validation_stage": "pre_deployment_checks",
    "project_name": "anihpj_jobpost_application"
}

# Version requirements specification
version_requirements = {
    "docker_compose": {
        "minimum_version": "2.20.0",
        "recommended_version": "2.23.3",
        "blocking_threshold": "2.19.0",
        "feature_dependencies": {
            "compose_profiles": "2.20.0",
            "secrets_management": "2.18.0",
            "health_check_improvements": "2.21.0",
            "build_context_enhancements": "2.22.0"
        }
    },
    "docker_engine": {
        "minimum_version": "24.0.0",
        "recommended_version": "24.0.7",
        "blocking_threshold": "23.0.0"
    },
    "compose_file_format": {
        "target_version": "3.8",
        "minimum_supported": "3.7",
        "model_specific_requirements": {
            "User": "3.7",
            "JobPost": "3.8",
            "Skills": "3.8",
            "Author": "3.7",
            "Location": "3.8"
        }
    }
}

# Model deployment version dependencies
model_deployment_dependencies = {
    "User_model_deployment": {
        "compose_features_required": ["secrets", "health_checks"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.18.0",
        "deployment_constraints": [
            "authentication_service_availability",
            "session_storage_connectivity",
            "gdpr_compliance_verification"
        ],
        "version_sensitive_operations": [
            "password_hashing_configuration",
            "session_timeout_settings",
            "multi_factor_auth_setup"
        ]
    },
    "JobPost_model_deployment": {
        "compose_features_required": ["profiles", "build_context", "health_checks"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.22.0",
        "deployment_constraints": [
            "search_service_elasticsearch_ready",
            "background_worker_celery_ready",
            "notification_service_configured"
        ],
        "version_sensitive_operations": [
            "search_index_migration",
            "job_recommendation_engine",
            "application_workflow_setup"
        ]
    },
    "Skills_model_deployment": {
        "compose_features_required": ["external_networks", "profiles"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.20.0",
        "deployment_constraints": [
            "verification_api_connectivity",
            "cache_service_availability",
            "demand_analysis_pipeline"
        ],
        "version_sensitive_operations": [
            "skill_verification_integration",
            "demand_tracking_configuration",
            "matching_algorithm_deployment"
        ]
    },
    "Author_model_deployment": {
        "compose_features_required": ["volumes", "secrets"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.18.0",
        "deployment_constraints": [
            "portfolio_storage_configured",
            "rating_service_available",
            "availability_calendar_connected"
        ],
        "version_sensitive_operations": [
            "portfolio_media_migration",
            "rating_aggregation_setup",
            "availability_sync_configuration"
        ]
    },
    "Location_model_deployment": {
        "compose_features_required": ["health_checks", "external_networks"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.21.0",
        "deployment_constraints": [
            "geocoding_api_configured",
            "postgis_extension_available",
            "timezone_service_ready"
        ],
        "version_sensitive_operations": [
            "geospatial_index_creation",
            "market_analysis_setup",
            "coordinate_validation_deployment"
        ]
    }
}

# Version validation results simulation
validation_results = {
    "compose_version_check": {
        "detected_version": "2.23.3",
        "meets_minimum": True,
        "meets_recommended": True,
        "feature_compatibility": {
            "compose_profiles": True,
            "secrets_management": True,
            "health_check_improvements": True,
            "build_context_enhancements": True
        }
    },
    "docker_engine_check": {
        "detected_version": "24.0.7",
        "meets_minimum": True,
        "meets_recommended": True
    },
    "model_deployment_readiness": {
        "User": {"ready": True, "blocking_issues": []},
        "JobPost": {"ready": True, "blocking_issues": []},
        "Skills": {"ready": True, "blocking_issues": []},
        "Author": {"ready": True, "blocking_issues": []},
        "Location": {"ready": True, "blocking_issues": []}
    }
}

# Pipeline gate decision
gate_decision = {
    "overall_status": "PASSED",
    "deployment_approved": True,
    "confidence_score": 98,
    "blocking_issues": [],
    "warnings": ["Consider updating to latest patch version"],
    "recommendations": [
        "Enable verbose logging for deployment monitoring",
        "Configure rollback triggers for health check failures"
    ]
}

# Print CI/CD version validation report
print("Django CI/CD Pipeline Version Validation - anihpj Deployment:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Pipeline: {cicd_validation_config['pipeline_id']}")
print(f"Stage: {cicd_validation_config['validation_stage'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📋 Version Requirements:")
compose_req = version_requirements['docker_compose']
engine_req = version_requirements['docker_engine']
print(f"   Docker Compose: >={compose_req['minimum_version']} (Recommended: {compose_req['recommended_version']})")
print(f"   Docker Engine: >={engine_req['minimum_version']} (Recommended: {engine_req['recommended_version']})")

print(f"\n✅ Version Detection Results:")
compose_check = validation_results['compose_version_check']
engine_check = validation_results['docker_engine_check']

compose_icon = "🟢" if compose_check['meets_recommended'] else "🟡"
engine_icon = "🟢" if engine_check['meets_recommended'] else "🟡"

print(f"   {compose_icon} Docker Compose: {compose_check['detected_version']}")
print(f"   {engine_icon} Docker Engine: {engine_check['detected_version']}")

print(f"\n🔧 Feature Compatibility:")
for feature, compatible in compose_check['feature_compatibility'].items():
    feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
    icon = "✅" if compatible else "❌"
    print(f"   {icon} {feature_name}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Deployment Readiness:")
for model, readiness in validation_results['model_deployment_readiness'].items():
    ready = readiness['ready']
    icon = "🟢" if ready else "🔴"
    status = "Ready" if ready else "Blocked"
    print(f"   {icon} {model}: {status}")
    
    if not ready and readiness['blocking_issues']:
        for issue in readiness['blocking_issues']:
            print(f"      ❌ {issue}")

print(f"\n🚦 Pipeline Gate Decision:")
gate_icon = "🟢" if gate_decision['overall_status'] == "PASSED" else "🔴"
print(f"   {gate_icon} Status: {gate_decision['overall_status']}")
print(f"   📊 Confidence: {gate_decision['confidence_score']}%")
print(f"   ✅ Deployment Approved: {gate_decision['deployment_approved']}")

if gate_decision['warnings']:
    print(f"\n⚠️ Warnings:")
    for warning in gate_decision['warnings']:
        print(f"   - {warning}")

print(f"\n🎯 Deployment Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All version requirements met")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models deployment-ready")
print(f"   🚀 Pipeline gate: PASSED")
print(f"   🔧 Proceed with confidence")
Docker Compose version v2.23.3 v2.23.3 Django CI/CD Pipeline Version Validation - anihpj Deployment: ================================================================= Pipeline: anihpj_deploy_2026_01_12_build_456 Stage: Pre Deployment Checks 📋 Version Requirements: Docker Compose: >=2.20.0 (Recommended: 2.23.3) Docker Engine: >=24.0.0 (Recommended: 24.0.7) ✅ Version Detection Results: 🟢 Docker Compose: 2.23.3 🟢 Docker Engine: 24.0.7 🔧 Feature Compatibility: ✅ Compose Profiles ✅ Secrets Management ✅ Health Check Improvements ✅ Build Context Enhancements 📊 Model Deployment Readiness: 🟢 User: Ready 🟢 JobPost: Ready 🟢 Skills: Ready 🟢 Author: Ready 🟢 Location: Ready 🚦 Pipeline Gate Decision: 🟢 Status: PASSED 📊 Confidence: 98% ✅ Deployment Approved: True ⚠️ Warnings: - Consider updating to latest patch version 🎯 Deployment Summary: ✅ All version requirements met 📊 All 5 Django models deployment-ready 🚀 Pipeline gate: PASSED 🔧 Proceed with confidence
Example 3: Troubleshooting Version Mismatch (Debugging Feature Availability)

Diagnose Docker Compose version-related issues affecting Django anihpj feature availability, identifying compatibility problems with User sessions, JobPost search, Skills verification, Author profiles, and Location geocoding services.

# Full version information with build details
docker compose version

# Get detailed version in JSON
docker compose version --format json

# Check Docker engine version alignment
docker info --format '{{.ServerVersion}}'

# Verify compose file version support
docker compose config --quiet

Django Models Context & Version Troubleshooting:

# Version mismatch troubleshooting for Django anihpj services
# Diagnosing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location feature issues

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Troubleshooting session context
troubleshooting_context = {
    "session_id": "debug_2026_01_12_feature_mismatch",
    "issue_type": "compose_feature_unavailable",
    "reported_by": "development_team",
    "environment": "staging"
}

# Version mismatch detection
version_analysis = {
    "current_compose_version": "2.19.1",
    "required_compose_version": "2.22.0",
    "version_gap": "3_minor_versions",
    "feature_availability": {
        "compose_profiles": {"required": "2.20.0", "available": False, "impact": "high"},
        "improved_health_checks": {"required": "2.21.0", "available": False, "impact": "medium"},
        "build_context_improvements": {"required": "2.22.0", "available": False, "impact": "high"},
        "secrets_v2": {"required": "2.18.0", "available": True, "impact": "none"},
        "basic_compose_features": {"required": "2.15.0", "available": True, "impact": "none"}
    }
}

# Model-specific feature impact
model_feature_impact = {
    "User_model_impact": {
        "affected_features": ["conditional_auth_service", "health_monitored_sessions"],
        "symptoms": [
            "Profile service not starting conditionally",
            "Health checks running with legacy behavior",
            "Session persistence issues during restarts"
        ],
        "workarounds": [
            "Use manual service startup instead of profiles",
            "Implement application-level health endpoints",
            "Configure external session storage"
        ],
        "severity": "medium"
    },
    "JobPost_model_impact": {
        "affected_features": ["search_service_profiles", "build_cache_optimization"],
        "symptoms": [
            "Cannot selectively start search services",
            "Slow builds due to missing cache improvements",
            "Elasticsearch service always starting"
        ],
        "workarounds": [
            "Use separate compose files for search services",
            "Implement manual build caching strategy",
            "Use docker-compose override files"
        ],
        "severity": "high"
    },
    "Skills_model_impact": {
        "affected_features": ["verification_service_profiles", "external_api_health_checks"],
        "symptoms": [
            "Verification services not conditionally available",
            "Cannot monitor external API connectivity",
            "Cache warming services always running"
        ],
        "workarounds": [
            "Manual service management for verification",
            "Implement custom API health monitoring",
            "Use environment variables for service control"
        ],
        "severity": "medium"
    },
    "Author_model_impact": {
        "affected_features": ["portfolio_build_optimization", "media_service_health"],
        "symptoms": [
            "Portfolio image builds slower than expected",
            "Media service health not properly monitored",
            "Storage services starting unnecessarily"
        ],
        "workarounds": [
            "Pre-build portfolio images manually",
            "Use external health monitoring tools",
            "Configure service dependencies explicitly"
        ],
        "severity": "low"
    },
    "Location_model_impact": {
        "affected_features": ["geocoding_service_profiles", "postgis_health_monitoring"],
        "symptoms": [
            "Geocoding services always starting",
            "PostGIS extension health not tracked",
            "Market analysis services not conditional"
        ],
        "workarounds": [
            "Use environment-based service control",
            "Implement database-level health checks",
            "Manual market service management"
        ],
        "severity": "medium"
    }
}

# Resolution recommendations
resolution_plan = {
    "immediate_actions": [
        {
            "action": "upgrade_docker_compose",
            "from_version": "2.19.1",
            "to_version": "2.23.3",
            "estimated_time": "15_minutes",
            "risk_level": "low",
            "testing_required": "full_service_verification"
        }
    ],
    "alternative_solutions": [
        {
            "solution": "use_compose_file_overrides",
            "description": "Create environment-specific compose files",
            "complexity": "medium",
            "maintenance_overhead": "high"
        },
        {
            "solution": "implement_workarounds",
            "description": "Apply model-specific workarounds",
            "complexity": "high",
            "maintenance_overhead": "very_high"
        }
    ],
    "recommended_path": "upgrade_docker_compose"
}

# Print troubleshooting analysis
print("Django Version Mismatch Troubleshooting - anihpj Features:")
print("=" * 62)
print(f"Session: {troubleshooting_context['session_id']}")
print(f"Issue: {troubleshooting_context['issue_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🔍 Version Analysis:")
print(f"   Current Version: {version_analysis['current_compose_version']}")
print(f"   Required Version: {version_analysis['required_compose_version']}")
print(f"   Gap: {version_analysis['version_gap'].replace('_', ' ')}")

print(f"\n📊 Feature Availability Matrix:")
for feature, details in version_analysis['feature_availability'].items():
    feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
    available = details['available']
    required = details['required']
    impact = details['impact'].title()
    
    icon = "✅" if available else "❌"
    impact_icon = "🔴" if impact == "High" else "🟡" if impact == "Medium" else "🟢"
    
    print(f"   {icon} {feature_name}")
    print(f"      Required: v{required} | Impact: {impact_icon} {impact}")

print(f"\n🎯 Model-Specific Impact Analysis:")
for model, impact in model_feature_impact.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_impact', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    severity = impact['severity'].title()
    severity_icon = "🔴" if severity == "High" else "🟡" if severity == "Medium" else "🟢"
    
    print(f"\n   {severity_icon} {model_name} Model ({severity} Severity):")
    print(f"      Affected Features: {len(impact['affected_features'])}")
    print(f"      Symptoms: {len(impact['symptoms'])}")
    print(f"      Workarounds: {len(impact['workarounds'])}")

print(f"\n🔧 Resolution Recommendations:")
immediate = resolution_plan['immediate_actions'][0]
print(f"   Recommended: {immediate['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"   Upgrade: v{immediate['from_version']} → v{immediate['to_version']}")
print(f"   Time: {immediate['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')}")
print(f"   Risk: {immediate['risk_level'].title()}")

print(f"\n✅ Troubleshooting Summary:")
print(f"   🔍 Root cause: Outdated Docker Compose version")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models affected to varying degrees")
print(f"   🔧 Solution: Upgrade to v2.23.3")
print(f"   ⏱️ Resolution time: ~15 minutes")
Docker Compose version v2.19.1 { "version": "v2.19.1", "buildTime": "2023-05-14T19:17:14Z", "gitCommit": "de7d7f0", "platform": "linux/amd64" } Django Version Mismatch Troubleshooting - anihpj Features: ============================================================== Session: debug_2026_01_12_feature_mismatch Issue: Compose Feature Unavailable 🔍 Version Analysis: Current Version: 2.19.1 Required Version: 2.22.0 Gap: 3 minor versions 📊 Feature Availability Matrix: ❌ Compose Profiles Required: v2.20.0 | Impact: 🔴 High ❌ Improved Health Checks Required: v2.21.0 | Impact: 🟡 Medium ❌ Build Context Improvements Required: v2.22.0 | Impact: 🔴 High ✅ Secrets V2 Required: v2.18.0 | Impact: 🟢 None 🎯 Model-Specific Impact Analysis: 🟡 User Model (Medium Severity): Affected Features: 2 Symptoms: 3 Workarounds: 3 🔴 Jobpost Model (High Severity): Affected Features: 2 Symptoms: 3 Workarounds: 3 🔧 Resolution Recommendations: Recommended: Upgrade Docker Compose Upgrade: v2.19.1 → v2.23.3 Time: 15 minutes Risk: Low ✅ Troubleshooting Summary: 🔍 Root cause: Outdated Docker Compose version 📊 All 5 Django models affected to varying degrees 🔧 Solution: Upgrade to v2.23.3 ⏱️ Resolution time: ~15 minutes
Example 4: Production Environment Standardization (Version Compliance Enforcement)

Enforce Docker Compose version standardization across Django anihpj production infrastructure, ensuring consistent deployment capabilities for User security, JobPost reliability, Skills accuracy, Author integrity, and Location precision services.

# Check version across all servers
docker compose version --short

# Get JSON version for compliance reporting
docker compose version --format json

# Verify version meets production standards
docker compose version && echo "Version check passed"

# Compare with required version
docker compose version --format json | jq -r '.version'

Django Models Context & Production Standardization:

# Production version standardization for Django anihpj infrastructure
# Enforcing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location deployment standards

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Production standardization context
standardization_context = {
    "audit_id": "prod_version_audit_2026_01_12",
    "scope": "anihpj_production_infrastructure",
    "compliance_framework": "internal_devops_standards"
}

# Production server inventory with versions
production_inventory = {
    "prod_web_cluster": {
        "servers": ["prod-web-01", "prod-web-02", "prod-web-03"],
        "compose_versions": ["2.23.3", "2.23.3", "2.23.3"],
        "docker_versions": ["24.0.7", "24.0.7", "24.0.7"],
        "compliance_status": "compliant",
        "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "last_audit": "2026-01-10"
    },
    "prod_worker_cluster": {
        "servers": ["prod-worker-01", "prod-worker-02"],
        "compose_versions": ["2.23.3", "2.23.1"],
        "docker_versions": ["24.0.7", "24.0.6"],
        "compliance_status": "minor_deviation",
        "models_processed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "last_audit": "2026-01-08"
    },
    "prod_database_cluster": {
        "servers": ["prod-db-primary", "prod-db-replica"],
        "compose_versions": ["2.23.3", "2.23.3"],
        "docker_versions": ["24.0.7", "24.0.7"],
        "compliance_status": "compliant",
        "models_stored": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "last_audit": "2026-01-11"
    },
    "prod_cache_cluster": {
        "servers": ["prod-redis-01", "prod-redis-02"],
        "compose_versions": ["2.23.0", "2.22.0"],
        "docker_versions": ["24.0.5", "24.0.4"],
        "compliance_status": "upgrade_required",
        "models_cached": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"],
        "last_audit": "2026-01-05"
    }
}

# Version compliance standards
compliance_standards = {
    "required_compose_version": "2.23.3",
    "minimum_compose_version": "2.23.0",
    "required_docker_version": "24.0.7",
    "minimum_docker_version": "24.0.5",
    "compliance_tolerance": "one_minor_version",
    "audit_frequency": "weekly",
    "remediation_sla": "72_hours"
}

# Model-specific compliance requirements
model_compliance_requirements = {
    "User_model": {
        "security_features_required": ["secrets_v2", "health_checks"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.21.0",
        "compliance_critical": True,
        "audit_priority": "high"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "performance_features_required": ["build_cache", "profiles"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.22.0",
        "compliance_critical": True,
        "audit_priority": "high"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "integration_features_required": ["external_networks", "profiles"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.20.0",
        "compliance_critical": False,
        "audit_priority": "medium"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "storage_features_required": ["volumes_v2", "secrets"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.18.0",
        "compliance_critical": False,
        "audit_priority": "medium"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "geo_features_required": ["health_checks", "external_apis"],
        "minimum_compose": "2.21.0",
        "compliance_critical": False,
        "audit_priority": "medium"
    }
}

# Compliance audit results
audit_results = {
    "total_servers": 9,
    "compliant_servers": 5,
    "minor_deviation_servers": 2,
    "upgrade_required_servers": 2,
    "compliance_percentage": 77.8,
    "remediation_actions": [
        {
            "cluster": "prod_worker_cluster",
            "server": "prod-worker-02",
            "action": "upgrade_compose_to_2_23_3",
            "priority": "medium",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "cluster": "prod_cache_cluster",
            "servers": ["prod-redis-01", "prod-redis-02"],
            "action": "upgrade_compose_and_docker",
            "priority": "high",
            "models_affected": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"]
        }
    ]
}

# Print compliance report
print("Django Production Version Compliance Report - anihpj Infrastructure:")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"Audit ID: {standardization_context['audit_id']}")
print(f"Framework: {standardization_context['compliance_framework'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📋 Compliance Standards:")
print(f"   Required Compose: v{compliance_standards['required_compose_version']}")
print(f"   Minimum Compose: v{compliance_standards['minimum_compose_version']}")
print(f"   Required Docker: v{compliance_standards['required_docker_version']}")
print(f"   Remediation SLA: {compliance_standards['remediation_sla'].replace('_', ' ')}")

print(f"\n🖥️ Production Server Inventory:")
for cluster, details in production_inventory.items():
    cluster_name = cluster.replace('_', ' ').title()
    status = details['compliance_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    if status == "Compliant":
        icon = "🟢"
    elif status == "Minor Deviation":
        icon = "🟡"
    else:
        icon = "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {cluster_name}:")
    print(f"      Servers: {', '.join(details['servers'])}")
    print(f"      Compose Versions: {', '.join(details['compose_versions'])}")
    print(f"      Status: {status}")

print(f"\n📊 Compliance Summary:")
print(f"   Total Servers: {audit_results['total_servers']}")
print(f"   Compliant: {audit_results['compliant_servers']}")
print(f"   Minor Deviation: {audit_results['minor_deviation_servers']}")
print(f"   Upgrade Required: {audit_results['upgrade_required_servers']}")
print(f"   Compliance Rate: {audit_results['compliance_percentage']:.1f}%")

print(f"\n🔧 Remediation Actions Required:")
for action in audit_results['remediation_actions']:
    cluster = action['cluster'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = action['priority'].title()
    priority_icon = "🔴" if priority == "High" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {priority_icon} {cluster} ({priority} Priority):")
    print(f"      Action: {action['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Models Affected: {', '.join(action['models_affected'])}")

print(f"\n✅ Standardization Summary:")
print(f"   📊 77.8% infrastructure compliance achieved")
print(f"   🔧 4 servers require version updates")
print(f"   📋 All 5 Django models covered by compliance")
print(f"   ⏱️ Remediation within 72-hour SLA")

Example 5: Documentation Generation (Infrastructure Reporting)

Generate comprehensive Docker Compose version documentation for Django anihpj infrastructure reports, supporting audit requirements and operational documentation for User, JobPost, Skills, Author, and Location service deployments.

# Generate JSON version report
docker compose version --format json > version_report.json

# Get comprehensive version information
docker compose version && docker --version

# Create version inventory
docker compose version --short >> infrastructure_versions.txt

# Generate timestamp with version
echo "$(date): $(docker compose version --short)"

Django Models Context & Documentation Generation:

# Infrastructure documentation generation for Django anihpj
# Documenting User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location deployment versions

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Documentation generation context
doc_context = {
    "report_id": "infra_doc_2026_01_12",
    "report_type": "quarterly_infrastructure_audit",
    "generated_for": "anihpj_operations_team"
}

# Version information collected
version_documentation = {
    "docker_compose": {
        "version": "2.23.3",
        "build_time": "2023-12-07T14:51:20Z",
        "git_commit": "7eb2aad0",
        "platform": "linux/amd64",
        "go_version": "go1.21.5"
    },
    "docker_engine": {
        "version": "24.0.7",
        "api_version": "1.43",
        "git_commit": "afdd53b",
        "os_arch": "linux/amd64"
    },
    "compose_file_version": "3.8",
    "supported_features": [
        "profiles", "secrets", "configs", "healthchecks",
        "build_context", "deploy_replicas", "networks_v2"
    ]
}

# Model deployment documentation
model_deployment_docs = {
    "User_model": {
        "deployment_version": "v2.1.4",
        "compose_features_used": ["secrets", "healthchecks", "volumes"],
        "service_dependencies": ["db", "redis", "nginx"],
        "documentation_sections": [
            "authentication_configuration",
            "session_management_setup",
            "security_compliance_notes"
        ],
        "last_deployment": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "deployment_frequency": "weekly"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "deployment_version": "v2.1.4",
        "compose_features_used": ["profiles", "build_context", "healthchecks"],
        "service_dependencies": ["db", "elasticsearch", "celery", "redis"],
        "documentation_sections": [
            "search_service_configuration",
            "background_job_processing",
            "notification_system_setup"
        ],
        "last_deployment": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "deployment_frequency": "weekly"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "deployment_version": "v2.1.4",
        "compose_features_used": ["profiles", "external_networks"],
        "service_dependencies": ["db", "redis", "verification_api"],
        "documentation_sections": [
            "verification_api_integration",
            "caching_strategy",
            "demand_analysis_pipeline"
        ],
        "last_deployment": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "deployment_frequency": "bi_weekly"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "deployment_version": "v2.1.4",
        "compose_features_used": ["volumes", "secrets"],
        "service_dependencies": ["db", "media_storage", "notification_service"],
        "documentation_sections": [
            "portfolio_storage_configuration",
            "rating_system_setup",
            "availability_management"
        ],
        "last_deployment": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "deployment_frequency": "bi_weekly"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "deployment_version": "v2.1.4",
        "compose_features_used": ["healthchecks", "external_networks"],
        "service_dependencies": ["db", "postgis", "geocoding_api"],
        "documentation_sections": [
            "geospatial_database_setup",
            "geocoding_service_configuration",
            "market_analysis_integration"
        ],
        "last_deployment": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "deployment_frequency": "monthly"
    }
}

# Generate documentation report
documentation_report = {
    "report_metadata": {
        "title": "anihpj Django Infrastructure Version Report",
        "generated": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "report_period": "Q1 2026",
        "compliance_framework": "SOC2_Type_II"
    },
    "executive_summary": {
        "total_services": 12,
        "total_models": 5,
        "infrastructure_health": "excellent",
        "compliance_status": "fully_compliant",
        "version_currency": "up_to_date"
    },
    "technical_specifications": version_documentation,
    "model_deployments": model_deployment_docs,
    "recommendations": [
        "Continue current version update cadence",
        "Implement automated version monitoring",
        "Document version rollback procedures"
    ]
}

# Print documentation report
print("Django Infrastructure Version Documentation - anihpj Q1 2026:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Report ID: {doc_context['report_id']}")
print(f"Type: {doc_context['report_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📋 Executive Summary:")
summary = documentation_report['executive_summary']
print(f"   Total Services: {summary['total_services']}")
print(f"   Total Models: {summary['total_models']}")
print(f"   Infrastructure Health: {summary['infrastructure_health'].title()}")
print(f"   Compliance Status: {summary['compliance_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🐳 Docker Compose Version Details:")
compose = version_documentation['docker_compose']
print(f"   Version: {compose['version']}")
print(f"   Build Time: {compose['build_time']}")
print(f"   Platform: {compose['platform']}")

print(f"\n🔧 Docker Engine Version Details:")
engine = version_documentation['docker_engine']
print(f"   Version: {engine['version']}")
print(f"   API Version: {engine['api_version']}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Deployment Documentation:")
for model, docs in model_deployment_docs.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    version = docs['deployment_version']
    features = len(docs['compose_features_used'])
    deps = len(docs['service_dependencies'])
    
    print(f"\n   📖 {model_name} Model ({version}):")
    print(f"      Compose Features: {features}")
    print(f"      Dependencies: {deps}")
    print(f"      Doc Sections: {len(docs['documentation_sections'])}")

print(f"\n📝 Documentation Recommendations:")
for rec in documentation_report['recommendations']:
    print(f"   • {rec}")

print(f"\n✅ Documentation Summary:")
print(f"   📊 Complete version documentation generated")
print(f"   📋 All 5 Django models documented")
print(f"   🔧 Infrastructure specifications recorded")
print(f"   ✅ Ready for compliance audit submission")

docker compose events

Official Docs: docker compose events | Docker CLI: docker compose events

Stream real-time events from Django services showing container lifecycle and system activity.

Full Description

The docker compose events command streams real-time events from your anihpj Django services, showing container lifecycle events, service status changes, and system activity. This provides live monitoring of your Django application stack behavior and enables proactive issue detection.

For Django applications, this command is essential for monitoring service health, debugging deployment issues, tracking container lifecycle events, and maintaining visibility into your anihpj jobpost application's runtime behavior across all service components.

Syntax

# Stream all service events
docker compose events

# Monitor specific service events
docker compose events web

# Show events with timestamps
docker compose events --json

# Filter events by type
docker compose events --filter event=start

# Monitor multiple services
docker compose events web db redis

# Show events since specific time
docker compose events --since 2026-01-12T09:00:00

# Follow events in real-time
docker compose events --follow

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Monitor specific Django services for targeted event filtering. Focus on particular service components.
  • --json: Output Django events in JSON format with structured timestamps. Machine-readable format for log processing.
  • --filter: Filter Django events by type, service, or attributes. Focus on specific event categories or conditions.
  • --since: Show Django events since specific timestamp. Historical event analysis and debugging.
  • --follow: Follow Django events in real-time continuously. Live monitoring and alerting integration.

Attributes

  • Real-Time Monitoring: Live streaming of Django service events including start, stop, die, and health check events.
  • Service Lifecycle Tracking: Monitors Django container creation, startup, running state, and termination events.
  • Event Filtering: Selective Django event monitoring by service, event type, or time range for focused analysis.
  • Structured Output: JSON format Django events with timestamps, service names, and event attributes for automation.
  • Historical Analysis: Review Django service events from specific timeframes for troubleshooting and analysis.

Methods

Live Service Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose events --follow

Parameters: continuous Django service event streaming for real-time monitoring

Monitor Django service health and performance with live event streaming for proactive issue detection.

Troubleshooting Analysis

Syntax: docker compose events --since 1h --json

Parameters: historical Django events in structured format for issue analysis

Analyze Django service behavior over time periods for troubleshooting and performance optimization.

Return Value

Returns stream of Django service events showing timestamps, service names, event types, and related attributes.

  • Success (0): Events streamed successfully
  • No Events (0): No events found in specified timeframe
  • Service Error (1): Service not found or event stream error

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Deployment Monitoring (Service Orchestration Event Tracking)

Monitor Django production deployment events during rolling update of User authentication services, JobPost processing systems, Skills verification components, and Location services with real-time event analysis and alerting.

# Monitor all deployment events
docker compose events --follow --json

# Track specific service deployments
docker compose events web worker --since 10m

# Filter for health check events
docker compose events --filter event=health_status

# Monitor database events during migration
docker compose events db --follow

Django Models Context & Event Monitoring:

# Production deployment event monitoring for Django anihpj services
# Tracking User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model service events

import json
import datetime
import threading
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from collections import defaultdict

# Production deployment event monitoring
deployment_monitoring = {
    "deployment_id": "deploy_2026_01_12_v2_1_4",
    "start_time": "2026-01-12 10:00:00",
    "deployment_type": "rolling_update",
    "target_services": ["web", "worker", "db", "redis", "nginx"]
}

# Real-time event stream simulation
event_stream_data = [
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:15Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "stop",
        "id": "anihpj_web_1",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_1",
            "signal": "SIGTERM"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "temporary_service_interruption"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:18Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "die",
        "id": "anihpj_web_1",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_1",
            "exitCode": 0
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "graceful_shutdown_completed"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:25Z",
        "type": "service", 
        "action": "start",
        "id": "anihpj_web_2",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_2",
            "image": "anihpj:v2.1.4-production"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "new_version_service_starting"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:35Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "health_status",
        "id": "anihpj_web_2",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "health": "healthy",
            "name": "anihpj_web_2"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "service_ready_for_traffic"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:01:42Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "stop", 
        "id": "anihpj_worker_1",
        "service": "worker",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_1",
            "signal": "SIGTERM"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "background_task_processing_paused"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:01:48Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "create",
        "id": "anihpj_worker_2", 
        "service": "worker",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_2",
            "image": "anihpj:v2.1.4-production"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "new_worker_instance_created"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:02:15Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "start",
        "id": "anihpj_worker_2",
        "service": "worker", 
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_2"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "background_tasks_resumed"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:02:45Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "id": "anihpj_default",
        "service": "nginx",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_nginx_1",
            "network": "anihpj_default"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "reverse_proxy_connectivity_restored"
    }
]

# Event analysis and categorization
event_analysis = {
    "service_lifecycle_events": {
        "total_events": len(event_stream_data),
        "start_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'start']),
        "stop_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'stop']),
        "health_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'health_status']),
        "network_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['type'] == 'network'])
    },
    "service_specific_analysis": {
        "web_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'web'],
            "deployment_status": "successful_rolling_update", 
            "downtime": "20_seconds",
            "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "traffic_impact": "minimal_interruption"
        },
        "worker_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'worker'],
            "deployment_status": "successful_restart",
            "downtime": "30_seconds", 
            "models_processed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "task_impact": "queue_preserved_no_loss"
        },
        "nginx_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'nginx'],
            "deployment_status": "network_reconnection",
            "downtime": "5_seconds",
            "proxy_impact": "brief_connectivity_restoration"
        }
    }
}

# Model-specific impact analysis
model_impact_analysis = {
    "User_model_impact": {
        "authentication_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["login", "session_validation", "profile_access"],
            "user_sessions_preserved": True,
            "active_users_affected": 234,
            "recovery_time": "5_seconds"
        },
        "mitigation_strategies": [
            "session_persistence_in_redis",
            "load_balancer_health_checks", 
            "graceful_shutdown_procedures"
        ]
    },
    "JobPost_model_impact": {
        "job_search_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["search", "create", "application_submit"],
            "search_cache_preserved": True,
            "active_searches_affected": 89,
            "recovery_time": "8_seconds"
        },
        "background_processing": {
            "task_interruption": "30_seconds", 
            "affected_tasks": ["job_notifications", "indexing", "recommendations"],
            "queue_preservation": True,
            "pending_tasks": 45,
            "processing_resumed": "immediate"
        }
    },
    "Skills_model_impact": {
        "verification_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["verification", "demand_analysis", "matching"],
            "verification_cache_intact": True,
            "active_verifications_affected": 12,
            "recovery_time": "3_seconds"
        }
    },
    "Author_model_impact": {
        "profile_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds", 
            "affected_operations": ["profile_view", "portfolio_access", "availability"],
            "profile_cache_maintained": True,
            "active_profiles_affected": 67,
            "recovery_time": "4_seconds"
        }
    },
    "Location_model_impact": {
        "geolocation_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["geocoding", "search_filtering", "market_analysis"],
            "location_cache_preserved": True,
            "active_queries_affected": 34,
            "recovery_time": "6_seconds"
        }
    }
}

# Real-time alerting configuration
alerting_configuration = {
    "alert_thresholds": {
        "service_down_duration": "30_seconds",
        "health_check_failures": 3,
        "container_restart_frequency": "5_per_hour",
        "network_disconnect_duration": "10_seconds"
    },
    "notification_channels": {
        "critical_alerts": ["slack_ops", "pagerduty", "email_oncall"],
        "warning_alerts": ["slack_dev", "email_team"],
        "info_alerts": ["dashboard", "log_aggregation"]
    },
    "model_specific_alerts": {
        "User_model": {
            "alert_on": ["authentication_failures", "session_drops"],
            "escalation_time": "2_minutes",
            "business_impact": "high"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "alert_on": ["search_failures", "application_errors"],
            "escalation_time": "5_minutes", 
            "business_impact": "high"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "alert_on": ["verification_timeouts", "api_errors"],
            "escalation_time": "10_minutes",
            "business_impact": "medium"
        }
    }
}

# Deployment success metrics
deployment_metrics = {
    "overall_deployment": {
        "status": "successful",
        "total_duration": "2_minutes_45_seconds",
        "services_updated": 5,
        "zero_downtime_achieved": False,
        "planned_downtime": "minimal"
    },
    "performance_impact": {
        "average_response_time_increase": "5_milliseconds",
        "error_rate_during_deployment": "0.02%", 
        "user_experience_impact": "minimal",
        "sla_compliance": "maintained"
    },
    "model_service_recovery": {
        "User_auth_recovery": "5_seconds",
        "JobPost_search_recovery": "8_seconds",
        "Skills_verification_recovery": "3_seconds",
        "Author_profile_recovery": "4_seconds",
        "Location_services_recovery": "6_seconds"
    }
}

# Print deployment event monitoring analysis
print("Django Production Deployment Event Monitoring - anihpj Rolling Update:")
print("=" * 72)
print(f"Deployment ID: {deployment_monitoring['deployment_id']}")
print(f"Start Time: {deployment_monitoring['start_time']}")
print(f"Type: {deployment_monitoring['deployment_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Event Stream Analysis:")
lifecycle = event_analysis['service_lifecycle_events']
print(f"   Total Events: {lifecycle['total_events']}")
print(f"   Start Events: {lifecycle['start_events']}")
print(f"   Stop Events: {lifecycle['stop_events']}")
print(f"   Health Events: {lifecycle['health_events']}")
print(f"   Network Events: {lifecycle['network_events']}")

print(f"\n🐳 Service Deployment Timeline:")
for i, event in enumerate(event_stream_data):
    time_display = event['time'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    service = event['service'].title()
    action = event['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    impact = event['impact'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    # Event type icons
    if event['action'] == 'start':
        icon = "🟢"
    elif event['action'] == 'stop':
        icon = "🔴"
    elif event['action'] == 'health_status':
        icon = "💚"
    elif event['action'] == 'die':
        icon = "⚫"
    elif event['action'] == 'create':
        icon = "🆕"
    else:
        icon = "🔵"
    
    print(f"   {time_display} {icon} {service} - {action}")
    print(f"              Impact: {impact}")

print(f"\n🎯 Service-Specific Analysis:")
service_analysis = event_analysis['service_specific_analysis']

for service, details in service_analysis.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    status = details['deployment_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    downtime = details['downtime'].replace('_', ' ')
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name} Service:")
    print(f"      Status: {status}")
    print(f"      Downtime: {downtime}")
    print(f"      Events: {len(details['events'])}")
    
    if 'models_served' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_served'])
        print(f"      Models Served: {models}")
    elif 'models_processed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_processed'])
        print(f"      Models Processed: {models}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Impact Analysis:")
for model, impact in model_impact_analysis.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_impact', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    print(f"\n   📈 {model_name} Model:")
    
    for service_key, service_impact in impact.items():
        if service_key != 'mitigation_strategies' and isinstance(service_impact, dict):
            service_name = service_key.replace('_', ' ').title()
            downtime = service_impact.get('downtime_duration', service_impact.get('task_interruption', 'N/A'))
            affected_ops = len(service_impact.get('affected_operations', []))
            recovery = service_impact.get('recovery_time', 'immediate')
            
            print(f"      {service_name}:")
            print(f"        Downtime: {downtime}")
            print(f"        Operations Affected: {affected_ops}")
            print(f"        Recovery Time: {recovery}")

print(f"\n⚡ Real-Time Alerting:")
thresholds = alerting_configuration['alert_thresholds']
print(f"   Service Down Threshold: {thresholds['service_down_duration']}")
print(f"   Health Check Failures: {thresholds['health_check_failures']}")
print(f"   Network Disconnect Limit: {thresholds['network_disconnect_duration']}")

model_alerts = alerting_configuration['model_specific_alerts']
print(f"\n   Model-Specific Alerts:")
for model, alert_config in model_alerts.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    escalation = alert_config['escalation_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    business_impact = alert_config['business_impact'].title()
    
    print(f"     📊 {model_name}: {escalation} escalation ({business_impact} impact)")

print(f"\n🎯 Deployment Success Metrics:")
overall = deployment_metrics['overall_deployment']
performance = deployment_metrics['performance_impact']

print(f"   Overall Status: {overall['status'].title()}")
print(f"   Total Duration: {overall['total_duration'].replace('_', ' ')}")
print(f"   Services Updated: {overall['services_updated']}")
print(f"   SLA Compliance: {performance['sla_compliance'].title()}")

recovery = deployment_metrics['model_service_recovery']
print(f"\n   Model Recovery Times:")
for service, time in recovery.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_recovery', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_display = time.replace('_', ' ')
    print(f"     📊 {service_name}: {time_display}")

print(f"\n✅ Deployment Summary:")
print(f"   🚀 Rolling update completed successfully")
print(f"   ⏱️ Total deployment time: 2 minutes 45 seconds")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models recovered quickly")
print(f"   🔄 Zero data loss during service transitions")
print(f"   💚 All health checks passing post-deployment")
print(f"   📈 Performance impact minimal and within SLA")

print(f"\n📋 Event Monitoring Benefits:")
print(f"   - Real-time visibility into service lifecycle")
print(f"   - Proactive issue detection and alerting")
print(f"   - Deployment validation and rollback triggers") 
print(f"   - Model-specific impact assessment")
print(f"   - Performance and reliability insights")
