Stop Impact: Temporary Service Interruption 10:00:18 ⚫ Web - Die Impact: Graceful Shutdown Completed 10:00:25 🟢 Web - Start Impact: New Version Service Starting 10:00:35 💚 Web - Health Status Impact: Service Ready For Traffic 10:01:42 🔴 Worker - Stop Impact: Background Task Processing Paused 10:01:48 🆕 Worker - Create Impact: New Worker Instance Created 10:02:15 🟢 Worker - Start Impact: Background Tasks Resumed 10:02:45 🔵 Nginx - Connect Impact: Reverse Proxy Connectivity Restored 🎯 Service-Specific Analysis: 📋 Web Service: Status: Successful Rolling Update Downtime: 20 seconds Events: 4 Models Served: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📋 Worker Service: Status: Successful Restart Downtime: 30 seconds Events: 3 Models Processed: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📋 Nginx Service: Status: Network Reconnection Downtime: 5 seconds Events: 1 📊 Django Model Impact Analysis: 📈 User Model: Authentication Service: Downtime: 20_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: 5_seconds 📈 Jobpost Model: Job Search Service: Downtime: 20_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: 8_seconds Background Processing: Downtime: 30_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: immediate 🎯 Deployment Success Metrics: Overall Status: Successful Total Duration: 2 Minutes 45 Seconds Services Updated: 5 SLA Compliance: Maintained Model Recovery Times: 📊 User Auth: 5 seconds 📊 Jobpost Search: 8 seconds 📊 Skills Verification: 3 seconds 📊 Author Profile: 4 seconds 📊 Location Services: 6 seconds ✅ Deployment Summary: 🚀 Rolling update completed successfully ⏱️ Total deployment time: 2 minutes 45 seconds 📊 All 5 Django models recovered quickly 🔄 Zero data loss during service transitions 💚 All health checks passing post-deployment 📈 Performance impact minimal and within SLA
Example 2: Health Check Monitoring (Service Availability Tracking)

Monitor Django anihpj service health check events in real-time, tracking availability of User authentication, JobPost search, Skills verification, Author profile, and Location geocoding services with proactive alerting.

# Monitor health check events
docker compose events --filter event=health_status

# Track specific service health
docker compose events web --json | grep health

# Monitor database health events
docker compose events db --filter event=health_status

# Follow all health-related events
docker compose events --follow --json

Django Models Context & Health Monitoring:

# Health check event monitoring for Django anihpj services
# Tracking User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service availability

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from collections import defaultdict

# Health monitoring configuration
health_monitoring_config = {
    "monitoring_session": "health_2026_01_12_continuous",
    "monitoring_type": "proactive_availability_tracking",
    "alert_integration": "pagerduty_and_slack"
}

# Health check event stream
health_event_stream = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:00:05Z",
        "service": "web",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "healthy",
        "details": {"response_time_ms": 45, "check_type": "http"},
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:00:10Z",
        "service": "db",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "healthy",
        "details": {"connection_pool": 18, "check_type": "pg_isready"},
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:00:15Z",
        "service": "redis",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "healthy",
        "details": {"memory_usage_mb": 128, "check_type": "redis_ping"},
        "models_affected": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:05:05Z",
        "service": "web",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "unhealthy",
        "details": {"response_time_ms": 5200, "check_type": "http", "error": "timeout"},
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:05:35Z",
        "service": "web",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "healthy",
        "details": {"response_time_ms": 52, "check_type": "http", "recovery": True},
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T12:10:10Z",
        "service": "elasticsearch",
        "event": "health_status",
        "status": "unhealthy",
        "details": {"cluster_status": "yellow", "check_type": "cluster_health"},
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "Skills"]
    }
]

# Model-specific health dependencies
model_health_dependencies = {
    "User_model": {
        "critical_services": ["web", "db", "redis"],
        "health_check_frequency": "30_seconds",
        "availability_requirement": "99.9%",
        "affected_operations": [
            "login_authentication",
            "session_management",
            "profile_access",
            "password_operations"
        ],
        "degraded_mode_available": True
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "critical_services": ["web", "db", "elasticsearch", "celery"],
        "health_check_frequency": "30_seconds",
        "availability_requirement": "99.5%",
        "affected_operations": [
            "job_search",
            "job_creation",
            "application_submission",
            "recommendation_engine"
        ],
        "degraded_mode_available": True
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "critical_services": ["web", "db", "redis", "verification_api"],
        "health_check_frequency": "60_seconds",
        "availability_requirement": "99.0%",
        "affected_operations": [
            "skill_verification",
            "demand_analysis",
            "skill_matching",
            "cache_operations"
        ],
        "degraded_mode_available": True
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "critical_services": ["web", "db", "media_storage"],
        "health_check_frequency": "60_seconds",
        "availability_requirement": "99.0%",
        "affected_operations": [
            "portfolio_access",
            "rating_display",
            "availability_check",
            "profile_management"
        ],
        "degraded_mode_available": True
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "critical_services": ["web", "db", "geocoding_api"],
        "health_check_frequency": "60_seconds",
        "availability_requirement": "98.5%",
        "affected_operations": [
            "geocoding_lookups",
            "location_search",
            "market_analysis",
            "timezone_operations"
        ],
        "degraded_mode_available": True
    }
}

# Health analysis results
health_analysis = {
    "monitoring_period": "1_hour",
    "total_health_events": len(health_event_stream),
    "healthy_events": len([e for e in health_event_stream if e['status'] == 'healthy']),
    "unhealthy_events": len([e for e in health_event_stream if e['status'] == 'unhealthy']),
    "services_monitored": 5,
    "incidents_detected": 2,
    "incident_recovery_time_avg": "30_seconds"
}

# Print health monitoring analysis
print("Django Health Check Event Monitoring - anihpj Services:")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"Session: {health_monitoring_config['monitoring_session']}")
print(f"Type: {health_monitoring_config['monitoring_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Health Event Stream:")
for event in health_event_stream:
    time_display = event['timestamp'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    service = event['service'].title()
    status = event['status'].title()
    
    icon = "💚" if status == "Healthy" else "💔"
    
    print(f"   {time_display} {icon} {service}: {status}")
    
    if 'error' in event['details']:
        print(f"              Error: {event['details']['error']}")
    if 'recovery' in event['details']:
        print(f"              Recovery confirmed")

print(f"\n🏥 Model Health Dependencies:")
for model, deps in model_health_dependencies.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    services = ', '.join(deps['critical_services'])
    availability = deps['availability_requirement']
    
    print(f"\n   📊 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Critical Services: {services}")
    print(f"      Availability SLA: {availability}")
    print(f"      Operations: {len(deps['affected_operations'])}")

print(f"\n📈 Health Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   Total Events: {health_analysis['total_health_events']}")
print(f"   Healthy: {health_analysis['healthy_events']}")
print(f"   Unhealthy: {health_analysis['unhealthy_events']}")
print(f"   Incidents: {health_analysis['incidents_detected']}")

print(f"\n✅ Health Monitoring Summary:")
print(f"   💚 Continuous health tracking active")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models monitored")
print(f"   ⚡ Average recovery: 30 seconds")
print(f"   🔔 Alert integration: PagerDuty + Slack")
2026-01-12 12:00:05.123 container health_status anihpj_web_1 (health=healthy) 2026-01-12 12:00:10.456 container health_status anihpj_db_1 (health=healthy) 2026-01-12 12:00:15.789 container health_status anihpj_redis_1 (health=healthy) 2026-01-12 12:05:05.234 container health_status anihpj_web_1 (health=unhealthy) 2026-01-12 12:05:35.567 container health_status anihpj_web_1 (health=healthy) 2026-01-12 12:10:10.890 container health_status anihpj_elasticsearch_1 (health=unhealthy) Django Health Check Event Monitoring - anihpj Services: ========================================================== Session: health_2026_01_12_continuous Type: Proactive Availability Tracking 📊 Health Event Stream: 12:00:05 💚 Web: Healthy 12:00:10 💚 Db: Healthy 12:00:15 💚 Redis: Healthy 12:05:05 💔 Web: Unhealthy Error: timeout 12:05:35 💚 Web: Healthy Recovery confirmed 12:10:10 💔 Elasticsearch: Unhealthy 🏥 Model Health Dependencies: 📊 User Model: Critical Services: web, db, redis Availability SLA: 99.9% Operations: 4 📊 Jobpost Model: Critical Services: web, db, elasticsearch, celery Availability SLA: 99.5% Operations: 4 📈 Health Analysis Summary: Total Events: 6 Healthy: 4 Unhealthy: 2 Incidents: 2 ✅ Health Monitoring Summary: 💚 Continuous health tracking active 📊 All 5 Django models monitored ⚡ Average recovery: 30 seconds 🔔 Alert integration: PagerDuty + Slack
Example 3: Debugging Container Crashes (Issue Investigation)

Investigate Django anihpj container crash events and unexpected terminations affecting User sessions, JobPost processing, Skills verification, Author profiles, and Location services with root cause analysis.

# Monitor container die events
docker compose events --filter event=die

# Track crash events with JSON output
docker compose events --json | grep -E "die|oom|kill"

# Monitor specific service crashes
docker compose events worker --filter event=die

# Historical crash analysis
docker compose events --since 1h --filter event=die

Django Models Context & Crash Investigation:

# Container crash investigation for Django anihpj services
# Analyzing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service failures

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Crash investigation context
investigation_context = {
    "investigation_id": "crash_inv_2026_01_12_web_failure",
    "trigger": "multiple_container_deaths_detected",
    "severity": "high",
    "assigned_to": "sre_team"
}

# Crash event log
crash_events = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T14:23:15Z",
        "service": "web",
        "container": "anihpj_web_1",
        "event": "die",
        "exit_code": 137,
        "signal": "SIGKILL",
        "reason": "oom_killed",
        "memory_limit": "512MB",
        "memory_usage_at_crash": "510MB",
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T14:45:32Z",
        "service": "worker",
        "container": "anihpj_worker_1",
        "event": "die",
        "exit_code": 1,
        "signal": None,
        "reason": "application_error",
        "error_message": "celery_task_timeout",
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "Skills", "User"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T15:12:08Z",
        "service": "web",
        "container": "anihpj_web_2",
        "event": "die",
        "exit_code": 137,
        "signal": "SIGKILL",
        "reason": "oom_killed",
        "memory_limit": "512MB",
        "memory_usage_at_crash": "508MB",
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    }
]

# Model-specific crash impact
model_crash_impact = {
    "User_model": {
        "sessions_terminated": 145,
        "active_logins_affected": 89,
        "data_integrity": "preserved",
        "recovery_actions": [
            "session_restoration_from_redis",
            "authentication_token_refresh",
            "user_notification_sent"
        ],
        "prevention_recommendations": [
            "increase_memory_limits",
            "implement_connection_pooling",
            "add_request_rate_limiting"
        ]
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "searches_interrupted": 234,
        "applications_in_progress": 12,
        "data_integrity": "preserved",
        "recovery_actions": [
            "search_cache_rebuild",
            "application_state_recovery",
            "notification_queue_replay"
        ],
        "prevention_recommendations": [
            "optimize_search_queries",
            "implement_pagination_limits",
            "add_elasticsearch_circuit_breaker"
        ]
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "verifications_interrupted": 8,
        "cache_invalidations": 45,
        "data_integrity": "preserved",
        "recovery_actions": [
            "verification_retry_queue",
            "cache_warm_up_triggered",
            "api_connection_reset"
        ],
        "prevention_recommendations": [
            "add_verification_timeout",
            "implement_bulk_caching",
            "add_external_api_circuit_breaker"
        ]
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "profile_views_affected": 67,
        "portfolio_uploads_interrupted": 3,
        "data_integrity": "preserved",
        "recovery_actions": [
            "profile_cache_refresh",
            "upload_retry_notification",
            "availability_status_sync"
        ],
        "prevention_recommendations": [
            "optimize_portfolio_loading",
            "implement_lazy_media_loading",
            "add_upload_chunking"
        ]
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "geocoding_requests_failed": 23,
        "search_filters_affected": 45,
        "data_integrity": "preserved",
        "recovery_actions": [
            "geocoding_cache_rebuild",
            "search_index_refresh",
            "market_data_resync"
        ],
        "prevention_recommendations": [
            "cache_geocoding_results",
            "implement_fallback_geocoder",
            "add_location_validation_limits"
        ]
    }
}

# Root cause analysis
root_cause_analysis = {
    "primary_cause": "memory_exhaustion",
    "contributing_factors": [
        "high_traffic_spike",
        "inefficient_query_patterns",
        "large_response_payloads",
        "memory_leak_in_image_processing"
    ],
    "immediate_fixes": [
        "increase_memory_limit_to_1GB",
        "restart_affected_services",
        "enable_memory_monitoring_alerts"
    ],
    "long_term_solutions": [
        "implement_query_optimization",
        "add_response_pagination",
        "fix_memory_leak_in_portfolio_handler",
        "implement_horizontal_scaling"
    ]
}

# Print crash investigation report
print("Django Container Crash Investigation - anihpj Services:")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"Investigation: {investigation_context['investigation_id']}")
print(f"Severity: {investigation_context['severity'].title()}")

print(f"\n💥 Crash Event Timeline:")
for crash in crash_events:
    time_display = crash['timestamp'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    service = crash['service'].title()
    exit_code = crash['exit_code']
    reason = crash['reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    icon = "🔴" if exit_code == 137 else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {time_display} - {service} Service:")
    print(f"      Exit Code: {exit_code}")
    print(f"      Reason: {reason}")
    
    if crash.get('memory_usage_at_crash'):
        print(f"      Memory: {crash['memory_usage_at_crash']}/{crash['memory_limit']}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Impact Analysis:")
for model, impact in model_crash_impact.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    print(f"\n   📈 {model_name} Model:")
    for key, value in impact.items():
        if isinstance(value, int):
            print(f"      {key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}")

print(f"\n🔍 Root Cause Analysis:")
print(f"   Primary: {root_cause_analysis['primary_cause'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"   Contributing Factors: {len(root_cause_analysis['contributing_factors'])}")

print(f"\n🔧 Immediate Fixes:")
for fix in root_cause_analysis['immediate_fixes']:
    print(f"   • {fix.replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n✅ Investigation Summary:")
print(f"   💥 3 container crashes analyzed")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models impacted")
print(f"   🔍 Root cause: Memory exhaustion")
print(f"   🔧 Fix: Increase memory limits to 1GB")
2026-01-12 14:23:15.123 container die anihpj_web_1 (exitCode=137, signal=SIGKILL) 2026-01-12 14:45:32.456 container die anihpj_worker_1 (exitCode=1) 2026-01-12 15:12:08.789 container die anihpj_web_2 (exitCode=137, signal=SIGKILL) Django Container Crash Investigation - anihpj Services: ========================================================== Investigation: crash_inv_2026_01_12_web_failure Severity: High 💥 Crash Event Timeline: 🔴 14:23:15 - Web Service: Exit Code: 137 Reason: Oom Killed Memory: 510MB/512MB 🟡 14:45:32 - Worker Service: Exit Code: 1 Reason: Application Error 🔴 15:12:08 - Web Service: Exit Code: 137 Reason: Oom Killed Memory: 508MB/512MB 📊 Model Impact Analysis: 📈 User Model: Sessions Terminated: 145 Active Logins Affected: 89 📈 Jobpost Model: Searches Interrupted: 234 Applications In Progress: 12 🔍 Root Cause Analysis: Primary: Memory Exhaustion Contributing Factors: 4 🔧 Immediate Fixes: • Increase Memory Limit To 1Gb • Restart Affected Services • Enable Memory Monitoring Alerts ✅ Investigation Summary: 💥 3 container crashes analyzed 📊 All 5 Django models impacted 🔍 Root cause: Memory exhaustion 🔧 Fix: Increase memory limits to 1GB
Example 4: Network Event Analysis (Service Connectivity Tracking)

Monitor Django anihpj network events including service connections, disconnections, and network topology changes affecting User authentication flows, JobPost API calls, Skills verification requests, Author media delivery, and Location geocoding services.

# Monitor network events
docker compose events --filter type=network

# Track network connections in JSON
docker compose events --json | grep -E "connect|disconnect"

# Monitor specific network events
docker compose events --filter event=connect

# Follow network topology changes
docker compose events --follow --filter type=network

Django Models Context & Network Analysis:

# Network event analysis for Django anihpj services
# Tracking User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service connectivity

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Network monitoring context
network_monitoring = {
    "session_id": "network_analysis_2026_01_12",
    "analysis_type": "connectivity_topology_tracking",
    "network_name": "anihpj_default"
}

# Network event stream
network_events = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T16:00:05Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "network": "anihpj_default",
        "container": "anihpj_web_1",
        "service": "web",
        "ip_address": "172.18.0.5",
        "models_connectivity": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T16:00:08Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "network": "anihpj_default",
        "container": "anihpj_db_1",
        "service": "db",
        "ip_address": "172.18.0.2",
        "models_connectivity": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T16:15:32Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "disconnect",
        "network": "anihpj_default",
        "container": "anihpj_redis_1",
        "service": "redis",
        "reason": "container_restart",
        "models_affected": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T16:15:45Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "network": "anihpj_default",
        "container": "anihpj_redis_2",
        "service": "redis",
        "ip_address": "172.18.0.7",
        "models_connectivity": ["User", "Skills", "JobPost"]
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T16:30:15Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "network": "anihpj_external",
        "container": "anihpj_nginx_1",
        "service": "nginx",
        "ip_address": "172.19.0.2",
        "models_connectivity": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    }
]

# Model network dependencies
model_network_deps = {
    "User_model": {
        "required_networks": ["anihpj_default"],
        "service_connections": ["web", "db", "redis"],
        "network_operations": [
            "authentication_requests",
            "session_storage_access",
            "profile_database_queries"
        ],
        "connectivity_critical": True,
        "failover_strategy": "redis_cluster_failover"
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "required_networks": ["anihpj_default", "anihpj_search"],
        "service_connections": ["web", "db", "elasticsearch", "celery"],
        "network_operations": [
            "search_index_queries",
            "database_crud_operations",
            "background_task_dispatch"
        ],
        "connectivity_critical": True,
        "failover_strategy": "elasticsearch_replica_routing"
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "required_networks": ["anihpj_default", "anihpj_external"],
        "service_connections": ["web", "db", "redis", "verification_api"],
        "network_operations": [
            "verification_api_calls",
            "cache_read_write",
            "demand_data_aggregation"
        ],
        "connectivity_critical": False,
        "failover_strategy": "cache_first_degraded_mode"
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "required_networks": ["anihpj_default", "anihpj_storage"],
        "service_connections": ["web", "db", "media_storage"],
        "network_operations": [
            "portfolio_media_delivery",
            "profile_database_access",
            "rating_aggregation_queries"
        ],
        "connectivity_critical": False,
        "failover_strategy": "cdn_fallback_delivery"
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "required_networks": ["anihpj_default", "anihpj_external"],
        "service_connections": ["web", "db", "geocoding_api"],
        "network_operations": [
            "geocoding_api_requests",
            "location_database_queries",
            "market_analysis_aggregation"
        ],
        "connectivity_critical": False,
        "failover_strategy": "cached_geocoding_fallback"
    }
}

# Network topology analysis
topology_analysis = {
    "active_networks": 3,
    "total_connections": 12,
    "connection_events": 4,
    "disconnection_events": 1,
    "topology_changes": 2,
    "network_health": "stable"
}

# Print network analysis report
print("Django Network Event Analysis - anihpj Services:")
print("=" * 52)
print(f"Session: {network_monitoring['session_id']}")
print(f"Network: {network_monitoring['network_name']}")

print(f"\n🌐 Network Event Stream:")
for event in network_events:
    time_display = event['timestamp'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    action = event['action'].title()
    service = event['service'].title()
    network = event['network']
    
    icon = "🟢" if action == "Connect" else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {time_display} - {service}:")
    print(f"      Action: {action}")
    print(f"      Network: {network}")
    
    if 'ip_address' in event:
        print(f"      IP: {event['ip_address']}")
    if 'reason' in event:
        print(f"      Reason: {event['reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Network Dependencies:")
for model, deps in model_network_deps.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    networks = ', '.join(deps['required_networks'])
    critical = deps['connectivity_critical']
    
    icon = "🔴" if critical else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Networks: {networks}")
    print(f"      Services: {len(deps['service_connections'])}")
    print(f"      Critical: {'Yes' if critical else 'No'}")

print(f"\n📈 Topology Summary:")
print(f"   Active Networks: {topology_analysis['active_networks']}")
print(f"   Total Connections: {topology_analysis['total_connections']}")
print(f"   Connect Events: {topology_analysis['connection_events']}")
print(f"   Disconnect Events: {topology_analysis['disconnection_events']}")
print(f"   Network Health: {topology_analysis['network_health'].title()}")

print(f"\n✅ Network Analysis Summary:")
print(f"   🌐 3 networks actively monitored")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models connectivity mapped")
print(f"   🔄 1 reconnection event handled")
print(f"   💚 Overall network health: Stable")
2026-01-12 16:00:05.123 network connect anihpj_default (container=anihpj_web_1, ip=172.18.0.5) 2026-01-12 16:00:08.456 network connect anihpj_default (container=anihpj_db_1, ip=172.18.0.2) 2026-01-12 16:15:32.789 network disconnect anihpj_default (container=anihpj_redis_1) 2026-01-12 16:15:45.012 network connect anihpj_default (container=anihpj_redis_2, ip=172.18.0.7) 2026-01-12 16:30:15.345 network connect anihpj_external (container=anihpj_nginx_1, ip=172.19.0.2) Django Network Event Analysis - anihpj Services: ==================================================== Session: network_analysis_2026_01_12 Network: anihpj_default 🌐 Network Event Stream: 🟢 16:00:05 - Web: Action: Connect Network: anihpj_default IP: 172.18.0.5 🟢 16:00:08 - Db: Action: Connect Network: anihpj_default IP: 172.18.0.2 🔴 16:15:32 - Redis: Action: Disconnect Network: anihpj_default Reason: Container Restart 🟢 16:15:45 - Redis: Action: Connect Network: anihpj_default IP: 172.18.0.7 📊 Model Network Dependencies: 🔴 User Model: Networks: anihpj_default Services: 3 Critical: Yes 🔴 Jobpost Model: Networks: anihpj_default, anihpj_search Services: 4 Critical: Yes 📈 Topology Summary: Active Networks: 3 Total Connections: 12 Connect Events: 4 Disconnect Events: 1 Network Health: Stable ✅ Network Analysis Summary: 🌐 3 networks actively monitored 📊 All 5 Django models connectivity mapped 🔄 1 reconnection event handled 💚 Overall network health: Stable
Example 5: Automated Incident Response (Event-Driven Remediation)

Implement automated incident response triggered by Django anihpj service events, automatically remediating issues affecting User availability, JobPost functionality, Skills verification, Author profiles, and Location services.

# Monitor events for automated response
docker compose events --json --follow | python incident_handler.py

# Track events with timestamp for alerting
docker compose events --format json --since 5m

# Monitor critical service events
docker compose events web db --filter event=die --json

# Event stream for automation pipeline
docker compose events --json

Django Models Context & Automated Response:

# Automated incident response for Django anihpj services
# Auto-remediating User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location service issues

import json
from typing import Dict, List, Any, Callable
from datetime import datetime
import threading

# Incident response configuration
incident_response_config = {
    "system_id": "auto_remediation_2026_01_12",
    "response_mode": "automated_with_escalation",
    "max_auto_remediation_attempts": 3
}

# Event triggers and responses
event_response_rules = {
    "container_die_oom": {
        "trigger_conditions": {"event": "die", "exit_code": 137},
        "auto_response": "restart_with_increased_memory",
        "escalation_threshold": 3,
        "cooldown_period": "5_minutes",
        "models_protected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    "health_check_failure": {
        "trigger_conditions": {"event": "health_status", "status": "unhealthy"},
        "auto_response": "service_health_recovery",
        "escalation_threshold": 5,
        "cooldown_period": "2_minutes",
        "models_protected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
    },
    "network_disconnect": {
        "trigger_conditions": {"type": "network", "action": "disconnect"},
        "auto_response": "network_reconnection",
        "escalation_threshold": 2,
        "cooldown_period": "1_minute",
        "models_protected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"]
    }
}

# Incident event log
incident_log = [
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T17:15:23Z",
        "incident_id": "INC_001",
        "trigger_event": "container_die_oom",
        "service": "web",
        "auto_response_executed": True,
        "response_action": "restart_with_512mb_to_1gb",
        "response_result": "success",
        "recovery_time_seconds": 45,
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "escalated": False
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T17:45:12Z",
        "incident_id": "INC_002",
        "trigger_event": "health_check_failure",
        "service": "elasticsearch",
        "auto_response_executed": True,
        "response_action": "cluster_rebalance_triggered",
        "response_result": "success",
        "recovery_time_seconds": 120,
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "Skills"],
        "escalated": False
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2026-01-12T18:30:45Z",
        "incident_id": "INC_003",
        "trigger_event": "container_die_oom",
        "service": "worker",
        "auto_response_executed": True,
        "response_action": "restart_with_increased_memory",
        "response_result": "partial",
        "recovery_time_seconds": 180,
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "User", "Skills"],
        "escalated": True,
        "escalation_reason": "third_occurrence_within_hour"
    }
]

# Model-specific remediation strategies
model_remediation_strategies = {
    "User_model": {
        "priority": "critical",
        "auto_remediation_enabled": True,
        "strategies": [
            {
                "scenario": "authentication_service_down",
                "actions": ["restart_web_service", "clear_session_cache", "notify_users"],
                "max_downtime_target": "60_seconds"
            },
            {
                "scenario": "session_storage_failure",
                "actions": ["failover_to_backup_redis", "session_reconstruction", "alert_ops"],
                "max_downtime_target": "30_seconds"
            }
        ],
        "escalation_contacts": ["sre_lead", "security_team"]
    },
    "JobPost_model": {
        "priority": "critical",
        "auto_remediation_enabled": True,
        "strategies": [
            {
                "scenario": "search_service_degraded",
                "actions": ["elasticsearch_cluster_heal", "search_cache_rebuild", "degraded_mode"],
                "max_downtime_target": "120_seconds"
            },
            {
                "scenario": "background_worker_failure",
                "actions": ["worker_restart", "queue_replay", "task_redistribution"],
                "max_downtime_target": "180_seconds"
            }
        ],
        "escalation_contacts": ["sre_lead", "product_team"]
    },
    "Skills_model": {
        "priority": "high",
        "auto_remediation_enabled": True,
        "strategies": [
            {
                "scenario": "verification_api_timeout",
                "actions": ["api_circuit_breaker", "cached_response_fallback", "retry_queue"],
                "max_downtime_target": "300_seconds"
            }
        ],
        "escalation_contacts": ["sre_team"]
    },
    "Author_model": {
        "priority": "medium",
        "auto_remediation_enabled": True,
        "strategies": [
            {
                "scenario": "media_storage_unavailable",
                "actions": ["cdn_fallback", "placeholder_content", "storage_recovery"],
                "max_downtime_target": "600_seconds"
            }
        ],
        "escalation_contacts": ["sre_team"]
    },
    "Location_model": {
        "priority": "medium",
        "auto_remediation_enabled": True,
        "strategies": [
            {
                "scenario": "geocoding_service_failure",
                "actions": ["fallback_geocoder", "cached_locations", "graceful_degradation"],
                "max_downtime_target": "600_seconds"
            }
        ],
        "escalation_contacts": ["sre_team"]
    }
}

# Response metrics
response_metrics = {
    "total_incidents": 3,
    "auto_remediated": 3,
    "successful_recoveries": 2,
    "partial_recoveries": 1,
    "escalations": 1,
    "average_recovery_time": "115_seconds",
    "mttr_target": "120_seconds",
    "mttr_achieved": True
}

# Print incident response report
print("Django Automated Incident Response - anihpj Services:")
print("=" * 56)
print(f"System: {incident_response_config['system_id']}")
print(f"Mode: {incident_response_config['response_mode'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📋 Event Response Rules:")
for rule_name, rule in event_response_rules.items():
    rule_display = rule_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    response = rule['auto_response'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    threshold = rule['escalation_threshold']
    
    print(f"\n   🔧 {rule_display}:")
    print(f"      Auto Response: {response}")
    print(f"      Escalation After: {threshold} occurrences")

print(f"\n📊 Incident Log:")
for incident in incident_log:
    time_display = incident['timestamp'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    inc_id = incident['incident_id']
    service = incident['service'].title()
    result = incident['response_result'].title()
    recovery = incident['recovery_time_seconds']
    
    icon = "🟢" if result == "Success" else "🟡" if result == "Partial" else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {icon} {inc_id} ({time_display}):")
    print(f"      Service: {service}")
    print(f"      Result: {result}")
    print(f"      Recovery: {recovery}s")
    
    if incident.get('escalated'):
        print(f"      ⚠️ Escalated: {incident['escalation_reason'].replace('_', ' ')}")

print(f"\n🏥 Model Remediation Strategies:")
for model, strategy in model_remediation_strategies.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = strategy['priority'].title()
    scenarios = len(strategy['strategies'])
    
    priority_icon = "🔴" if priority == "Critical" else "🟡" if priority == "High" else "🟢"
    
    print(f"\n   {priority_icon} {model_name} Model ({priority}):")
    print(f"      Scenarios Covered: {scenarios}")
    print(f"      Auto-Remediation: {'Enabled' if strategy['auto_remediation_enabled'] else 'Disabled'}")

print(f"\n📈 Response Metrics:")
print(f"   Total Incidents: {response_metrics['total_incidents']}")
print(f"   Auto-Remediated: {response_metrics['auto_remediated']}")
print(f"   Successful: {response_metrics['successful_recoveries']}")
print(f"   Escalations: {response_metrics['escalations']}")
print(f"   Avg Recovery: {response_metrics['average_recovery_time'].replace('_', ' ')}")
print(f"   MTTR Target Met: {'Yes' if response_metrics['mttr_achieved'] else 'No'}")

print(f"\n✅ Automated Response Summary:")
print(f"   🤖 3 incidents auto-remediated")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models protected")
print(f"   ⏱️ Average recovery: 115 seconds")
print(f"   🎯 MTTR target (120s) achieved")
print(f"   📞 1 escalation to SRE team")
{"time":"2026-01-12T17:15:23Z","type":"container","action":"die","id":"anihpj_web_1","exitCode":137} {"time":"2026-01-12T17:15:68Z","type":"container","action":"start","id":"anihpj_web_2"} {"time":"2026-01-12T17:45:12Z","type":"container","action":"health_status","id":"anihpj_elasticsearch_1","health":"unhealthy"} {"time":"2026-01-12T17:47:12Z","type":"container","action":"health_status","id":"anihpj_elasticsearch_1","health":"healthy"} Django Automated Incident Response - anihpj Services: ======================================================== System: auto_remediation_2026_01_12 Mode: Automated With Escalation 📋 Event Response Rules: 🔧 Container Die Oom: Auto Response: Restart With Increased Memory Escalation After: 3 occurrences 🔧 Health Check Failure: Auto Response: Service Health Recovery Escalation After: 5 occurrences 📊 Incident Log: 🟢 INC_001 (17:15:23): Service: Web Result: Success Recovery: 45s 🟢 INC_002 (17:45:12): Service: Elasticsearch Result: Success Recovery: 120s 🟡 INC_003 (18:30:45): Service: Worker Result: Partial Recovery: 180s ⚠️ Escalated: third occurrence within hour 🏥 Model Remediation Strategies: 🔴 User Model (Critical): Scenarios Covered: 2 Auto-Remediation: Enabled 🔴 Jobpost Model (Critical): Scenarios Covered: 2 Auto-Remediation: Enabled 📈 Response Metrics: Total Incidents: 3 Auto-Remediated: 3 Successful: 2 Escalations: 1 Avg Recovery: 115 seconds MTTR Target Met: Yes ✅ Automated Response Summary: 🤖 3 incidents auto-remediated 📊 All 5 Django models protected ⏱️ Average recovery: 115 seconds 🎯 MTTR target (120s) achieved 📞 1 escalation to SRE team

docker compose ls

Official Docs: docker compose ls | Docker CLI: docker compose ls

List all Docker Compose projects showing Django application stacks and their running status.

Full Description

The docker compose ls command lists all Docker Compose projects currently available on your system, showing project names, status, configuration files, and services information. This provides an overview of all Django application stacks and their operational state across your development environment.

For Django applications, this command is essential for managing multiple project environments, monitoring deployment status across different anihpj configurations, and maintaining visibility into all Docker Compose stacks running your Django services and infrastructure components.

Syntax

# List all compose projects
docker compose ls

# Show projects with service details
docker compose ls --all

# List only running projects
docker compose ls --filter status=running

# Show projects in specific directory
docker compose ls --filter name=anihpj

# Format output as table
docker compose ls --format table

# Show project configuration files
docker compose ls --quiet

# List with JSON output
docker compose ls --format json

Parameters Description

  • --all: Show all Django Compose projects including stopped ones. Complete project inventory across environments.
  • --filter: Filter Django projects by status, name, or other attributes. Selective project listing and monitoring.
  • --format: Output Django projects in table or JSON format. Structured presentation and automation support.
  • --quiet: Show only Django project names without additional details. Simplified output for scripting.
  • Status Filtering: Filter Django projects by running, exited, or paused status for targeted management.

Attributes

  • Project Discovery: Lists all Django Compose projects with names, status, and service counts for environment overview.
  • Status Monitoring: Shows Django project operational status including running, stopped, and service health information.
  • Configuration Tracking: Displays Django Compose file locations and project working directories for project management.
  • Service Inventory: Shows Django service counts and running status for each project stack.
  • Environment Management: Enables Django multi-environment project management and deployment coordination.

Methods

Environment Overview

Syntax: docker compose ls --all

Parameters: complete Django project listing including all status types

Get comprehensive overview of Django development, staging, and production environments across all Compose projects.

Project Status Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose ls --filter status=running --format table

Parameters: filtered Django project status display with structured formatting

Monitor active Django projects and their service status for operational visibility and management.

Return Value

Returns table of Django Compose projects showing name, status, config files, and service information.

  • Success (0): Project list displayed successfully
  • No Projects (0): No compose projects found
  • Access Error (1): Permission denied or Docker daemon unavailable

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Multi-Environment Project Management (Development & Production Oversight)

Manage multiple Django anihpj project environments including development, testing, staging, and production stacks with User authentication, JobPost processing, Skills verification, Author management, and Location services across different deployment configurations.

# List all Django projects
docker compose ls --all

# Show only running projects
docker compose ls --filter status=running

# Get detailed project information
docker compose ls --format table

# List projects in JSON format for automation
docker compose ls --format json

Django Models Context & Multi-Environment Management:

# Multi-environment Django project management for anihpj application
# Managing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location models across environments

import json
import os
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Multi-environment project overview
environment_overview = {
    "management_timestamp": "2026-01-12 11:30:00",
    "total_environments": 5,
    "management_scope": "anihpj_django_project_lifecycle"
}

# Discovered Django Compose projects
discovered_projects = {
    "anihpj-development": {
        "name": "anihpj-development",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.dev.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-dev",
        "services_running": 4,
        "services_total": 5,
        "created": "2026-01-10T09:00:00Z",
        "environment_type": "development",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8000:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "5432:5432"}, 
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "6379:6379"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "exited", "port": "80:80"}
        },
        "development_features": [
            "debug_mode_enabled",
            "live_reload_active",
            "database_exposed_for_testing",
            "comprehensive_logging"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-testing": {
        "name": "anihpj-testing",
        "status": "running", 
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.test.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-test",
        "services_running": 6,
        "services_total": 6,
        "created": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "environment_type": "testing",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8001:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "5433:5432"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "6380:6379"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "selenium": {"status": "running", "port": "4444:4444"},
            "test_runner": {"status": "running", "port": "none"}
        },
        "testing_features": [
            "automated_test_execution",
            "selenium_browser_testing",
            "test_database_isolation",
            "performance_benchmarking"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-staging": {
        "name": "anihpj-staging",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.staging.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-staging",
        "services_running": 7,
        "services_total": 7,
        "created": "2026-01-08T16:45:00Z",
        "environment_type": "staging",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8002:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "running", "port": "443:443"},
            "elasticsearch": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "monitoring": {"status": "running", "port": "3000:3000"}
        },
        "staging_features": [
            "production_like_configuration",
            "ssl_certificate_testing",
            "performance_monitoring",
            "user_acceptance_testing"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-production": {
        "name": "anihpj-production",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.prod.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-prod", 
        "services_running": 8,
        "services_total": 8,
        "created": "2026-01-05T10:20:00Z",
        "environment_type": "production",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "running", "port": "443:443"},
            "elasticsearch": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "monitoring": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "backup": {"status": "running", "port": "none"}
        },
        "production_features": [
            "high_availability_setup",
            "automated_backups",
            "security_hardening",
            "performance_optimization"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-migration": {
        "name": "anihpj-migration", 
        "status": "exited",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.migration.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-migration",
        "services_running": 0,
        "services_total": 3,
        "created": "2026-01-09T20:15:00Z",
        "environment_type": "migration",
        "models_active": [],
        "service_details": {
            "migration_runner": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"},
            "source_db": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"},
            "target_db": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"}
        },
        "migration_purpose": [
            "database_schema_updates",
            "data_migration_tasks", 
            "model_relationship_updates",
            "production_data_sync"
        ]
    }
}

# Environment status analysis
environment_analysis = {
    "status_summary": {
        "running_projects": 4,
        "stopped_projects": 1,
        "total_services": 29,
        "running_services": 25,
        "service_uptime": "99.2%"
    },
    "model_deployment_status": {
        "User_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "good"
        },
        "Author_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "good"
        },
        "Location_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        }
    },
    "resource_utilization": {
        "total_cpu_usage": "45%",
        "total_memory_usage": "62%",
        "total_disk_usage": "34%",
        "network_throughput": "moderate"
    }
}

# Project management recommendations
management_recommendations = {
    "immediate_actions": [
        {
            "project": "anihpj-development",
            "issue": "nginx_service_down",
            "action": "restart_nginx_container",
            "priority": "medium",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "impact": "development_proxy_unavailable"
        },
        {
            "project": "anihpj-migration",
            "issue": "migration_project_stopped",
            "action": "cleanup_or_archive",
            "priority": "low", 
            "models_affected": [],
            "impact": "resource_cleanup_opportunity"
        }
    ],
    "optimization_opportunities": [
        {
            "scope": "all_environments",
            "opportunity": "resource_consolidation",
            "description": "optimize_service_resource_allocation",
            "estimated_savings": "15%_cpu_10%_memory",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "scope": "testing_staging",
            "opportunity": "shared_database_instances",
            "description": "consolidate_non_production_databases", 
            "estimated_savings": "25%_storage_reduction",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        }
    ]
}

# Environment-specific model performance
model_performance_analysis = {
    "development_performance": {
        "User_model": {
            "response_time": "45ms",
            "throughput": "120_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.1%",
            "debug_features": "full_logging_enabled"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "response_time": "125ms",
            "throughput": "85_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.2%",
            "debug_features": "query_analysis_active"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "response_time": "67ms",
            "throughput": "95_req_min", 
            "error_rate": "0.1%",
            "debug_features": "verification_testing_mode"
        }
    },
    "production_performance": {
        "User_model": {
            "response_time": "23ms",
            "throughput": "2400_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.01%",
            "optimization": "redis_caching_optimized"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "response_time": "89ms",
            "throughput": "1800_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.02%",
            "optimization": "elasticsearch_search_optimized"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "response_time": "34ms",
            "throughput": "1200_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.01%",
            "optimization": "api_response_caching"
        }
    }
}

# Security and compliance status
security_compliance_status = {
    "environment_security": {
        "development": {
            "security_level": "relaxed",
            "ssl_enabled": False,
            "debug_mode": True,
            "external_access": "limited",
            "compliance_score": 60
        },
        "testing": {
            "security_level": "moderate",
            "ssl_enabled": False,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "internal_only",
            "compliance_score": 75
        },
        "staging": {
            "security_level": "high",
            "ssl_enabled": True,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "restricted",
            "compliance_score": 90
        },
        "production": {
            "security_level": "maximum",
            "ssl_enabled": True,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "controlled",
            "compliance_score": 98
        }
    },
    "model_data_protection": {
        "User_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_staging_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments",
            "access_control": "role_based",
            "audit_logging": "enabled"
        },
        "JobPost_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments", 
            "access_control": "permission_based",
            "audit_logging": "enabled"
        },
        "Skills_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments",
            "access_control": "api_key_based",
            "audit_logging": "partial"
        }
    }
}

# Print multi-environment project analysis
print("Django Multi-Environment Project Management - anihpj Ecosystem:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Management Time: {environment_overview['management_timestamp']}")
print(f"Total Environments: {environment_overview['total_environments']}")
print(f"Project Scope: {environment_overview['management_scope'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🏗️ Project Environment Overview:")
status_summary = environment_analysis['status_summary']
print(f"   Running Projects: {status_summary['running_projects']}")
print(f"   Stopped Projects: {status_summary['stopped_projects']}")
print(f"   Total Services: {status_summary['total_services']}")
print(f"   Service Uptime: {status_summary['service_uptime']}")

print(f"\n📋 Environment Details:")
for project_name, details in discovered_projects.items():
    env_type = details['environment_type'].title()
    status = details['status'].title()
    running = details['services_running']
    total = details['services_total']
    
    status_icon = "🟢" if status == "Running" else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {project_name.replace('-', ' ').title()}:")
    print(f"      Type: {env_type}")
    print(f"      Status: {status}")
    print(f"      Services: {running}/{total} running")
    print(f"      Directory: {details['working_directory']}")
    
    models = details.get('models_active', [])
    if models:
        print(f"      Django Models: {', '.join(models)}")
    
    # Show key services
    if 'service_details' in details:
        running_services = [name for name, info in details['service_details'].items() 
                          if info['status'] == 'running']
        if running_services:
            print(f"      Key Services: {', '.join(running_services[:3])}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Deployment Matrix:")
model_status = environment_analysis['model_deployment_status']
for model, status in model_status.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    deployments = status['total_deployments']
    active = status['active_instances']
    health = status['service_health'].title()
    
    health_icon = "🟢" if health == "Excellent" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {health_icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Deployments: {deployments}")
    print(f"      Active Instances: {active}")
    print(f"      Health: {health}")
    print(f"      Environments: {', '.join(status['deployed_environments'])}")

print(f"\n🔧 Management Recommendations:")
immediate = management_recommendations['immediate_actions']
print(f"   Immediate Actions: {len(immediate)}")

for action in immediate:
    project = action['project'].replace('-', ' ').title()
    issue = action['issue'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    solution = action['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = action['priority'].title()
    
    print(f"\n   ⚠️ {project}:")
    print(f"      Issue: {issue}")
    print(f"      Action: {solution}")
    print(f"      Priority: {priority}")

optimization = management_recommendations['optimization_opportunities']
print(f"\n   Optimization Opportunities: {len(optimization)}")

for opt in optimization:
    scope = opt['scope'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    opportunity = opt['opportunity'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    savings = opt['estimated_savings'].replace('_', ' ')
    
    print(f"\n   💡 {scope}:")
    print(f"      Opportunity: {opportunity}")
    print(f"      Estimated Savings: {savings}")

print(f"\n⚡ Performance Comparison:")
dev_perf = model_performance_analysis['development_performance']
prod_perf = model_performance_analysis['production_performance']

print(f"   Development vs Production:")
for model in ['User_model', 'JobPost_model', 'Skills_model']:
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    dev_time = dev_perf[model]['response_time']
    prod_time = prod_perf[model]['response_time']
    
    print(f"     📊 {model_name}: {dev_time} (dev) → {prod_time} (prod)")

print(f"\n🔒 Security & Compliance Status:")
for env, security in security_compliance_status['environment_security'].items():
    env_name = env.title()
    score = security['compliance_score']
    ssl = security['ssl_enabled']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= 90 else "🟡" if score >= 70 else "🔴"
    ssl_icon = "🔐" if ssl else "🔓"
    
    print(f"   {score_icon} {env_name}: {score}% compliance {ssl_icon}")

print(f"\n🎯 Multi-Environment Management Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ 4/5 projects running successfully")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models deployed across environments")
print(f"   🚀 Production environment: 98% compliance score")
print(f"   ⚡ Performance optimized: prod 3-4x faster than dev")
print(f"   🔧 2 optimization opportunities identified")
print(f"   🛡️ Security levels appropriate per environment")

print(f"\n📋 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Restart nginx service in development environment")
print(f"   2. Archive completed migration project")
print(f"   3. Implement resource consolidation optimizations")
print(f"   4. Review and standardize security configurations")
print(f"   5. Set up automated environment health monitoring")

docker-compose.yml,docker-compose.staging.yml running 7/7 anihpj-production docker-compose.yml,docker-compose.prod.yml running 8/8 anihpj-migration docker-compose.yml,docker-compose.migration.yml docker compose cp

Official Docs: docker compose cp | Docker CLI: docker compose cp

Copy files between Django service containers and local filesystem for data transfer and debugging.

Full Description

The docker compose cp command copies files and directories between your anihpj Django service containers and the local filesystem. This enables data extraction, configuration updates, log retrieval, and debugging workflows by transferring files bidirectionally between containers and host system.

For Django applications, this command is essential for retrieving logs, updating configuration files, extracting database dumps, transferring media files, and debugging issues in your anihpj jobpost application by accessing container filesystem content directly.

Syntax

# Copy file from container to host
docker compose cp web:/app/logs/django.log ./logs/

# Copy file from host to container
docker compose cp ./config.py web:/app/config/

# Copy directory from container
docker compose cp web:/app/media ./backup/media/

# Copy to specific container instance
docker compose cp web-1:/app/db.sqlite3 ./db_backup.sqlite3

# Copy with archive mode
docker compose cp --archive web:/app/static ./static_backup/

# Follow symbolic links
docker compose cp --follow-link web:/app/logs ./logs/

Parameters Description

  • Source Path: Django container path or host path for file source location. Specify origin of files to copy.
  • Destination Path: Django target path for copied files. Specify where files should be placed.
  • --archive: Preserve Django file permissions, ownership, and timestamps during copy operation.
  • --follow-link: Follow Django symbolic links in container filesystem. Resolve symlinks to actual files.
  • Container Instance: Specify Django service container instance for multi-replica deployments.

Attributes

  • Bidirectional Transfer: Copy Django files from container to host or host to container for flexible data management.
  • Directory Support: Transfer entire Django directories and subdirectories with recursive copy operations.
  • Permission Preservation: Maintain Django file permissions and ownership when using archive mode.
  • Real-Time Access: Access Django container filesystem while services are running without interruption.
  • Multi-Service Support: Copy files between different Django services and containers within the same project.

Methods

Log Extraction

Syntax: docker compose cp web:/app/logs/ ./debug_logs/

Parameters: container log directory to host location for Django troubleshooting and analysis

Extract Django application logs, error traces, and debug information for offline analysis and debugging.

Configuration Management

Syntax: docker compose cp ./updated_settings.py web:/app/anihpj/

Parameters: host configuration file to Django container for live updates

Update Django configuration files, environment settings, and application parameters in running containers.

Return Value

Returns success status of Django file copy operation showing transfer completion and any error conditions.

  • Success (0): File copy completed successfully
  • Not Found (1): Source file or directory not found
  • Permission Error (2): Insufficient permissions for copy operation

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Debugging & Log Analysis (Critical Issue Investigation)

Extract Django production logs and database files for critical issue investigation involving User authentication failures, JobPost processing errors, Skills verification timeouts, and Location service anomalies requiring offline analysis and forensic debugging.

# Extract Django application logs
docker compose cp web:/app/logs/ ./investigation/logs/

# Copy database for analysis
docker compose cp db:/var/lib/postgresql/data/ ./investigation/db_backup/

# Extract media files for inspection
docker compose cp web:/app/media/ ./investigation/media/

# Copy configuration files
docker compose cp web:/app/anihpj/settings/ ./investigation/config/

Django Models Context & Debugging Investigation:

# Production debugging and log analysis for Django anihpj application
# Investigating User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model issues

import os
import json
import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Any
import shutil

# Critical issue investigation context
investigation_context = {
    "incident_id": "ANIHPJ_CRITICAL_2026_01_12_001",
    "incident_start": "2026-01-12 14:30:00",
    "incident_detection": "2026-01-12 14:35:00",
    "investigation_start": "2026-01-12 14:40:00",
    "severity": "critical",
    "affected_models": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
}

# File extraction and analysis plan
extraction_plan = {
    "logs_extraction": {
        "source_paths": [
            "/app/logs/django.log",
            "/app/logs/error.log", 
            "/app/logs/access.log",
            "/app/logs/celery.log",
            "/app/logs/nginx.log"
        ],
        "destination": "./investigation/logs/",
        "priority": "immediate",
        "models_relevance": {
            "django.log": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "error.log": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
            "celery.log": ["JobPost", "Skills", "Location"],
            "nginx.log": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        }
    },
    "database_analysis": {
        "source_paths": [
            "/var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.conf",
            "/var/lib/postgresql/data/pg_log/",
            "/var/lib/postgresql/data/base/"
        ],
        "destination": "./investigation/db_analysis/",
        "priority": "high",
        "analysis_focus": [
            "user_authentication_tables",
            "jobpost_search_indexes",
            "skills_verification_logs",
            "location_query_performance"
        ]
    },
    "configuration_audit": {
        "source_paths": [
            "/app/anihpj/settings/production.py",
            "/app/anihpj/urls.py", 
            "/app/anihpj/wsgi.py",
            "/app/requirements.txt",
            "/etc/nginx/nginx.conf"
        ],
        "destination": "./investigation/config/", 
        "priority": "medium",
        "security_analysis": True
    },
    "media_forensics": {
        "source_paths": [
            "/app/media/uploads/",
            "/app/media/avatars/",
            "/app/media/portfolios/",
            "/app/static/admin/"
        ],
        "destination": "./investigation/media/",
        "priority": "low",
        "models_involved": ["User", "Author", "Skills"]
    }
}

# Extracted file analysis results
extracted_files_analysis = {
    "django_log_analysis": {
        "file_size": "2.3GB",
        "lines_count": 4567890,
        "error_entries": 1234,
        "warning_entries": 5678,
        "time_range": "2026-01-10 00:00:00 to 2026-01-12 14:40:00",
        "model_error_breakdown": {
            "User_model_errors": {
                "authentication_failures": 456,
                "session_timeouts": 234,
                "permission_denials": 123,
                "critical_patterns": [
                    "IntegrityError in user creation",
                    "Session key validation failed",
                    "LDAP authentication timeout"
                ]
            },
            "JobPost_model_errors": {
                "search_index_corruption": 89,
                "application_submission_failures": 156,
                "recommendation_timeouts": 78,
                "critical_patterns": [
                    "Elasticsearch connection lost",
                    "Database deadlock in job_post table",
                    "Memory error in recommendation engine"
                ]
            },
            "Skills_model_errors": {
                "verification_api_failures": 234,
                "demand_calculation_errors": 67,
                "cache_miss_cascades": 145,
                "critical_patterns": [
                    "External API rate limit exceeded",
                    "Redis connection timeout",
                    "Skill matching algorithm crash"
                ]
            },
            "Author_model_errors": {
                "portfolio_upload_failures": 45,
                "rating_calculation_errors": 23,
                "availability_sync_issues": 67,
                "critical_patterns": [
                    "S3 bucket access denied",
                    "Rating aggregation SQL timeout",
                    "Calendar API integration failure"
                ]
            },
            "Location_model_errors": {
                "geocoding_api_timeouts": 123,
                "market_analysis_crashes": 34,
                "coordinate_validation_errors": 56,
                "critical_patterns": [
                    "Google Maps API quota exceeded",
                    "PostGIS extension not responding",
                    "Timezone calculation overflow"
                ]
            }
        }
    },
    "database_log_analysis": {
        "postgresql_log_size": "856MB",
        "slow_queries": 345,
        "connection_errors": 67,
        "deadlocks": 12,
        "model_performance_issues": {
            "User_table": {
                "slow_queries": 123,
                "deadlocks": 3,
                "index_usage": "suboptimal",
                "connection_pool": "95%_utilization"
            },
            "JobPost_table": {
                "slow_queries": 156,
                "deadlocks": 5,
                "index_usage": "critical_missing_indexes",
                "full_table_scans": 45
            },
            "Skills_table": {
                "slow_queries": 78,
                "deadlocks": 2,
                "index_usage": "good",
                "cache_hit_rate": "67%"
            }
        }
    },
    "configuration_audit_results": {
        "security_issues": [
            {
                "file": "production.py",
                "issue": "debug_mode_accidentally_enabled",
                "severity": "critical",
                "models_exposed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
            },
            {
                "file": "nginx.conf",
                "issue": "insufficient_rate_limiting",
                "severity": "high", 
                "vulnerability": "api_abuse_possible"
            }
        ],
        "performance_issues": [
            {
                "file": "wsgi.py",
                "issue": "worker_count_misconfiguration",
                "impact": "request_queuing_delays",
                "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost"]
            }
        ]
    }
}

# Investigation findings and root cause analysis
root_cause_analysis = {
    "primary_causes": [
        {
            "cause": "debug_mode_in_production",
            "severity": "critical",
            "discovery_method": "configuration_file_extraction",
            "models_impacted": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "impact_description": "performance_degradation_and_security_exposure",
            "evidence_files": ["production.py", "django.log"],
            "fix_priority": "immediate"
        },
        {
            "cause": "elasticsearch_cluster_failure",
            "severity": "high",
            "discovery_method": "log_analysis_pattern_matching",
            "models_impacted": ["JobPost", "Skills"],
            "impact_description": "search_functionality_complete_failure",
            "evidence_files": ["django.log", "celery.log"],
            "fix_priority": "urgent"
        },
        {
            "cause": "database_connection_pool_exhaustion",
            "severity": "high",
            "discovery_method": "postgresql_log_analysis",
            "models_impacted": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "impact_description": "intermittent_service_unavailability",
            "evidence_files": ["postgresql.log", "django.log"],
            "fix_priority": "urgent"
        }
    ],
    "secondary_causes": [
        {
            "cause": "insufficient_nginx_rate_limiting",
            "severity": "medium",
            "models_impacted": ["User", "JobPost"],
            "impact_description": "api_abuse_amplifying_primary_issues"
        }
    ]
}

# Remediation action plan
remediation_plan = {
    "immediate_actions": [
        {
            "action": "disable_debug_mode_in_production",
            "steps": [
                "update_production_settings_file",
                "restart_web_services",
                "verify_debug_disabled"
            ],
            "estimated_time": "5_minutes",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "action": "restart_elasticsearch_cluster",
            "steps": [
                "diagnose_elasticsearch_failure",
                "restart_elasticsearch_services",
                "rebuild_search_indexes"
            ],
            "estimated_time": "20_minutes",
            "models_benefited": ["JobPost", "Skills"]
        },
        {
            "action": "increase_database_connection_pool",
            "steps": [
                "update_database_configuration",
                "restart_database_service",
                "monitor_connection_metrics"
            ],
            "estimated_time": "10_minutes",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        }
    ],
    "follow_up_actions": [
        {
            "action": "implement_proper_rate_limiting",
            "estimated_time": "30_minutes",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost"]
        },
        {
            "action": "optimize_database_indexes",
            "estimated_time": "60_minutes",
            "models_benefited": ["JobPost", "User"]
        }
    ]
}

# Print investigation analysis
print("Django Production Debugging Investigation - anihpj Critical Incident:")
print("=" * 68)
print(f"Incident ID: {investigation_context['incident_id']}")
print(f"Severity: {investigation_context['severity'].upper()}")
print(f"Investigation Start: {investigation_context['investigation_start']}")

print(f"\n📁 File Extraction Summary:")
for category, details in extraction_plan.items():
    category_name = category.replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = details['priority'].title()
    source_count = len(details['source_paths'])
    
    print(f"\n   📂 {category_name}:")
    print(f"      Priority: {priority}")
    print(f"      Files/Paths: {source_count}")
    
    if 'models_relevance' in details:
        print(f"      Model Relevance: High")
    elif 'analysis_focus' in details:
        print(f"      Analysis Focus: Database Performance")

print(f"\n🔍 Extracted File Analysis:")
django_analysis = extracted_files_analysis['django_log_analysis']
print(f"   Django Log: {django_analysis['file_size']}, {django_analysis['lines_count']:,} lines")
print(f"   Errors: {django_analysis['error_entries']:,}, Warnings: {django_analysis['warning_entries']:,}")

print(f"\n📊 Model-Specific Error Analysis:")
model_errors = django_analysis['model_error_breakdown']
for model, errors in model_errors.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_errors', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    total_errors = sum(errors[key] for key in errors.keys() if isinstance(errors[key], int))
    
    print(f"\n   🔴 {model_name} Model ({total_errors} errors):")
    for error_type, count in errors.items():
        if isinstance(count, int):
            error_name = error_type.replace('_', ' ').title()
            print(f"      - {error_name}: {count}")
    
    if 'critical_patterns' in errors:
        critical_pattern = errors['critical_patterns'][0]
        print(f"      Critical: {critical_pattern}")

print(f"\n💾 Database Performance Issues:")
db_analysis = extracted_files_analysis['database_log_analysis']
print(f"   Log Size: {db_analysis['postgresql_log_size']}")
print(f"   Slow Queries: {db_analysis['slow_queries']}")
print(f"   Deadlocks: {db_analysis['deadlocks']}")

db_perf = db_analysis['model_performance_issues']
for table, issues in db_perf.items():
    table_name = table.replace('_table', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    slow_queries = issues.get('slow_queries', 0)
    deadlocks = issues.get('deadlocks', 0)
    
    print(f"   📊 {table_name}: {slow_queries} slow queries, {deadlocks} deadlocks")

print(f"\n🎯 Root Cause Analysis:")
primary = root_cause_analysis['primary_causes']
print(f"   Primary Causes: {len(primary)}")

for i, cause in enumerate(primary, 1):
    cause_name = cause['cause'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    severity = cause['severity'].title()
    models = ', '.join(cause['models_impacted'])
    
    print(f"\n   {i}. {cause_name} ({severity}):")
    print(f"      Models Impacted: {models}")
    print(f"      Impact: {cause['impact_description'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Priority: {cause['fix_priority'].title()}")

print(f"\n🔧 Remediation Plan:")
immediate = remediation_plan['immediate_actions']
print(f"   Immediate Actions: {len(immediate)}")

for action in immediate:
    action_name = action['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_est = action['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    models = ', '.join(action['models_benefited'])
    
    print(f"\n   ⚡ {action_name}:")
    print(f"      Time: {time_est}")
    print(f"      Models Benefited: {models}")
    print(f"      Steps: {len(action['steps'])}")

print(f"\n✅ Investigation Summary:")
print(f"   🔍 Complete file extraction and analysis performed")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models error patterns identified")
print(f"   🎯 3 critical root causes discovered")
print(f"   🔧 Immediate remediation plan established")
print(f"   ⚡ Estimated resolution time: 35 minutes")
print(f"   💾 Evidence preserved for post-incident review")

print(f"\n📋 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Execute immediate remediation actions")
print(f"   2. Monitor system recovery metrics")
print(f"   3. Implement preventive measures")
print(f"   4. Conduct post-incident review")
print(f"   5. Update monitoring and alerting systems")
